histogram.summary: The number of credit card deleted during a major version upgrade because they have not been used recently enough and are expired.

histogram.name: Autofill.CreditCardsSuppressedForDisuse
histogram.summary: The number of credit card suggestions not shown in the Autofill popup because they have not been used recently enough and are expired. Note that suppression only applies when the user has not typed any text into the field. If any text is in the field, all matching cards are displayed, regardless of how recently they have been used and their expiration status.

histogram.name: Autofill.CreditCardUploadDisallowedForNetwork
histogram.summary: When a credit card is not allowed to be offered upload save due to its network, logs what the card network was.

histogram.name: Autofill.DaysSinceLastUse.CreditCard
histogram.summary: Logs the number of days that have passed since the credit card was last used.

histogram.name: Autofill.DaysSinceLastUse.Profile
histogram.summary: Logged at the time of autofill address use, this histogram captures the number of days that have passed since the address was last used. I.e., the distribution of time between uses of an "active" autofill address.

histogram.name: Autofill.DaysSinceLastUse.StoredCreditCard
histogram.summary: Logs the number of days that have passed since each credit card belonging to a given Chrome Profile was last used. This is logged for each Autofill credit card once per Chrome User Profile load.

histogram.name: Autofill.DaysSinceLastUse.StoredProfile
histogram.summary: This histogram captures the number of days that have passed since each stored address profile belonging to a given Chrome Profile was last used. This is logged for each Autofill address profile once per Chrome User Profile load.

histogram.name: Autofill.DaysSincePreviousUseAtSubmission.Profile
histogram.summary: Logs the number of days between most recent use without modification and the penultimate use of the profile when submitting a credit card form.

histogram.name: Autofill.DeveloperEngagement
histogram.summary: Measures the adoption of the HTML autocomplete type hint specification (see http://is.gd/whatwg_autocomplete for more details). For each fillable form detected, logs whether that form includes author-specified type hints.

histogram.name: Autofill.FieldCount
histogram.summary: Number of fields in a form that Autofill encounters.

histogram.name: Autofill.FieldPrediction
histogram.summary: Predicted and actual form field type. This is a computed sparse histogram where the value is ((predicted << 16)| actual). Predicted and actual are taken from the autofill::ServerFieldType enumeration.

histogram.name: Autofill.FieldPredictionQuality.Aggregate
histogram.summary: Aggregate Autofill field-type prediction outcomes. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Confusion_matrix for an explanation of the values.

histogram.name: Autofill.FieldPredictionQuality.ByFieldType
histogram.summary: Autofill field-type prediction outcomes, broken down by field type. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Confusion_matrix for an explanation of the values.

histogram.name: Autofill.FillDuration.FromInteraction.WithAutofill
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between the user's first interaction with a form and the form's submission, for an autofilled form.

histogram.name: Autofill.FillDuration.FromInteraction.WithoutAutofill
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between the user's first interaction with a form and the form's submission, for a non-autofilled form.

histogram.name: Autofill.FillDuration.FromLoad.WithAutofill
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between form load and form submission, for an autofilled form.

histogram.name: Autofill.FillDuration.FromLoad.WithoutAutofill
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between form load and form submission, for a non-autofilled form.

histogram.name: Autofill.FormEvents.Address
histogram.summary: Autofill form events for address forms. These are recorded when the user interacts with a form requesting an address.
histogram.details: Important caveat about submission metrics: - Submission using autofill data is determined by simply evaluating if there was a fill operation in this page. So, if the user filled with local data, completed erased or modified the data after and then submitted, we would only emit one "Submitted with server suggestion filled (once)"; - The submission segmentation works by checking what kind of data was last filled for this type of form in the page load. So, if a user initially filled with local data and after that filled with server, we will only emit "Submitted with server suggestion filled (once)".

histogram.name: Autofill.FormEvents.CreditCard
histogram.summary: Autofill form events for credit card forms. These are recorded when the user interacts with a form requesting a credit card.
histogram.details: Important caveat about submission metrics: - Submission using autofill data is determined by simply evaluating if there was a fill operation in this page. So, if the user filled with local data, completed erased or modified the data after and then submitted, we would only emit one "Submitted with server suggestion filled (once)"; - The submission segmentation works by checking what kind of data was last filled for this type of form in the page load. So, if a user initially filled with local data and after that filled with server, we will only emit "Submitted with server suggestion filled (once)".

histogram.name: Autofill.FormEvents.CreditCard.BankNameDisplayed
histogram.summary: Autofill form events for credit card forms. These are recorded when the user interacts with a form requesting a credit card, a dropdown of suggestions is shown and at least one of the suggestions has a bank name. Form events are logged at most once per page load.
histogram.details: These metrics are used to measure the impact of the bank name experiment. They are used to calculate the CTR of the autofill UI with bank names available. Not all credit cards will have bank names even if we launch the experiment. With these metrics we can run the experiment on 2 groups. For one group, we will show bank names if available. For the other, we won't shown.

histogram.name: Autofill.FormEvents.CreditCard.OnNonsecurePage
histogram.summary: Autofill form events for credit card forms on nonsecure pages. These are recorded when the user interacts with a form requesting a credit card.
histogram.details: Important caveat about submission metrics: - Submission using autofill data is determined by simply evaluating if there was a fill operation in this page. So, if the user filled with local data, completed erased or modified the data after and then submitted, we would only emit one "Submitted with server suggestion filled (once)"; - The submission segmentation works by checking what kind of data was last filled for this type of form in the page load. So, if a user initially filled with local data and after that filled with server, we will only emit "Submitted with server suggestion filled (once)".

histogram.name: Autofill.FormSubmittedState
histogram.summary: The autofill state related to a submitted form.

histogram.name: Autofill.HasModifiedProfile.CreditCardFormSubmission
histogram.summary: Whether user modified an address profile shortly before submitting a credit card form.

histogram.name: Autofill.HiddenOrPresentationalSelectFieldsFilled
histogram.summary: Logs when a hidden or presentational field is autofilled. The hidden or presentational fields are only autofilled for 'select' fields to support synthetic fields.

histogram.name: Autofill.IcuCollatorCreationSuccess
histogram.summary: Tracks whether Autofill was able to create the ICU collator successfully.

histogram.name: Autofill.InvalidProfileData.UsedForMetrics
histogram.summary: Tracks whether or not autofill suppressed sending votes or calculating quality metrics because the profile data was marked as invalid. Logged during field-type validation if/when a field marked as invalid is found to match the submitted data.

histogram.name: Autofill.InvalidProfileData.UsedForSuggestion
histogram.summary: Tracks whether or not autofill suppressed offering an autofill suggestion because the profile data was marked as invalid. Logged during autofill suggestion generation when a suggestion is about to generated based on a field marked as invalid.

histogram.name: Autofill.IsEnabled.PageLoad
histogram.summary: Tracks whether Autofill is enabled on page load for a page containing forms.

histogram.name: Autofill.IsEnabled.Startup
histogram.summary: Tracks whether Autofill is enabled when Chrome launches.

histogram.name: Autofill.KeyboardAccessoryButtonsIOS_ScreenReaderOff
histogram.summary: [iOS] Measures the frequency of button presses on the iOS Autofill keyboard accessory view when VoiceOver is off.

histogram.name: Autofill.KeyboardAccessoryButtonsIOS_ScreenReaderOn
histogram.summary: [iOS] Measures the frequency of button presses on the iOS Autofill keyboard accessory view when VoiceOver is on.

histogram.name: Autofill.LocalCardMigrationBubbleOffer
histogram.summary: Record events related to bubble showing. Logged when bubble is requested or is actually shown to users.

histogram.name: Autofill.LocalCardMigrationBubbleUserInteraction
histogram.summary: Record how bubble is closed by different user interactions.

histogram.name: Autofill.LocalCardMigrationDialogActiveDuration
histogram.summary: Record duration of the local card migration dialog being visible to users.

histogram.name: Autofill.LocalCardMigrationDialogOffer
histogram.summary: Record events related to showing the local card migration dialog.

histogram.name: Autofill.LocalCardMigrationDialogUserInteraction
histogram.summary: Record user interactions related to local card migration dialog.

histogram.name: Autofill.LocalCardMigrationDialogUserSelectionPercentage
histogram.summary: Record the percentage of cards selected by the user in the migration dialog.

histogram.name: Autofill.LocalCardMigrationOrigin
histogram.summary: Records when local card migration prompts are shown and/or accepted.

histogram.name: Autofill.MacAddressBook
histogram.summary: When Chrome tries to access the user's Address Book, OSX presents a blocking dialog which disrupts the user experience. A new Chrome feature has been introduced wherein Chrome only shows this blocking dialog if the user explicitly asked Chrome to access the user's Address Book. If a form's field looks like it might support Autofill suggestions from the user's Address Book and there are no other suggestions, Chrome shows an Autofill entry that prompts the user to give Chrome access to the user's Address Book. This histogram tracks the frequency that this Autofill entry is presented, and the frequency that this Autofill entry is selected.

histogram.name: Autofill.MacAddressBook.AcceptedSuggestionIsFromAddressBook
histogram.summary: This metric is emitted each time the user accepts an Autofill suggestion. It records whether the result is from the Address Book.

histogram.name: Autofill.MacAddressBook.AccessTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time spent accessing the OSX Address Book the first time after Chrome was launched. If this time is larger than ~100ms, this it is likely that the user was shown a blocking, modal dialog.

histogram.name: Autofill.MacAddressBook.ContainedMeCard
histogram.summary: After a Chrome is given access to the Mac Address Book, whether the Address Book contained a Me card.

histogram.name: Autofill.MacAddressBook.MeCard.HadAddress
histogram.summary: When Chrome is given access to the Me Card of the Address Book, whether the card has an address that contained a street number and either a city or zip code.

histogram.name: Autofill.MacAddressBook.MeCard.HadEmail
histogram.summary: When Chrome is given access to the Me Card of the Address Book, whether the card has an email.

histogram.name: Autofill.MacAddressBook.MeCard.HadName
histogram.summary: When Chrome is given access to the Me Card of the Address Book, whether the card has a name.

histogram.name: Autofill.MacAddressBook.MeCard.HadPhoneNumber
histogram.summary: When Chrome is given access to the Me Card of the Address Book, whether the card has a phone number.

histogram.name: Autofill.MacAddressBook.NumShowsBeforeSelected
histogram.summary: The number of times that the access Address Book prompt has been shown when the user selects the prompt.

histogram.name: Autofill.ManageCardsPrompt
histogram.summary: The frequency of user interactions with the Manage Cards prompt.

histogram.name: Autofill.NumberOfEditedAutofilledFieldsAtSubmission
histogram.summary: The number of autofilled fields that were subsequently edited prior to the form being submitted.

histogram.name: Autofill.NumberOfProfilesConsideredForDedupe
histogram.summary: The number of Autofill profiles that have been considered for deduplication.

histogram.name: Autofill.NumberOfProfilesRemovedDuringDedupe
histogram.summary: The number of Autofill profiles that have been removed during deduplication.

histogram.name: Autofill.NumElementsMatchesNumFields
histogram.summary: Records whether the number of fillable form elements matches the number of fields in the form's description. Instances where this is recorded as False, denote cases where autofill failed to preview/fill an autofill suggestion. This can happen when a fields is filtered out of a formless form.

histogram.name: Autofill.PasswordFormQueryVolume
histogram.summary: Tracks the increased load on the Autofill server if the restriction on querying for password forms with fewer than 3 fields were omitted.

histogram.name: Autofill.PayloadCompressionRatio
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 1/2016, autofill payload compression was removed.
histogram.summary: Compression ratio of the query and upload payload that are sent to the Autofill server. The payload is compressed using gzip.

histogram.name: Autofill.PaymentsCustomerDataBillingIdIsValid
histogram.summary: When PaymentsCustomerData is used to send a request to Google Payments, we log the validity state of the billing customer ID.

histogram.name: Autofill.PaymentsCustomerDataBillingIdStatus
histogram.summary: When PaymentsCustomerData is used to send a request to Google Payments, we log the validity state of the billing customer ID.

histogram.name: Autofill.ProfileActionOnFormSubmitted
histogram.summary: The profile action that took place when a form was submitted.

histogram.name: AutoFill.ProfileCount
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 3/2011, replaced by Autofill.StoredProfileCount.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of Autofill address profiles a user has.

histogram.name: AutoFill.Quality
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 3/2011, replaced by Autofill.Quality.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The quality of the AutoFill implementation.

histogram.name: Autofill.Quality
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 2/2014 (M35), replaced by Autofill.UserHappiness.
histogram.summary: The quality of the Autofill implementation.

histogram.name: Autofill.Quality.HeuristicType
histogram.summary: The quality of Autofill's heuristic field type detection.

histogram.name: Autofill.Quality.HeuristicType.ByFieldType
histogram.summary: The quality of Autofill's heuristic field type detection, broken down by the specific field type. Fields with multiple possible types (based on the stored Autofill data) are logged as having ambiguous type.

histogram.name: Autofill.Quality.PredictedType
histogram.summary: The overall quality of the Autofill field type predictions.

histogram.name: Autofill.Quality.PredictedType.ByFieldType
histogram.summary: The overall quality of the Autofill field type predictions, broken down by the specific field type. Fields with multiple possible types (based on the stored Autofill data) are logged as having ambiguous type.

histogram.name: Autofill.Quality.ServerType
histogram.summary: The quality of the Autofill server's field type detection.

histogram.name: Autofill.Quality.ServerType.ByFieldType
histogram.summary: The quality of the Autofill server's field type detection, broken down by the specific field type. Fields with multiple possible types (based on the stored Autofill data) are logged as having ambiguous type.

histogram.name: Autofill.QueriedCreditCardFormIsSecure
histogram.summary: For credit card forms that are queried for Autofill, a ratio of how many are within secure contexts (which includes mixed passive content).

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.BackoffDelay
histogram.summary: The delay of a network request for a query due to exponential backoff.

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.FailingPayloadSize
histogram.summary: The number of bytes that were sent in a query that subsequently failed. This is to help determine the appropriate threshold at which autofill queries should switch from GET to POST, and if there are certain sizes of queries which are prone to failure.

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.HttpResponseCode
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 8/2018, replaced by Autofill.Query.HttpResponseOrErrorCode.
histogram.summary: The HTTP response code returned on a query.

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.HttpResponseOrErrorCode
histogram.summary: The http response code or net error code returned on a query.

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.Method
histogram.summary: The HTTP method used to query the autofill server.

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.RequestDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of a network request for a query.

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.WasInCache
histogram.summary: Was the autofill query response retrieved from the HTTP cache.

histogram.name: Autofill.RationalizationQuality.PhoneNumber
histogram.summary: The quality of fields rationalization, putting rationalization result into good/ok/bad categories.

histogram.name: Autofill.ResetFullServerCards.NumberOfCardsReset
histogram.summary: The number of full server cards that were reset.

histogram.name: Autofill.ResetFullServerCards.NumberOfCardsReset.DryRun
histogram.summary: The number of full server cards that would have been reset.

histogram.name: Autofill.ResetFullServerCards.SyncServiceNotActiveOnInitialized
histogram.summary: Records whether a sync service in a not active state is passed to Autofill OnSyncServiceInitialized.

histogram.name: Autofill.ResetFullServerCards.SyncServiceNullOnInitialized
histogram.summary: Records whether a null sync service is passed to Autofill OnSyncServiceInitialized.

histogram.name: Autofill.ResetFullServerCards.SyncServiceStatusOnStateChanged
histogram.summary: Records the sync service state when OnStateChanged is called.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCardCardholderNamePrefilled
histogram.summary: If the cardholder name fix flow is shown when credit card upload is offered, records if the cardholder name textfield was prefilled with the name from the user's Google Account.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCardCardholderNameWasEdited
histogram.summary: If the cardholder name fix flow is shown during credit card upload and the user accepts upload, logs whether the final cardholder name was changed from its prefilled value or not.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCardWithFirstAndLastNameComplete
histogram.summary: Records if a card was saved from a form that had split name fields.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCardWithFirstAndLastNameOffered
histogram.summary: Records if a card was offered to be saved from a form that had split name fields.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCreditCardPrompt
histogram.summary: The relative frequency with which users accept or deny the Autofill save credit card prompt.

histogram.name: Autofill.ScanCreditCard.Completed
histogram.summary: Whether a credit card scan was completed or cancelled.

histogram.name: Autofill.ScanCreditCard.Duration
histogram.summary: How long a credit card took to scan.

histogram.name: Autofill.ScanCreditCardPrompt
histogram.summary: Usage of the "Scan card" control item.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerExperimentId
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2011, replaced by Autofill.ServerExperimentId.Query.
histogram.summary: The experiment ID received in response to an Autofill server query.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerExperimentId.Query
histogram.summary: The experiment ID received in response to an Autofill server query.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerExperimentId.Upload
histogram.summary: The experiment ID received at the time of an Autofill upload.

histogram.name: AutoFill.ServerQueryResponse
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 3/2011, replaced by Autofill.ServerQueryResponse.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The usefulness of AutoFill server information.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerQueryResponse
histogram.summary: The usefulness of Autofill server information.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerResponseHasDataForForm
histogram.summary: Tracks whether Autofill server had at least some prediction data for a given form at query response time.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredCreditCardCount
histogram.summary: The number of credit cards a user has stored, measured at Chrome profile launch time.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredCreditCardDisusedCount
histogram.summary: The number of disused credit cards a user has stored, measured at Chrome profile launch time. This is only reported for users who have at least one stored credit card.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredLocalCreditCardCount
histogram.summary: The number of local credit cards a user has stored, measured at Chrome profile launch time.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredProfileCount
histogram.summary: The number of Autofill addresses a user has stored, measured at launch time.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredProfileCountAtAutofillableFormSubmission
histogram.summary: The number of Autofill addresses a user has stored, measured when an autofillable form is submitted.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredProfileDisusedCount
histogram.summary: The number of stored Autofill address profiles which have not been used in a sufficiently long time for autofill to consider them disused. Measured once per Chrome user profile launch.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredServerCreditCardCount
histogram.summary: The number of server credit cards a user has stored, measured at Chrome profile launch time.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.CreditCardSaveNotOfferedDueToMaxStrikes
histogram.summary: Records when credit card save is not offered (either at all on mobile or by simply not showing the bubble on desktop) due to the candidate card having too many strikes.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.NthStrikeAdded.CreditCardSave
histogram.summary: Records the number of "strikes" a given card has, when a user dismisses a prompt to save a new credit card. The strike count is incremented each time the user dismisses the prompt.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.StrikesPresentWhenLocalCardSaved
histogram.summary: Records the number of "strikes" a given card had when a user opted to save the card to Chrome.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.StrikesPresentWhenServerCardSaved
histogram.summary: Records the number of "strikes" a given card had when a user successfully uploaded the card to Google Payments.

histogram.name: Autofill.SubmittedCardState
histogram.summary: Metric to measure credit card state when form is submitted, specifically if it had a valid card number and/or expiration date.

histogram.name: Autofill.SubmittedServerCardExpirationStatus
histogram.summary: Metric to measure if a submitted card's expiration date matches the same server card's expiration date (unmasked or not). Cards are considered to be the same if they have the same card number (if unmasked) or if they have the same network and last four digits (if masked).

histogram.name: Autofill.SuggestionAcceptedIndex
histogram.summary: The index of the accepted Autofill suggestion in the popup.

histogram.name: Autofill.SuggestionAcceptedIndex.Autocomplete
histogram.summary: The index of the accepted Autocomplete suggestion in the popup.

histogram.name: Autofill.Timing.DetermineHeuristicTypes
histogram.summary: Number of milliseconds passed between the start and end of FormStructure::DetermineHeuristicTypes().

histogram.name: Autofill.Timing.ParseForm
histogram.summary: Number of milliseconds passed between the start and end of parsing a single form.

histogram.name: Autofill.Unknown.BackoffDelay
histogram.summary: The delay of a network request for which the download manager seems to have lost the request type due to exponential backoff. This should never be logged.

histogram.name: Autofill.Unknown.FailingPayloadSize
histogram.summary: The number of bytes that were sent in a request for which the download manager seems to have lost the request type. This should never be logged.

histogram.name: Autofill.Unknown.HttpResponseCode
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 8/2018, replaced by Autofill.Unknown.HttpResponseOrErrorCode.
histogram.summary: The HTTP response code returned on a request for which the download manager seems to have lost the request type. This should never be logged.

histogram.name: Autofill.Unknown.HttpResponseOrErrorCode
histogram.summary: The http response code or net error code returned on a request for which the download manager seems to have lost the request type. This should never be logged.

histogram.name: Autofill.Unknown.RequestDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of a network request for which the download manager seems to have lost the request type. This should never be logged.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.Duration
histogram.summary: Duration of the interaction with the UnmaskPrompt since it was shown until it was closed. This prompt is triggered when a user selects a masked card in an autofill dropdown.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.Events
histogram.summary: Events tracking the usage of the unmasking prompt. This prompt is triggered when a user selects a masked card in a autofill dropdown.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.GetRealPanDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of the GetRealPan API call. This API call is triggered by the unmasking prompt when the user types information to verify their ownership of the card being fetched.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.GetRealPanResult
histogram.summary: Tracks the result of the GetRealPan API call. This API call is triggered by the unmasking prompt when the user types information to verify their ownership of the card being fetched.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.TimeBeforeAbandonUnmasking
histogram.summary: Time the user waited before abandoning an unmasking operation. In such a scenario, the user is cancelling a pending unmasking operation (possibly because it is taking too long). The time measured here corresponds to the time between the user clicking the "Verify" button and then clicking "Cancel".

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.UnmaskingDuration
histogram.summary: Duration between the user clicking the Verify button within the unmasking prompt until we get a response back from the server. This prompt is triggered when a user selects a masked card in an autofill dropdown.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnownedFieldsWereFiltered
histogram.summary: Records incidents where an implicit (formless) form has elements filtered out of it. This happens to unowned fields (directly embedded in the page with no enclosing <form> tag) without autocomplete attributes if formless forms are restricted to "checkout" pages. Logged when parsing forms/fields.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.BackoffDelay
histogram.summary: The delay of a network request for an upload due to exponential backoff.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.FailingPayloadSize
histogram.summary: The number of bytes that were sent in an upload that subsequently failed. This is to help determine if there are certain upload sizes which are prone to failure.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.HttpResponseCode
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 8/2018, replaced by Autofill.Upload.HttpResponseOrErrorCode.
histogram.summary: The HTTP response code returned on an upload.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.HttpResponseOrErrorCode
histogram.summary: The http response code or net error code returned on an upload.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.RequestDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of a network request for an upload.

histogram.name: Autofill.UploadAcceptedCardOrigin
histogram.summary: Measures if a card for which upload was accepted is already stored as a local card on the device or if it has not yet been seen.

histogram.name: Autofill.UploadEvent
histogram.summary: Whether or not an upload was sent after having been triggered by a form submission or proxy-form-submission.

histogram.name: Autofill.UploadOfferedCardOrigin
histogram.summary: Measures if a card for which upload was offered is already stored as a local on the device or if it has not yet been seen.

histogram.name: Autofill.UserHappiness
histogram.summary: Measures the frequency of various events in the Autofill user interaction flow. By comparing frequencies, we can compute several interesting "user happiness" metrics.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletAddressConversionType
histogram.summary: Tracks how the different wallet addresses are converted to local autofill profiles.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletAddressesAdded
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet addresses that were added to Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletAddressesAddedOrRemoved
histogram.summary: The total number of Wallet addresses that were added to or removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletAddressesRemoved
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet addresses that were removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletCardsAdded
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet credit cards that were added to Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletCardsAddedOrRemoved
histogram.summary: The total number of Wallet credit cards that were added to or removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletCardsRemoved
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet credit cards that were removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.AutofillSession
histogram.summary: Records the state of an autofill session.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.CreatedByActivityContext
histogram.summary: Whether the autofill is created by activity context.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.Enabled
histogram.summary: Whether the autofill service is enabled in Android platform.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.SubmissionSource
histogram.summary: Records the source of form submission.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.SuggestionTime
histogram.summary: The time taken to display suggestion.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.UserChangedAutofilledField
histogram.summary: Whether the user changed autofilled field.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.EventDispatchFailure.Dispatch
histogram.summary: Records the Service Worker status code that caused a Background Fetch API event to fail dispatching whilst trying to dispatch an event on the active Service Worker.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.EventDispatchFailure.FindWorker
histogram.summary: Records the Service Worker status code that caused a Background Fetch API event to fail dispatching whilst trying to find the Service Worker.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.EventDispatchFailure.StartWorker
histogram.summary: Records the Service Worker status code that caused a Background Fetch API event to fail dispatching whilst trying to start the active Service Worker.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.EventDispatchResult
histogram.summary: Records the result of dispatching one of the Background Fetch API events to the Service Worker associated with the event.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.HasDuplicateRequests
histogram.summary: Records whether the fetch requests contains any duplicate URLs. This is called after the provided requests are verified, but before the fetch is started.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.HasRequestsWithBody
histogram.summary: Records whether the background fetch contains any requests with a body. Called before the fetch is started.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.IncompleteFetchesOnStartup
histogram.summary: Records the number of Background Fetch registrations found on browser start-up. This is called after the initialization database task runs successfully.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.Manager
histogram.summary: Records the duration of the mojo call for the exposed BackgroundFetchManager APIs.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.RegistrationCreatedError
histogram.summary: Records the error after the Background Fetch Data Manager creates a registration.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.RegistrationDeletedError
histogram.summary: Records the error after the Background Fetch Data Manager deletes a registration.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.SchedulerFinishedError
histogram.summary: Records the error after the Background Fetch Data Manager marks a registration for deletion. This is called when the Scheduler Controller is finished.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.Storage
histogram.summary: Records the storage error when running a database task.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.BackgroundApplicationsCount
histogram.summary: The number of background applications (extensions) for a profile that were loaded after the profile was registered with the BackgroundModeManager.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.BackgroundModeEnabledPrefChanged
histogram.summary: Logged if BackgroundModeManager is running and listening for pref changes, and the pref does indeed change. The new value is logged.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.MenuItemClick
histogram.summary: Logged when an item in the system tray icon menu is clicked.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.OnStartup.AutoLaunchState
histogram.summary: Logged during BackgroundModeManager's initialization. Indicates the AutoLaunchState the current browser process was launched in.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.OnStartup.IsBackgroundModePrefEnabled
histogram.summary: Logged during BackgroundModeManager's initialization. Indicates the state of the kBackgroundModeEnabled pref on startup.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.TimeBeforeOptimizedRestart
histogram.summary: The duration of a session before the browser got restarted in the background by the BackgroundOptimizer to purge the memory.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.BatchSize
histogram.summary: Records the number of sync events which were fired in a batch. A batch is defined as the set of sync events dispatched at the same time by the BackgroundSyncManager. One-shots usually run individually (a batch of one), unless the device was offline and multiple are waiting for the device to go back online.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.OneShotResult
histogram.obsolete: As of 10/2015 this has been replaced by BackgroundSync.Event.OneShotResultPattern.
histogram.summary: Records whether a one-shot sync event succeeded or failed.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.OneShotResultPattern
histogram.summary: Records whether a periodic sync event succeeded or failed and whether the sync event finished in the foreground or background.
histogram.details: A sync event finished in the foreground if the associated Service Worker Registration has a client at the time that the event finished.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.OneShotStartedInForeground
histogram.summary: Records whether a one-shot sync started firing in the foreground or background. Called shortly before the event is fired.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.PeriodicResult
histogram.obsolete: As of 10/2015 this has been replaced by BackgroundSync.Event.PeriodicResultPattern.
histogram.summary: Records whether a periodic sync event succeeded or failed.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.PeriodicResultPattern
histogram.summary: Records whether a one-shot sync event succeeded or failed and whether the sync event finished in the foreground or background.
histogram.details: A sync event finished in the foreground if the associated Service Worker Registration has a client at the time that the event finished.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.PeriodicStartedInForeground
histogram.summary: Records whether a periodic sync event started firing in the foreground or background. Called shortly before the event is fired.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.Time
histogram.summary: Time taken to execute a batch of sync events. A batch is defined as the set of sync events dispatched at the same time by the BackgroundSyncManager. Periodic syncs often run in a batch. One-shots usually run individually (a batch of one), unless the device was offline and multiple are waiting for the device to go back online.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.LaunchTask.CancelSuccess
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to cancel a future browser launch using the GCM Network Manager on Android.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.LaunchTask.PlayServicesAvailable
histogram.summary: Records whether Google Play Services is available to the Background Sync system on Android, for scheduling future sync events when the browser is not running. This will be logged at most once per run.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.LaunchTask.ScheduleSuccess
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to schedule a future browser launch using the GCM Network Manager on Android.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.NetworkObserver.HasPermission
histogram.summary: Records whether the browser has sufficient permissions to create a BackgroundSyncNetworkObserver object on Android, at the point when it tries to create it. This should never be false, but is being tracked in order to help resolve crbug.com/545473.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.OneShot
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to register a one-shot sync.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.OneShot.CouldFire
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to register a one-shot sync in a situation where the sync could fire immediately.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.OneShot.IsDuplicate
histogram.summary: Records whether a one-shot sync registration exactly duplicates an existing registered sync.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.Periodic
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to register a periodic sync.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.Periodic.IsDuplicate
histogram.summary: Records whether a periodic sync registration exactly duplicates an existing registered sync.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Unregistration.OneShot
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to unregister a one-shot sync.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Unregistration.Periodic
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to unregister a periodic sync.

histogram.name: BackgroundTracing.SamplingProfilerUnwindResult
histogram.summary: Records the reason for unwind failues in sampling profiler.

histogram.name: BatteryStatus.NumberBatteriesLinux
histogram.summary: Number of batteries reported by the UPower service on Linux at the start of the Battery Status API.

histogram.name: BatteryStatus.NumberBatteriesMac
histogram.summary: Number of internal batteries reported by MacOS at the start of the Battery Status API.

histogram.name: BatteryStatus.NumberBatteriesWin
histogram.summary: Number of batteries reported by Windows at the start of the Battery Status API.

histogram.name: BatteryStatus.StartAndroid
histogram.summary: Whether the Battery Status API was successfully started up on Android.

histogram.name: Blacklist.Blocked
histogram.summary: Records the name hashes of all the dlls that are blocked from the browser process.

histogram.name: Blacklist.PatchedInRenderer
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times a renderer process is started with the browser blacklist patch. This should never be hit.

histogram.name: Blacklist.RetryAttempts.Success
histogram.summary: Records the number of attempts needed before the blacklist is properly set up. This is logged immediately after a successful setup.

histogram.name: Blacklist.Setup
histogram.summary: Records the successes and failures when running the browser blacklist setup code. Used to determine if the blacklist is working as intended during startup (since the blacklist runs before crash reporting is set up). This only occurs on Windows.

histogram.name: Blimp.Compositor.CommitSizeKb
histogram.summary: Records the size of the uncompressed commit messages received by Blimp compositor.

histogram.name: Blimp.Connected
histogram.summary: Records whether connection attempt to the Blimp engine was successful.

histogram.name: Blimp.Supported
histogram.summary: Records whether Blimp is supported.

histogram.name: Blimp.Tab.Visible
histogram.summary: Records whenever a Blimp tab toggles visibility.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.CreateV8ContextForMainFrame
histogram.summary:
Time spent to create a v8::Context instance during a page loading in the main-frame window.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.CreateV8ContextForNonMainFrame
histogram.summary:
Time spent to create a v8::Context instance during a page loading in a non-main-frame window, e.g. iframe.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeMainLocalWindowProxy
histogram.summary:
Time spent initializing LocalWindowProxy during a page loading in main windows.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeMainRemoteWindowProxy
histogram.summary:
Time spent initializing RemoteWindowProxy during a page loading in main frame of OOPIF.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeMainWindowProxy
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2017. This metric was split into two metrics depending if it figures time for local window proxies or remote ones.
histogram.summary: Time spent initializing WindowProxy during a page loading in main windows.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeNonMainLocalWindowProxy
histogram.summary:
Time spent initializing LocalWindowProxy during a page loading in non-main windows, e.g. iframe.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeNonMainRemoteWindowProxy
histogram.summary:
Time spent initializing RemoteWindowProxy during a page loading in OOPIF.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeNonMainWindowProxy
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2017. This metric was split into two metrics depending if it figures time for local window proxies or remote ones.
histogram.summary: Time spent initializing WindowProxy during a page loading in non-main windows, e.g. iframe.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeWindowProxy
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 10/2016. This metric was split into two metrics depending if it figures time for main windows.
histogram.summary: Time spent initializing WindowProxy during a page loading.

histogram.name: Blink.BudgetAPI.QueryBudget
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 08/2018 (M70) with the removal of the Budget API.
histogram.summary: Whenever the Budget API framework receives a query for the current budget, this records the total budget available to the origin, which is an internal Chrome value for the amount of background processing an origin can do without visibly alerting the user. Scale for the budget is 0 to 100. Care should be taken when drawing conclusions from this metric, as budget is based on both engagement and usage, so could vary significantly based on user behaviour and origin.

histogram.name: Blink.BudgetAPI.Reserve
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 08/2018 (M70) with the removal of the Budget API.
histogram.summary: Whenever the Budget API framework receives a request to reserve budget for a future background operation, this records whether the reserve request succeeded or not.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.2DLayerBridgeIsDeferred
histogram.summary: Records if a 2D Layer Bridge is using deferred rendering when it is destroyed. Emitted in Canvas2DLayerBridge::~Canvas2DLayerBridge.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ContextType
histogram.summary: Records the context type names used to create canvas rendering contexts.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.DrawImage
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas drawImage API call.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.DrawImage.Duration
histogram.summary:
Time spent on the main thread during a 2D canvas drawImage API call.
Note: This metric drops reports on clients with low-resolution clocks, which means these reports will be biased against a portion of the population on Windows. See Windows.HasHighResolutionTimeTicks for the affected sample.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.GetImageData
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas getImageData API call.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.GetImageDataScaledDuration
histogram.summary:
Time in microseconds * 10 (10e-5 seconds) spent on the 2D canvas getImageData API call, divided by the square root of the total number of pixels of the image extracted from the canvas. Smaller is faster.
The idea behind this elaborated unit is that the bigger the area of a canvas the longer the operation GetImageData takes, so we want a way to measure the overall performance regardless of the size of the canvas.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.IsComposited
histogram.summary: Records if the canvas context is composited or not. Recorded in HTMLCanvasElement::GetCanvasRenderingContext.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.NumCanvasesPerPage
histogram.summary: When a Document is shutdown, reports the number of canvases on that page.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.OffscreenCommitTimer
histogram.summary:
Wall clock durations of OffscreenCanvas.commit() calls.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.PutImageData
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas putImageData API call.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.PutImageDataScaledDuration
histogram.summary:
Time in microseconds * 10 (10e-5 seconds) spent on the 2D canvas putImageData API call, divided by the square root of the total number of pixels of the imageData painted into the canvas. Smaller is faster.
The idea behind this elaborated unit is that the bigger the area of a canvas the longer the operation PutImageData takes, so we want a way to measure the overall performance regardless of the size of the canvas.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ResourceProviderIsAccelerated
histogram.summary: Logs if the canvas resource provider is accelerated or not.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ResourceProviderType
histogram.summary: Records the type of resource provider used for a canvas (texture, bitmap or shared bitmap).

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.SqrtNumberOfPixels
histogram.summary: Stores the square root of the number of pixels in a new or resized offscreen canvas. Emitted from CanvasRenderingContextHost::RecordCanvasSizeToUMA.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlob.CompleteEncodingDelay
histogram.summary:
This metric measures the total time spent on completing encoding all the rows of an image (jpeg or png), as part of a canvas.toBlob API call. Encoding occurs during one or more idle periods on the main thread and the waiting time of the next idle period is included in the measurement. If the code has swtiched to force encoding path, we will not measure the delay in this metric.
This metric is useful in helping us adjust the IdleTaskCompleteTimeoutDelay in canvas.toBlob. When the encoding idle task is delayed for longer than IdleTaskCompleteTimeoutDelay, the browser will switch to a non-idle task to force encoding to happen on the main thread.
In addition, metric values from OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob API call are also gathered into this histogram, because the logic flow is exactly the same as canvas.toBlob. It's worth to note that the values can come from idle tasks on either main or worker thread.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlob.IdleEncodeDuration
histogram.summary: This metric measures the total time spent on encoding all the rows of an image (jpeg or png), excluding the waiting time of next idle periods. This is part of a canvas.toBlob API call. Encoding occurs during one or more idle periods on the main thread.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlob.IdleTaskStatus
histogram.summary:
Records the status of the idle task when finishing a toBlob call.
In addition, metric values from OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob API call are also gathered into this histogram, because the logic flow is exactly the same as canvas.toBlob. It's worth to note that the values can come from idle tasks on either main or worker thread.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlob.InitiateEncodingDelay
histogram.summary:
This metric measures the time spent from initiating image encoding (jpeg or png) on idle task to the actual execution time of initiation, as part of a canvas.toBlob API call.
This metric is useful in helping us adjust the IdleTaskStartTimeoutDelay in canvas.toBlob. When the initialization idle task is delayed for longer than IdleTaskStartTimeoutDelay, the browser will switch to a non-idle task to force initialization and encoding to occur on the main thread.
In addition, metric values from OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob API call are also gathered into this histogram, because the logic flow is exactly the same as canvas.toBlob. It's worth to note that the values can come from idle tasks on either main or worker thread.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlobDuration
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas toBlob API call.
In addition, metric values from OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob API call are also gathered into this histogram, because the logic flow is exactly the same as canvas.toBlob. It's worth to note that the values can come from idle tasks on either main or worker thread.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToDataURL
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas toDataURL API call.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.CheckerableImageCount
histogram.summary: The number of images which could have been checkered (async decoded) for a performance gain, split by the DOM element type on which they were used. This is recorded each time an image for an element created by the page is loaded, either from the network or the local cache. The browser heuristics define an image to be checkerable if it is expected to have a long decode duration. Since these images may be on the rendering critical path, decoding them asychronously from painting other content can provide better performance be reducing overall content checkerboarding.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorGamut.Destination
histogram.summary: Gamut properties of destination color space.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorGamut.Source
histogram.summary: Gamut properties of image color space.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination
histogram.summary: Gamma properties of destination color space.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.ExtractedRawData
histogram.summary: Whether or not the output color space ICC profile was able to produce raw transfer function tables.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.ICCResult
histogram.summary: The result of analyzing the destiation color space's ICC profile.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.LinearFitError
histogram.obsolete: Linear fit found to be always inaccurate on 2017-02-28.
histogram.summary: The L-infinity error (in 8-bit values) of the numerical approximation of table-based ICC profile transfer functions, when falling back to a linear approximation.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.Matrix
histogram.summary: Whether or not to-XYZD50 matrix was extracted from the output color space ICC profile.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.NonlinearFitConverged
histogram.obsolete: Nonlinear fit code moved to skcms on 2018-04-25.
histogram.summary: Whether or not the nonlinear least squares fit of the table-based ICC profile transfer function for a single channel converged.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.NonlinearFitError
histogram.obsolete: Nonlinear fit code moved to skcms on 2018-04-25.
histogram.summary: The L-infinity error (in 8-bit values) of the numerical approximation of table-based ICC profile transfer functions for a single channel, when the nonlinear approximation converged.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.NonlinearFitErrorCombined
histogram.summary: The L-infinity error (in 8-bit values) of the numerical approximation of table-based ICC profile transfer functions for all channels combined, when the nonlinear approximation converged.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.Numerical
histogram.summary: Whether or not the output color space ICC profile has a numerical transfer function.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.OneMinusTMax
histogram.obsolete: TMax found to almost always be 1 on 2017-02-28.
histogram.summary: One minus the maximum value of table-based ICC profile transfer functions as an 8-bit fixed-point value. This histogram is temporary, to see if we can bake in the assumption that it will almost always be 255.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.TMin
histogram.obsolete: TMin found to almost always be 0 on 2017-02-28.
histogram.summary: The minimum value of table-based ICC profile transfer functions as an 8-bit fixed-point value. This histogram is temporary, to see if we can bake in the assumption that it will almost always be 0.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Source
histogram.summary: Gamma properties of image color space.

histogram.name: Blink.Compositing.LayerPromotionCount
histogram.summary: Number of composited layers based on various reasons. Recorded when a page finishes its layer updates.

histogram.name: Blink.Compositing.UpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating compositing in the Blink document lifecycle. This is the legacy compositing approach. BlinkGenPropertyTrees launches part of the new compositing approach and will use both this and Blink.CompositingCommit.UpdateTime.
Note: As of M70, this histogram has stopped recording metrics on machines with low-resolution clocks.

histogram.name: Blink.CompositingCommit.UpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating and pushing layers at the end of the paint step in the Blink document lifecycle. This is the new compositing approach in slimming paint V2 and is largely implemented by PaintArtifactCompositor. BlinkGenPropertyTrees is an incremental launch of part of the new compositing approach and will use both this and Blink.Compositing.UpdateTime.
Note: As of M70, this histogram has stopped recording metrics on machines with low-resolution clocks.

histogram.name: Blink.CookieJar.SyncCookiesSetTime
histogram.summary:
Microseconds per sync IPC call to set cookies.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.CookieJar.SyncCookiesTime
histogram.summary:
Microseconds per sync IPC call to fetch cookies.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImage.CanvasExpanded
histogram.summary: The original canvas dimensions were sufficient to determine image size. This is logged once per image header decode, which happens typically twice per image on the page.

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImage.EffectiveDimensionsLocation
histogram.summary: How many bytes of the file were read before an image width and height were determined. This is logged once per image header decode, which happens typically twice per image on the page.

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImage.JpegDensity
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 08/23/2018. Replaced by Blink.DecodedImage.JpegDensity.*
histogram.summary: The compressed image density measured in 0.01 bits per pixel. This is logged once per image load after the whole image is loaded and only for JPEGs with at least 100 pixels on each dimension.

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImage.JpegDensity.1000px
histogram.summary: The compressed image density measured in 0.01 bits per pixel. This is logged once per image load after the whole image is loaded and only for JPEGs with at least 1000 pixels on the smallest dimension (width or height).

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImage.JpegDensity.100px
histogram.summary: The compressed image density measured in 0.01 bits per pixel. This is logged once per image load after the whole image is loaded and only for JPEGs with at least 100 pixels on the smallest dimension (width or height) but less than 400 pixels on the smallest dimension.

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImage.JpegDensity.400px
histogram.summary: The compressed image density measured in 0.01 bits per pixel. This is logged once per image load after the whole image is loaded and only for JPEGs with at least 400 pixels on the smallest dimension (width or height) but less than 1000 pixels on the smallest dimension.

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImage.Orientation
histogram.summary: Image orientation inferred during decode.

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImage.WebPFileFormat
histogram.summary: WebP file format parsed during Blink's header parsing phase (which should happen only once per image).

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImage.XCanvasExpansion
histogram.summary: How much the canvas width needed to be expanded as a result of the first frame's width and x-offset being larger than the initial canvas width. This is logged once per image header decode, which happens typically twice per image on the page.

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImage.YCanvasExpansion
histogram.summary: How much the canvas height needed to be expanded as a result of the first frame's height and y-offset being larger than the initial canvas height. This is logged once per image header decode, which happens typically twice per image on the page.

histogram.name: Blink.DecodedImageType
histogram.summary: Image codec inferred during decode.

histogram.name: Blink.EventListenerDuration.Resize
histogram.summary: Time it takes to execute all the `resize` event listeners on a page when a resize event is fired. Tracked only for the main frame.

histogram.name: Blink.Fetch.RequestResourceTime
histogram.summary:
The total microseconds spent in ResourceFetcher::requestResource.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Fonts.HarfBuzzFaceZeroCopyAccess
histogram.summary: Counts success or failure of attempting to access font tables using the zero copy instantiation method in the HarfBuzz integration layer. This value is only recorded on non-Mac platforms. Reported each time a HarfBuzz face object is created.

histogram.name: Blink.Fonts.ShapeCache
histogram.summary: Total number of entries in all Blink ShapeCache instances. The value is reported each time the FontCache::purge() method is called.

histogram.name: Blink.Fonts.VariableFontsRatio
histogram.summary: Tracks adoption ratio of variable fonts compared to conventional (in the sense of non-variable) web fonts. Recorded at the time of instantiating a Skia SkTypeface from the successfully decoded web font blob.

histogram.name: Blink.ForcedStyleAndLayout.UpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time spent computing layouts due to Javascript value requests. Specifically, time spent in Blink Document::UpdateStyleAndLayoutIgnorePendingStylesheets.
This histogram only records metrics on machines with high-resolution clocks.

histogram.name: Blink.Gesture.Merged
histogram.summary: How many times two user gesture tokens were merged by UserGestureIndicator, and whether each token had an active gesture. One of the new token's gestures, if any, is moved to the old token.

histogram.name: Blink.ImageDecoders.Jpeg.Area
histogram.summary: Number of pixels in a decoded JPEG image. Recorded after decoding is done by Blink's JPEG decoder (as opposed to, possibly, a hardware decode accelerator). Tracking the image area may include degenerate cases, e.g., an image with area = 10000 and dimensions = 1x10000. However, it is reasonable to assume these cases are relatively rare and the area of the image can serve as a proxy of how useful a decode accelerator can be (the larger the area, the better).

histogram.name: Blink.ImageDecoders.Jpeg.ColorSpace
histogram.summary: JPEG color space of a decoded image as guessed by libjpeg_turbo. Recorded after decoding is done by Blink's JPEG decoder (as opposed to, possibly, a hardware decode accelerator). When the color space is YCbCr, the chroma subsampling is also recorded.

histogram.name: Blink.IntersectionObservation.UpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time spent computing IntersectionObserver observations in the Blink document lifecycle.
Note: As of M70, this histogram has stopped recording metrics on machines with low-resolution clocks.

histogram.name: Blink.KeyboardLock.MethodCalled
histogram.summary: Records each call to the navigator.keyboard{Lock|Unlock} methods.

histogram.name: Blink.LazyLoad.CrossOriginFrames.InitialDeferralAction
histogram.summary: Records the initial lazy loading action taken for a cross-origin iframe.

histogram.name: Blink.LazyLoad.CrossOriginFrames.LoadStartedAfterBeingDeferred
histogram.summary: Records the effective connection type whenever a lazily-loaded iframe that wasn't initially hidden or near the viewport starts loading. The count for an effective connection type in this histogram can be subtracted from the 'Deferred' bucket from the Blink.LazyLoad.CrossOriginFrames.InitialDeferralAction.* histogram for the corresponding effective connection type to determine the number of iframes that LazyLoad is avoiding to load altogether.

histogram.name: Blink.LazyLoad.CrossOriginFrames.VisibleAfterBeingDeferred
histogram.summary: Records the effective connection type whenever a lazily-loaded iframe that wasn't initially hidden or near the viewport becomes visible. The count for an effective connection type in this histogram can be subtracted from the corresponding count in Blink.LazyLoad.CrossOriginFrames.LoadStartedAfterBeingDeferred to determine how many iframes were loaded unnecessarily by LazyLoad.

histogram.name: Blink.MainFrame.CompositingCommitRatio
histogram.summary: The percentage of time between a BeginMainFrame and paint results commit in Blink that is used for committing paint results to the compositor.

histogram.name: Blink.MainFrame.CompositingRatio
histogram.summary: The percentage of time between a BeginMainFrame and paint results commit in Blink that is used for computing Compositing.

histogram.name: Blink.MainFrame.ForcedStyleAndLayoutRatio
histogram.summary: The time between each paint results commit used in computing forced style recalc and layouts for this document and ancestors as a percentage of the Blink local frame lifecycle update time.

histogram.name: Blink.MainFrame.IntersectionObservationRatio
histogram.summary: The percentage of time between a BeginMainFrame and paint results commit in Blink that is used for computing Intersection Observations.

histogram.name: Blink.MainFrame.PaintRatio
histogram.summary: The percentage of time between a BeginMainFrame and paint results commit in Blink that is used for computing Paint.

histogram.name: Blink.MainFrame.PrePaintRatio
histogram.summary: The percentage of time between a BeginMainFrame and paint results commit in Blink that is used for computing PrePaint.

histogram.name: Blink.MainFrame.StyleAndLayoutRatio
histogram.summary: The percentage of time between a BeginMainFrame and paint results commit in Blink that is used for computing Style and Layout.

histogram.name: Blink.MainFrame.UpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time between a BeginMainFrame and paint results commit in Blink.
This histogram will not record metrics on machines with low-resolution clocks.

histogram.name: Blink.MediaDocument.DownloadButton
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 08/2017 as it is integrated into media controller.
histogram.summary: Records how the download button on the MediaDocument is used.

histogram.name: Blink.MediaElement.Autoplay
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 11/2016 in Issue 666370 with the deprecation of Autoplay experiment.
histogram.summary: Records user observation and interaction with Media HTML elements that feature autoplay, either script-controlled or through the attribute.

histogram.name: Blink.MemoryCache.RevalidationPolicy
histogram.summary: RevalidationPolicy used for non-preloading requests for each resource type. https://crbug.com/579496

histogram.name: Blink.MemoryCache.RevalidationPolicy.Dead
histogram.summary: RevalidationPolicy used for requests that hit Resource only referenced from MemoryCache for each resource type. https://crbug.com/579496 Requests counted by this are also counted by Blink.MemoryCache.RevalidationPolicy or Blink.MemoryCache.RevalidationPolicy.Preload.

histogram.name: Blink.MemoryCache.RevalidationPolicy.Preload
histogram.summary: RevalidationPolicy used for preloading requests for each resource type. https://crbug.com/579496

histogram.name: Blink.OffscreenCanvas.ContextType
histogram.summary: Records the context type names used to create offscreen canvas rendering contexts. Recorded in OffscreenCanvas::GetCanvasRenderingContext

histogram.name: Blink.OffscreenCanvas.NewOffscreenCanvas
histogram.summary: Records the creation of a new offscreen canvas in OffscreenCanvas::Create.

histogram.name: Blink.OffscreenCanvas.SqrtNumberOfPixels
histogram.summary: Stores the square root of the number of pixels in a new or resized offscreen canvas. Emitted from CanvasRenderingContextHost::RecordCanvasSizeToUMA.

histogram.name: Blink.OffscreenCanvas.TransferControlToOffscreen
histogram.summary: Records a call to transfer a canvas offscreen. True indicates a successful transfer, false for a failure.

histogram.name: Blink.OffscreenCanvas.Transferred
histogram.summary: Records when an offscreen canvas has been transferred from the main thread to a worker.

histogram.name: Blink.Paint.UpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating paint in the Blink document lifecycle.
Note: As of M70, this histogram has stopped recording metrics on machines with low-resolution clocks.

histogram.name: Blink.PaintInvalidation.UpdateTime
histogram.obsolete: SlimmingPaintInvalidation is enabled by default, so this histogram is no longer being logged.
histogram.summary: Time spent updating paint invalidation in the Blink document lifecycle. Not available when SlimmingPaintInvalidation or SlimmingPaintV2 is enabled.

histogram.name: Blink.PrePaint.UpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating paint properties and paint invalidation in the Blink document lifecycle. Available when SlimmingPaintInvalidation or SlimmingPaintV2 is enabled.
Note: As of M70, this histogram has stopped recording metrics on machines with low-resolution clocks.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceFetcher.StaleWhileRevalidate
histogram.summary: Count of resources attempted Stale Revalidation.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceFetcher.StaleWhileRevalidateDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of completed stale revalidation attempts.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceLoadScheduler.DecodedBytes
histogram.summary: Count received data size in bytes for each resource.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceLoadScheduler.DecodedBytes.KBPerFrameStatus
histogram.summary:
Total decoded size of resources associated with frames of a particular type. Recorded when a fetch has been completed.
Each bucket of the histogram cointains the total size of all requests associated with frames with a particular frame status in kilobytes.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceLoadScheduler.PeakRequests
histogram.summary: The largest number of outstanding resource requests issued by a frame until the network 2-quiet (no more than 2 active network requests for 1 seconds).

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceLoadScheduler.RequestCount
histogram.summary: Count resource request circumstance, e.g. from the main frame vs sub-frames, or in throttled state vs in not-throttled state, on each resource load completion.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceLoadScheduler.ThrottlingStateChangeCount
histogram.summary: Count how many times the scheduler has changed throttling status from the frame creation until network activity quiets.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceLoadScheduler.TotalDecodedBytes
histogram.summary: Total received data size in bytes to load resources from the frame creation until network activity quiets.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceLoadScheduler.TotalRequestCount
histogram.summary: Total number of resource requests completed from the frame creation until network activity quiets.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceLoadScheduler.TotalTrafficBytes
histogram.summary: Total traffic data in bytes transferred over networks to load resources from the frame creation until network activity quiets.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceLoadScheduler.TrafficBytes
histogram.summary: Count traffic data size in bytes transferred over networks to load each resource.

histogram.name: Blink.ResourceLoadScheduler.TrafficBytes.KBPerFrameStatus
histogram.summary:
Total encoded size of resources associated with frames of a particular type. Recorded when a fetch has been completed.
Each bucket of the histogram cointains the total size of all requests associated with frames with a particular frame status in kilobytes.

histogram.name: Blink.RestoredCachedStyleSheet
histogram.summary: On each link stylesheet tag resolve, record whether a Blink MemoryCached StyleSheetContents was reused.

histogram.name: Blink.RestoredCachedStyleSheet2
histogram.summary: On each link stylesheet tag resolve, record which cache Blink hit.

histogram.name: Blink.ScanPendingActivityDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of time taken to scan pending activities of all V8 wrappers in a worker thread.

histogram.name: Blink.Script.SchedulingType
histogram.summary: Number of script elements for each scheduling type, recorded for each successful #prepare-a-script.

histogram.name: Blink.ScriptValueSerializer.DOMWrapperCount
histogram.obsolete: Used to justify distribution of serialized data in the wild, but unlikely to be useful to track long-term.
histogram.summary: Number of DOM wrappers serialized as part of an object passed to postMessage, IndexedDB, or another API that serializes script values.

histogram.name: Blink.ScriptValueSerializer.JSObjectCount
histogram.obsolete: Used to justify distribution of serialized data in the wild, but unlikely to be useful to track long-term.
histogram.summary: Number of JavaScript objects (other than DOM wrappers) serialized as part of an object passed to postMessage, IndexedDB, or another API that serializes script values.

histogram.name: Blink.ScriptValueSerializer.PrimitiveCount
histogram.obsolete: Used to justify distribution of serialized data in the wild, but unlikely to be useful to track long-term.
histogram.summary: Number of primitive values (numbers, strings, etc.) serialized as part of an object passed to postMessage, IndexedDB, or another API that serializes script values.

histogram.name: Blink.ScrollingCoordinator.UpdateTime
histogram.summary:
The time it took to update scrolling coordinator data (scroll gesture regions, touch event rects, and main thread scrolling reasons). These values are calculated during the main-thread blink lifecycle.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.SharedBuffer.FailedLock
histogram.summary: Number of failed SharedBuffer::lock() calls for each resource type. https://crbug.com/603791

histogram.name: Blink.SharedBuffer.SuccessfulLock
histogram.summary: Number of successful SharedBuffer::lock() calls for each resource type. https://crbug.com/603791

histogram.name: Blink.SharedBuffer.Unlock
histogram.summary: Number of SharedBuffer::unlock() calls for each resource type. https://crbug.com/603791

histogram.name: Blink.StyleAndLayout.UpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating style and layout in the Blink document lifecycle.
Note: As of M70, this histogram has stopped recording metrics on machines with low-resolution clocks.

histogram.name: Blink.UseCounter.AnimatedCSSProperties
histogram.summary: Records usage of animated CSS properties used on a page, either statically or dynamically, from the time the page is initialised to when it is closed or navigated away from. Each property is counted at most once per page per view via PageLoadMetricsObserver.
histogram.details:
This histogram counts CSS properties only when they are animated by a CSS Animation. Refer to Blink.UseCounter.CSSProperties for more details.
As of M69, Blink.UseCounter.AnimatedCSSProperties is moved from the blink side to the browser side.

histogram.name: Blink.UseCounter.AnimatedCSSProperties_TestBrowserProcessLogging
histogram.obsolete: Renamed to Blink.UseCounter.AnimatedCSSProperties in 03/2018, M69. The old blink UseCounter is flawed in OOPIF, so moved to the browser side instead.
histogram.summary: Records usage of animated CSS properties used across all frames in a page, either statically or dynamically, from the time the page is initialised to when it is closed or navigated away from. Each property is counted at most once per page view except kTotalPagesMeasuredCSSSampleId which represents a page load (enum value of 1) and is counted exactly once per page view.

histogram.name: Blink.UseCounter.CSSProperties
histogram.summary: Records usage of CSS properties used on a page, either statically or dynamically, from the time the page is initialised to when it is closed or navigated away from. Each property is counted at most once per page per view via PageLoadMetricsObserver.
histogram.details:
