histogram.summary: Tracks the Feed storage success rate during commit operations.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.OperationCommitTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the Feed to commit operations from the storage.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.RequestSizeKB.Compressed
histogram.summary: Android: the size in kb of gzip-compressed requests sent by the Feed's networking host. Recorded when a request is sent.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.ResponseSizeKB
histogram.summary: Android: the uncompressed size in KB of responses received by the Feed's networking host. Recorded when a successful response is received.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Scheduler.RefreshTrigger
histogram.summary: The scheduler watches for various triggers, which cause it to decide if a refresh is currently warranted or not. This histogram is emitted when a trigger causes a refresh.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Scheduler.RequestBehavior
histogram.summary: When NTP is opened, the scheduler host tells the Feed library how to act, if the existing content should be shown, immediately or with timeout, and if a refresh request should be started.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.TokenFetchStatus
histogram.summary: Android: failure reason when attempting to fetch an OAuth token for the feed. Recorded when a token fetch completes.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.FetchPendingPlaceholder.VisibleDuration
histogram.summary: Android: How long the content suggestion placeholder is shown. This is tracked based on when the placeholder is enabled in the UI, not how long it is actually visible on screen. Depending on the screen size, the used value could be bigger.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.FetchPendingSpinner.VisibleDuration
histogram.summary: Android: How long the content suggestion loading spinner is shown. This is tracked based on when the spinner is enabled in the UI, not how long it is actually visible on screen. Depending on the screen size, the used value could be bigger.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.CancelSystemTouches
histogram.summary: Logs true when the system touches are cancelled in order to show the Context Menu instead of performing a default system action. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.DelayedElementDetails
histogram.summary: Logged when DOM element details are not yet available when the user completes a long press. Also logs if the context menu was shown once the element details became available or if the display was cancelled. This ensures that all context menu gestures are eventually processed. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.DOMElementFetchDuration
histogram.summary: How long it takes to fetch the details of the DOM element the user is selecting for the context menu. On iOS, JavaScript must be run in order to get the details of the DOM element. If the element is not inside the main frame, latency could be added due to the communication between frames. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.DOMElementFrame
histogram.summary: Logged when the Context Menu will be displayed. Also logs if the element for the last touch was found in the main frame or a iframe. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.iOS.CopyImage
histogram.summary: Logged when the copying image from context menu is invoked and relevant events happen during the copying image procedure.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.iOS.GetImageDataByJsResult
histogram.summary: Result of getting image data by JavaScript for CopyImage, SaveImage and SearchByImage from iOS context menu. Recorded when the JavaScript returns, or fails for timeout.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.SaveLinkType
histogram.summary: Type of the url when user saves the content through the save link context menu.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.SelectedOption
histogram.summary: The option that the user selected from a context menu.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.Shown
histogram.summary: Recorded when a context menu is shown, sliced by whether a web contents was present. (It generally should be; indeed, this code path should not be followed when it is absent. For instance, invoking a context menu from the new tab page or from the sad tab page will not cause an emission to this histogram.) Note that this refers to link context menus, not the cut-copy-paste context menus that can appear during long presses on text on a web page or long presses in the omnibox. The value of this histogram for "present" could be usefully compared with the total count of ContextMenu.SelectedOption to determine the clickthrough rate for context menus.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.UnexpectedFindElementResultHandlerMessage
histogram.summary: Logged when an unexpected FindElementResultHandler message was received by the application. An unexpected message may be sent by a malicious frame. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.WaitingForElementDetails
histogram.summary: Logs true when the context menu gesture recognizer fully recognizes a long press, but is still waiting on the DOM element details before the context menu can be shown. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.EnabledState
histogram.summary: Android: Whether contextual suggestions are enabled. Recorded when the enabled state changes. Note that this may be recorded multiple times per session.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.Events
histogram.summary: Records each event handled by the Contextual Suggestions backend. Recorded when the event is sent to the backend, typically when a UI or network action is registered. Implemented for Android.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchErrorCode
histogram.summary: Android: The net::Error status code of contextual suggestion fetches. Recorded when a fetch completes.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchLatencyMilliseconds
histogram.summary: Android: the amount of time taken elapsed between a contextual suggestion fetch starting and ending. Recorded when a fetch is completed.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchRequestProtoSizeKB
histogram.summary: Android: The size of the serialized and base64-encoded proto sent to the server when fetching contextual suggestions. Recorded when a fetch is initiated.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchResponseCode
histogram.summary: Android: The http status code of contextual suggestion fetches. Recorded when a fetch completes.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchResponseNetworkBytes
histogram.summary: Android: The number of bytes in the response received when a contextual suggestions fetch completes. Recorded when the response is received.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchResponseSizeKB
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2018-06-01 in favor of a histogram that measures bytes received over the network directly instead of the uncompressed KB.
histogram.summary: Android: The size of the response body received when a contextual suggestions fetch completes. Recorded when the response is received.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.PageViewClickLength
histogram.summary: Android: The length of a visit on a page. Reported for HTTP and HTTPS pages provided there was something visible printed to the screen. Selecting a tab or loading a new URL in current tab starts timing a visit. Switching to a different tab, closing the tab or loading a new URL finishes timing and reports the time if the page did a visually non-empty paint or finished loading. Bucketed by short/medium/long.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.PageViewTime
histogram.summary: Android: The length of a visit on a page. Reported for HTTP and HTTPS pages provided there was something visible printed to the screen. Selecting a tab or loading a new URL in current tab starts timing a visit. Switching to a different tab, closing the tab or loading a new URL finishes timing and reports the time if the page did a visually non-empty paint or finished loading.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.Preference.State
histogram.summary: Android: Whether the user preference for contextual suggestions is enabled. Recorded on cold start and when the user has changed the preference state. Note that this may be recorded multiple times per session.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.ResultsReturnedInOverviewMode
histogram.summary: Android: Whether the contextual suggestions results are returned to the UI-layer while the user is in overview mode.

histogram.name: Cookie.AgeFor
histogram.summary: Records the age (in days) of the oldest cookie delivered along with a request. Recorded when setting the `Cookie` header for a given request.

histogram.name: Cookie.AllAgesFor
histogram.summary: Records the age (in days) of all cookies delivered along with a request. Recorded when setting the `Cookie` header for a given request.

histogram.name: Cookie.BackingStoreUpdateResults
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Whether or not updates to the backing store succeeded or failed, recorded every update.

histogram.name: Cookie.BetweenAccessIntervalMinutes
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2015-08-17 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/521135).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Intervals between access time updates for each cookie.

histogram.name: Cookie.CommitProblem
histogram.summary: Recorded when a problem occurs trying to commit changes to the cookie store back to disk, in the SQLite store.

histogram.name: Cookie.CookieDeleteEquivalent
histogram.summary: Record when at attempt is made to delete an equivalent cookie on a set cookie operation and when such a cookie is found and deleted. Also measure when strict secure cookie rules affect this outcome and prevent a cookie from being deleted.

histogram.name: Cookie.CookieLineCookieValueValidity
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2017-07-14 as it demonstrated that making cookie parsing rules stricter was not feasible. See https://crbug.com/638117.
histogram.summary: Indicates whether a cookie initialised from a Set-Cookie header value was valid or not. This is only logged after cookies containing control characters have already been rejected, so "Invalid" here means that the cookie contains quotes, commas, backslashes or top-bit-set characters.

histogram.name: Cookie.CookiePrefix
histogram.summary: Number of times a cookie was set with a name prefixed by "__Secure-" or "__Host-" (prefixes reserved by https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-west-cookie-prefixes).

histogram.name: Cookie.CookiePrefixBlocked
histogram.summary: Number of times a cookie was set with a name prefixed by "__Secure-" or "__Host-" that violate cookie prefix rules. That is, a __Secure- cookie must be set over a secure connection with the Secure attribute, and a __Host- cookie must be set over a secure connection, with the Secure attribute, with no Domain attribute, and with a Path attribute of "/". (As defined in https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-west-cookie-prefixes.)

histogram.name: Cookie.CookieSourceScheme
histogram.summary: For each cookie added to the store, record whether its source URL has a cryptographic scheme, broken down by Secure and not Secure.

histogram.name: Cookie.CorruptMetaTable
histogram.summary: Records the detection of a corrupted meta table. See http://crbug.com/111376 .

histogram.name: Cookie.CorruptMetaTableRecoveryFailed
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records a failure to recover a corrupted meta table. See http://crbug.com/111376 .

histogram.name: Cookie.Count
histogram.summary: Number of cookies in the store (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time)

histogram.name: Cookie.DBSizeInKB
histogram.summary: The size, on disk, of the cookie database as it is being loaded.

histogram.name: Cookie.DeletionCause
histogram.summary: For each cookie removed from the store, the reason it was removed.

histogram.name: Cookie.DomainCount
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2015-08-17 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/521135).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For each domain, number of cookies in that domain (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time).

histogram.name: Cookie.DomainPerEtldp1Count
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2015-08-17 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/521135).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For every top level domain, number of subdomains in that top level domain (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time).

histogram.name: Cookie.Etldp1Count
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2015-08-17 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/521135).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For every top level domain, number of cookies in that domain (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time).

histogram.name: Cookie.EvictedLastAccessMinutes
histogram.summary: For each evicted (not expired) cookie, the amount of time since it was last used

histogram.name: Cookie.ExpirationDurationMinutes
histogram.summary: Number of minutes until cookie expires when set.

histogram.name: Cookie.GetCookieListCompletionTime
histogram.summary: The length of time between when the network stack receives a request for a cookie list and when it actually responds to the request or abandons it (for e.g. a closed renderer).

histogram.name: Cookie.HeaderLength
histogram.summary: The size of each outgoing request's 'Cookie' header field's value, in bytes.

histogram.name: Cookie.KillDatabaseResult
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Whether killing the database because it was corrupted beyond repair succeeded.

histogram.name: Cookie.LoadProblem
histogram.summary: Recorded when a problem is recorded when loading the persistent cookie store.

histogram.name: Cookie.NetworkSecurity
histogram.summary: Degree of protection against cookie theft. Recorded for each cookie when setting the `Cookie` header for a request.

histogram.name: Cookie.NumberOfLoadedCookies
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: This histogram records the total number of cookies loaded from disk, including any cookies that are discarded during loading (for whatever reason).

histogram.name: Cookie.ParsedCookieStatus
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 9/2013. Experiment to measure control characters in cookies is finished.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: When parsing a cookie, indicates if control characters were present in any of the cookie values and if any of the cookie values were invalid. Specifically, checks that all of the parsed values are valid according to the valid token definition in Section 2.2 of RFC2616 which specifies a token must have no separators (i.e. no characters from the following string, ignoring the starting and ending single quote: '()<>@,;:\"/[]?={} \t') and no control characters.

histogram.name: Cookie.PriorityBlockingTime
histogram.summary: This histogram records the total duration of time during which at least one web request was blocked waiting for the cookie store to load. If no requests were affected, the value is 0. If two requests are simultaneously blocked for 1 second, the value is 1 second. If two requests are consecutively blocked for 1 second each, the value is two seconds.

histogram.name: Cookie.PriorityLoadCount
histogram.summary: The number of distinct Effective Top-Level Domains Plus One (ETLD+1, i.e., google.com, bbc.co.uk) for which a priority cookie load occurred.

histogram.name: Cookie.ReinstatedCookies
histogram.summary: The duration in seconds between a cookie getting evicted (due to the number of cookies exceeding a domain limit), and subsequently reinstated.

histogram.name: Cookie.SetAttributePairCharsValidity
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 9/2013. Experiment to measure control characters in cookies is finished.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Indicates whether a cookie attribute pair was set with both a valid key and a valid attribute value or not. For the key, this implies that it was a valid token as defined in Section 2.2 of RFC2616 which specifies a token must have no separators (i.e. no characters from the following string, ignoring the starting and ending single quote: '()<>@,;:\"/[]?={} \t') and no control characters. For the value, this implies that it contained no control characters and no semicolon.

histogram.name: Cookie.SetNameValidity
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 9/2013. Experiment to measure control characters in cookies is finished.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Indicates whether a cookie name was set with a valid token. A valid token is defined in Section 2.2 of RFC2616 which specifies a token must have no separators (i.e. no characters from the following string, ignoring the starting and ending single quote: '()<>@,;:\"/[]?={} \t') and no control characters.

histogram.name: Cookie.SetValueCookieValueValidity
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 9/2013. Experiment to measure control characters in cookies is finished.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Indicates whether a cookie value was valid or invalid when there was an attempt to set it, where a valid value is defined in RFC 6265 as ASCII characters excluding controls, whitspace, comma, semicolon, and backslash.

histogram.name: Cookie.Shutdown.NumberOfCookiesDeleted
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 9/2015. The associated experiment was flawed.
histogram.summary: The number of session cookies deleted on shutdown. This metric is emitted even if no cookies are deleted.

histogram.name: Cookie.Shutdown.TimeSpentDeletingCookies
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 9/2015. The associated experiment was flawed.
histogram.summary: The amount of time required to delete session cookies on shutdown. This metric is emitted even if no cookies are deleted.

histogram.name: Cookie.Startup.NumberOfCookiesDeleted
histogram.summary: The number of session cookies deleted on startup. This metric is emitted even if no cookies are deleted. This metric is not emitted if session cookies are restored, rather than deleted on startup.

histogram.name: Cookie.Startup.TimeSpentDeletingCookies
histogram.summary: The amount of time required to delete session cookies on startup. This metric is emitted even if no cookies are deleted. This metric is not emitted if session cookies are restored, rather than deleted on startup.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeBlockedOnLoad
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) between the cookie store load starting and completing.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV10
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v9 cookie database to v10.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV5
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 04/2015. The migration has finished for most users.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v4 database to v5.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV6
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 04/2015. The migration has finished for most users.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v5 database to v6.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV7
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 04/2015. The migration has finished for most users.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v6 database to v7.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV8
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 04/2015. The migration has finished for most users.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v7 database to v8.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV9
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v8 database to v9.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeGet
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to get cookies for each URL request.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeInitializeDB
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to initialize the cookies database.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeInitializeDomainMap
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to read and parse the domain map from the cookies database.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeKeyLoadDBQueueWait
histogram.summary: This histogram records the wall-clock delay between a priority load task being posted to the DB-thread and its execution.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeKeyLoadTotalWait
histogram.summary: This histogram records the the total amount of time spent fetching the cookies associated with an eTLD+1 from disk, including time spent waiting in task queues.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeLoad
histogram.summary: This histogram records the sum of the durations of all initial tasks loading cookies from the database.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeLoadDBQueueWait
histogram.summary: This histogram records the wall-clock delay between the Load task being posted to the DB-thread and its execution.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeLoadDomains
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to read the domain map from the cookies database.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeParseDomains
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to parse the domains already loaded from the cookies database and put them in the domain map.

histogram.name: Cookie.Type
histogram.summary: For each cookie added to the store, record it's type(s).

histogram.name: CopylessPaste.CacheHit
histogram.summary: The cache hit status of metadata extraction for AppIndexing.

histogram.name: CopylessPaste.ExtractionFailedUs
histogram.summary:
The time spent on scanning the document in the main frame to extract the metadata. The metadata would be used to feed Icing for CopylessPaste feature on Android. This only counts pages with failed or empty JSON extraction.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: CopylessPaste.ExtractionStatus
histogram.summary: The error status of metadata extraction for AppIndexing.

histogram.name: CopylessPaste.ExtractionUs
histogram.summary:
The time spent on scanning the document in the main frame to extract the metadata. The metadata would be used to feed Icing for CopylessPaste feature on Android. This only counts pages with successful JSON extraction.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Cras.HighestInputHardwareLevel
histogram.summary: The highest hardware level of input device in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). The hardware level is the remaining frames in the device. For input device, it can show the latency between capturing and actual reading.

histogram.name: Cras.HighestOutputHardwareLevel
histogram.summary: The highest hardware level of output device in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). The hardware level is the remaining frames in the device. For output device, it can show the latency between writing and actual playout.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamCallbackThreshold
histogram.summary: The callback threshold of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). It is the number of frames when to request more from the client. This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamFlags
histogram.summary: The flags of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). It is the special handling for stream. This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamSamplingFormat
histogram.summary: The sampling format of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamSamplingRate
histogram.summary: The sampling rate of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamTimeoutMilliSeconds
histogram.summary: The longest additional time CRAS(Chrome OS audio server) ever waits for a stream exceeding the timeout threshold. This value is recorded per stream when it gets removed and used to investigate the audio glitch/skip problem on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: Cras.UnderrunsPerDevice
histogram.summary: The number of underruns happened on one audio device. Underrun here means there are no samples in device buffer while device is playing. It is recorded when device is closed by CRAS(Chrome OS audio server).

histogram.name: CrashExitCodes.Extension
histogram.summary:
The exit codes for crashed extension processes. Note: Due to https://crbug.com/805754, the enum labels are off by 1 for M56 and earlier.
See also Stability.CrashedProcessAge.Extension that is usually logged at the same time.

histogram.name: CrashExitCodes.Renderer
histogram.summary:
The exit codes for crashed renderer processes. Note: Due to https://crbug.com/805754, the enum labels are off by 1 for M56 and earlier.
See also Stability.CrashedProcessAge.Renderer that is usually logged at the same time.

histogram.name: Crashpad.CrashReportPending
histogram.summary: The reason that a crash report entered the upload pending state.

histogram.name: Crashpad.CrashReportSize
histogram.summary: The size of a crash report minidump on disk when captured.

histogram.name: Crashpad.CrashUpload.AttemptSuccessful
histogram.summary: Logs whether a crash uploaded succeeded or failed.

histogram.name: Crashpad.CrashUpload.Skipped
histogram.summary: The reason that a crash report upload was skipped.

histogram.name: Crashpad.ExceptionCaptureResult
histogram.summary: The outcome of execution of the Crashpad exception handler.

histogram.name: Crashpad.ExceptionCode.Mac
histogram.summary: The exception code encountered in a crash on Mac OS X.

histogram.name: Crashpad.ExceptionCode.Win
histogram.summary: The exception code encountered in a crash on Windows.

histogram.name: Crashpad.ExceptionEncountered
histogram.summary: A count of the number of exceptions handled by Crashpad with two buckets for starting and finishing.

histogram.name: Crashpad.HandlerCrash.ExceptionCode.Mac
histogram.summary: The exception code encountered for a crash of the crash handler process on Mac OS X.

histogram.name: Crashpad.HandlerCrash.ExceptionCode.Win
histogram.summary: The exception code encountered for a crash of the crash handler process on Windows.

histogram.name: Crashpad.HandlerLifetimeMilestone
histogram.summary: Handler start/crash/exit events.

histogram.name: CrashReport.BreakpadCrashDumpOutcome
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 04/2016 as CrashPad does not implement this.
histogram.summary: Reports the result of an attempt to report a crash dump via Breakpad. Success indicates only that the out-of-process crash reporter succesfully generated a dump. The success or failure of the eventual upload is not measured. This stability metric is logged from the first Chrome launch post-dump.

histogram.name: CrashReport.BreakpadDumpWithoutCrashOutcome
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 04/2016 as CrashPad does not implement this.
histogram.summary: Reports the result of an attempt to report a dump via Breakpad without crashing. Success indicates only that the out-of-process crash reporter succesfully generated a dump. The success or failure of the eventual upload is not measured. This stability metric is logged from the first Chrome launch post-dump.

histogram.name: CrashReport.CrashBackgroundUploadDelay
histogram.summary: Time delta between when the crash took place and when the crash was uploaded by the app as a background task. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: CrashReport.PendingReportsOnBackgroundWakeUp
histogram.summary: The number of crash reports present when the app wakes up as a background task to upload crash reports. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: CrashReport.PermanentUploadFailure
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 01/2017 in Issue 680162 with the removal of Kasko.
histogram.summary: Counts crash reports that the Kasko crash reporter fails to upload and hands off to Chrome. Kasko stores the failed reports in a location specified by Chrome. Chrome records this metric when it detects new minidump files in that location. Currently the reports are discarded, but they could eventually be uploaded via the Chrome network stack.

histogram.name: Cros.ClickOnShelf
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 12/2013. Default pinned apps trial is finished.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS shelf clicks.

histogram.name: CrosDisks.ArchiveType
histogram.summary: The type of archive file that Chrome OS cros-disks daemon is requested to mount.

histogram.name: CrosDisks.DeviceMediaType
histogram.summary: The media type of removable device that Chrome OS cros-disks daemon is requested to mount.

histogram.name: CrosDisks.FilesystemType
histogram.summary: The type of file system that Chrome OS cros-disks daemon is requested to mount.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.MountCompletedError
histogram.summary: The error code of disk mount signals received from the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.MountErrorMountType
histogram.summary: The {mount type}x{mount error code} received from the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.MountTime
histogram.summary: Time taken by the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon to perform a Mount operation.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.UnmountError
histogram.summary: The error code of disk unmount operations returned from the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.UnmountTime
histogram.summary: Time taken by the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon to perform an Unmount operation.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.DialogShown
histogram.summary: Records the number of times when first-run dialog was shown.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.FurthestStep
histogram.summary: Index of furthest step that was reached during tutorial. Since order of steps could change eventially and new steps could apear we use index here instead of step name.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.TimeSpent
histogram.summary: The total time that user spent on first-run tutorial.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.TimeSpentOnStep
histogram.summary: The time that user spent on some step of tutorial.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.TutorialCompletion
histogram.summary: Tracks the way how user left tutorial.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.TutorialLaunched
histogram.summary: Records the number of times when first-run tutorial has been launched.

histogram.name: Crostini.AppLaunch
histogram.summary: Recorded each time a Crostini app is launched, recording whether the app is the built in terminal, a registered app, or an unknown app.

histogram.name: Crostini.AppsInstalledAtLogin
histogram.summary: Records the number of Crostini apps that surface in the launcher at login time (not including the Terminal). This only logs if Crostini is enabled for the current user.

histogram.name: Crostini.Crosvm.CpuPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of the system CPU that crosvm processes used during the 10-minute measuring period. This is recorded once every 10 minutes when crosvm is running.

histogram.name: Crostini.Crosvm.Processes.Count
histogram.summary: The number of crosvm processes that are running at the end of the 10-minute interval. This is recorded once every 10 minutes when crosvm is running.

histogram.name: Crostini.Crosvm.RssPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of the system memory that crosvm processes used at the end of the 10-minute measuring period. This is recorded once every 10 minutes when crosvm is running.

histogram.name: Crostini.SetupResult
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the user completes the Crostini setup UI, recording the result of the setup.

histogram.name: Crostini.SetupSource
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the user initiates the Crostini setup UI, recording the UI surface that invoked the setup.

histogram.name: Crostini.TimeFromDeviceSetupToInstall
histogram.summary: The time from a user setting up their device, to the user enabling Crostini.

histogram.name: Crostini.UninstallResult
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the user completes the Crostini uninstall UI, recording the result of the uninstall.

histogram.name: Crostini.UninstallSource
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the user initiates the Crostini uninstall UI, recording the UI surface that initiated the uninstall.

histogram.name: Crostini.UpgradeSource
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the user sees the Crostini upgrade UI, recording the UI surface that initiated the upgrade.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncSoftwareFeaturesResult
histogram.summary: Indicates that there was no issue retrieving supported and enabled software features, or that there were enabled features which were not in the supported feature set. See the histogram "CryptAuth.DeviceSyncSoftwareFeaturesResult.Failures" for a breakdown of failures by feature type.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncSoftwareFeaturesResult.Failures
histogram.summary: Breaks down by feature type "CryptAuth.DeviceSyncSoftwareFeaturesResult"'s count of enabled features which were not in the supported feature set.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.Enrollment.Result
histogram.summary: The top-level result of the CryptAuth device enrollment process.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.ChecksumStatus
histogram.summary: Checksum status of critical persistent data used by Cryptohome. This status is recorded every time the cryptohomed daemon reads a file.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DeletedUserProfiles
histogram.summary: In low disk space scenarios, Cryptohome erases whole user profiles. This is a number of user profiles deleted during cleanup. Only reported when non-zero.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationEndStatus
histogram.summary: The status of the user home directory migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once at the end of each migration.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationFailedErrorCode
histogram.summary: The error code of file operation by which the user home directory migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto has failed. This is logged once the migration encounters a file I/O error.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationFailedOperationType
histogram.summary: The type of file operation at which the user home directory migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto has failed. This is logged once the migration encounters a file I/O error.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationFailedPathType
histogram.summary: The category of the path where the user home directory migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto has failed. This is logged once the migration encounters a file I/O error.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationInitialFreeSpaceInMb
histogram.summary: The total amount of free disk space on the system when the user started the migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is only logged when the user encounters an insufficient space error.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationNoSpaceFailureFreeSpaceInMb
histogram.summary: The total amount of free disk space on the system when the user received an insufficient space error during the migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is only logged when the user encounters an insufficient space error.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationNoSpaceXattrSizeInBytes
histogram.summary: The total number of bytes of xattr data that we are trying to store on a file when we receive an insufficient space error. This includes both xattr data already present on the file as well as the new xattr we are trying to set. This is only logged during ecryptfs to ext4-crypto migration if we receive an insufficient space error when setting a new extended attribute.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationStartStatus
histogram.summary: The status of the user home directory migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once at the start of each migration.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationTotalByteCountInMb
histogram.summary: The total byte count (MB) of the data in a user's home directory which is being migrated from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once when a new migration starts.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationTotalFileCount
histogram.summary: The total file count in a user's home directory which is being migrated from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once when a new migration starts.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMinimalMigrationEndStatus
histogram.summary: The status of the user home directory minimal migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once at the end of each minimal migration.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMinimalMigrationStartStatus
histogram.summary: The status of the user home directory minimal migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once at the start of each minimal migration.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DiskCleanupProgress
histogram.summary: In low disk space scenarios, Cryptohome erases caches while target amount of free disk space is not reached. It starts from low priority categories. This histogram reports which topmost priority was reached to fulfill a cleanup request.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.Errors
histogram.summary: Cryptohome errors.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.FreedGCacheDiskSpaceInMb
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 05/2018 in favor of Cryptohome.GCache.FreedDiskSpaceInMb. Reports zero in 99% of hits.
histogram.summary: The amount of drive cache (MB) which was evicted by cryptohome due to shortage of remaining disk space.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.FreeDiskSpaceTotalTime
histogram.summary: In low disk space scenarios, Cryptohome performs a lot of disk space operations to erase data. This is a number of milliseconds taken to perform a cleanup. Reported only if longer than 5 ms.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.GCache.FreedDiskSpaceInMb
histogram.summary: This is an amount of Google Drive cache (MB) which was evicted by cryptohome due to shortage of remaining disk space. Reported only if something was deleted (greater than zero).

histogram.name: Cryptohome.HomedirEncryptionType
histogram.summary: The encryption type used for a user's cryptohome directory. This is logged each time the cryptohome is mounted.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.LECredential
histogram.summary: Events related to Low Entropy (LE) credential management. These are logged every time an operation involving a LE credential is performed.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationToGaiaId
histogram.summary: This is the status of cryptohome migration to GaiaId. Every time user logs in current migration status is recorded.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.ConsumedBatteryPercent
histogram.summary: The amount of consumed battery level during cryptohome encryption migration. This is logged when the battery level decreases during migration, and the data is used to check if the minimum battery level required to start migration is appropriate.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.FirstScreen
histogram.summary: The first screen in the encryption migration UI, which is shown when a user attempts to log in to the system and old encryption (eCryptfs) is detected.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.MigrationResult
histogram.summary: The result of encryption migration from eCryptfs to Ext4 dircrypto. The recorded result is what the migration UI in Chrome side is notified from cryptohomed.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.RemoveCryptohomeResult
histogram.summary: The result of the removal of user's cryptohome. When the migration UI is notified that the migration failed, the UI tries to remove the user's cryptohome to make sure that the user can create clean crytohome directory in the next sign-in.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.UserChoice
histogram.summary: User's choice when the system is ready to migrate encryption. The user can start migration or skip it. It is used to know how many users have skipped migration.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.VisibleScreen
histogram.summary: How many times each screen in migration UI is shown to the user. A screen is recorded as a visible screen when the screen is kept displayed at least for a second.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToCompleteDircryptoMigration
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for the user's home directory to be migrated from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. If the migration is interrupted and subsequently resumed (such as due to power loss) then no value is recorded for either attempt.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToCompleteDircryptoMinimalMigration
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for the user's home directory to be migrated from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto if the minimal migration method was used. If the minimal migration is interrupted and subsequently resumed (such as due to power loss) then no value is recorded for either attempt.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToInitPkcs11
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome daemon to initialize the PKCS#11. Initializations under 1 second represent checks on a previously initialized PKCS#11, and should be discarded for analysis.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToMountAsync
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome to mount the encrypted home directory asynchronously.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToMountGuestAsync
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome to mount the encrypted guest home directory asynchronously.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToMountGuestSync
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome to mount the encrypted guest home directory synchronously.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToMountSync
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome to mount the encrypted home directory synchronously.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToTakeTpmOwnership
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome daemon to take ownership of the TPM.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TpmResults
histogram.summary: The errors resulting from interacting with the Trusted Platform Module (TPM) device.

histogram.name: CustomTab.SessionDuration
histogram.summary: Records the time duration in CustomTab Activity from onStart/onResume to onStop/onPause, if that activity is opened by Webapp/WebApk/Trusted Web Activity.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ClientAppId
histogram.summary: Android: AppId declared by the launching application in EXTRA_APPLICATION_ID in a custom tab. This histogram is used as a means to count the total instances of custom tabs created meanwhile binning them across launching apps that have identified themselves.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ConnectionStatusOnReturn.GSA
histogram.summary: Android: Recorded whenever user closes the custom tab by hitting the close button or back to return to the caller app. Keeps track of the connection status and whether the app was using a KeepAlive connection. Recorded only for AGSA.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ConnectionStatusOnReturn.NonGSA
histogram.summary: Android: Recorded whenever user closes the custom tab by hitting the close button or back to return to the caller app. Keeps track of the connection status and whether the app was using a KeepAlive connection. Recorded for all apps other than AGSA.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DetachedResourceRequest.Duration
histogram.summary: Time between the start of a detached resource request and its completion (failure or success). Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DetachedResourceRequest.FinalStatus
histogram.summary: Reports the final status of the detached request, including success. Recorded once per detached resource request. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DetachedResourceRequest.RedirectsCount
histogram.summary: Number of redirects followed by a detached resource request until success or failure. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.CreateActivityDelegateTime
histogram.summary: Time to create an activity delegate for a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.CreatePackageContextTime
histogram.summary: Time to create the package context for a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.DestructionReason
histogram.summary: Possible reasons for destroying a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.EntryPointInitTime
histogram.summary: Time to initialize the entry point class for a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.EntryPointLoadClassTime
histogram.summary: Time to load the entry point class for a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.EntryPointNewInstanceTime
histogram.summary: Time to instantiate the entry point class for a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.LoadResult
histogram.summary: Possible results when loading a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.IntentToFirstCommitNavigationTime
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival in Chrome and the first navigation commit, if the navigation is successful. Similar in principle to Startup.FirstCommitNavigationTime.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.IntentToFirstCommitNavigationTime2
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival to a Custom Tab and the navigation start (note: not the navigation commit, as the name suggests). Recorded when the page has finished loading. Non-"Herb" mode.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.IntentToFirstCommitNavigationTime3
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival to a Custom Tab and the first navigation commit. Recorded for every custom tabs intent when the page has finished loading.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.IntentToFirstNavigationStartTime
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival to a Custom Tab and the navigation start. Recorded when the page has finished loading. Non-"Herb" mode.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.IntentToPageLoadedTime
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival in Chrome and the first "page loaded" event, if the navigation is successful. Non-"Herb" mode.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.MayLaunchUrlType
histogram.summary: Android only. Which flavors of mayLaunchUrl() were used before a tab launch. Can be low and/or high confidence. Recorded at the same time as CustomTabs.WarmupStateOnLaunch for the buckets "Session, No Warmup, Warmup called from another UID", "Session, No Warmup" and "Session, Warmup".

histogram.name: CustomTabs.NonDefaultSessionPrerenderMatched
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 4/2018 when prerender was removed from Custom Tabs.
histogram.summary: Android: When there is a prerender for a session with non-default parameters, whether the prerender was matched when navigating.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ParallelRequestStatusOnStart
histogram.summary: Recorded only on Android. Whether a parallel request was requested by the calling application, and if so its initial status. Recorded once per handled intent, when the request is initiated.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.PredictionStatus
histogram.summary: For Custom Tabs, records whether mayLaunchUrl() has been called, and if so, whether the call was later matched by a URL launch.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.PredictionToLaunch
histogram.summary: When a URL prediction succeeds, time in ms between the prediction and the actual launch.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.PrerenderSessionUsesDefaultParameters
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 4/2018 when prerender was removed from Custom Tabs.
histogram.summary: Android: When prerendering a URL for Custom Tabs, whether the client session uses the default parameters.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ResourcePrefetch.Duration
histogram.summary: Time between the start of a detached resource request for resource prefetch and its completion (failure or success). Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ResourcePrefetch.FinalStatus
histogram.summary: Reports the final status of the detached request for resource prefetch, including success. Recorded once per resource prefetch request. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.SpareWebContents.Status
histogram.summary: Android: When a spare WebContents is created from Custom Tabs, record creation, and whether it was used, killed or destroyed. Creation is recorded to get the "abandoned" case, that is when Chrome is killed before the renderer.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.SpareWebContents.TimeBeforeDeath
histogram.summary: Android: When a spare WebContents is created from Custom Tabs, record how long is took before the render process was killed.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.SpeculationStatusOnStart
histogram.summary: Recorded only for Android. How a speculation was started or why it was disallowed. This is recorded when a speculation of any kind is requested, with either a "Speculation allowed" value, or a reason why it was not allowed. If the speculation is allowed and is attempted to be started, the status on start will be counted in addition: one of "Prefetch", "Prerender", "Background Tab" or "Prerender not started". The last case is when a prerender is abandoned during start.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.SpeculationStatusOnSwap
histogram.summary: Recorded only for Android. Recorded when a speculation is requesting to swap into a visible tab.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.Visible
histogram.summary: Recorded only for Android. Records on every metrics upload whether the currently visible tab is a custom tab. The custom tab is launched by a client app with customized UI. Some metrics like page load time will behave differently for these tabs since it will be possible to warm up the browser process and prepare resources for specific urls ahead of time via a service connection. This histogram can be used to cluster these metric differently.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.WarmupStateOnLaunch
histogram.summary: Recorded only on Android. Reports whether warmup() has been called when a Custom Tabs Intent is received from an application, and if so, whether the call could be matched with a session.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.WebContentsStateOnLaunch
histogram.summary: Android: When a CustomTabActivity creates its Tab, whether we use a prerendered WebContents, a spare renderer or create a new WebContents.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.AutoLoFiAccuracy
histogram.summary: Counts the accuracy of estimated network quality when using Lo-Fi. Counters are incremented when a main frame URL request is handled by Data Reduction Proxy and session was in Auto Lo-Fi enabled field trial.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.AutoLoFiRequestHeaderState
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 8/2017, per server-directed preview decision.
histogram.summary: Counts the changes in the state of Lo-Fi request header (q=low) being added to the Chrome proxy request header. Counters are incremented when a main frame URL request is handled by Data Reduction Proxy and session was in Auto Lo-Fi enabled field trial.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BlockTypeFallback
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to block the fallback configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BlockTypePrimary
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to block the primary configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassedBytes
histogram.summary: Counts the response bytes that did not go through the data reduction proxy as the result of a bypass event.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassedBytes.NotBypassed
histogram.summary: Counts the response bytes that went through the data reduction proxy and were not bypassed.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassInfoFallback
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2014, replaced by DataReductionProxy.BypassTypeFallback.
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to bypass the fallback configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassInfoPrimary
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2014, replaced by DataReductionProxy.BypassTypePrimary.
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to bypass the primary configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassOnNetworkErrorFallback
histogram.summary: Positive net error code that caused the fallback data reduction proxy to be bypassed and put on the proxy retry list. Called after a failure to connect or resolve a host name.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassOnNetworkErrorPrimary
histogram.summary: Positive net error code that caused the primary data reduction proxy to be bypassed and put on the proxy retry list. Called after a failure to connect or resolve a host name.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassTypeFallback
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to bypass the fallback configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassTypePrimary
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to bypass the primary configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.CaptivePortalDetected.Platform
histogram.summary: Records if the platform detected that a captive portal is present on the current network. Recorded at the time of Chrome startup and on IP change event. Recorded only for users that have data saver enabled.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigFetchLostBytesCL
histogram.summary: Size of the response body. This is the actual number of bytes received, which usually agrees with but is not necessarily the same as the size specified by the Content-Length header. Only recorded if the request is sent while a simulated Data Reduction Proxy configuration fetch is taking place.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigFetchLostBytesDiff
histogram.summary: The difference between the size specified in the X-Original-Content-Length header and the size of the response body. Only recorded if the request is sent while a simulated Data Reduction Proxy configuration fetch is taking place. Only positive values are logged, so if X-Original-Content-Length is not specified or if it equals or exceeds the content length, it is not logged.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigFetchLostBytesOCL
histogram.summary: Size specified in the X-Original-Content-Length header. If this header is not present in the response, the size of the response body is used. Only recorded if the request is sent while a simulated Data Reduction Proxy configuration fetch is taking place.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.AuthExpired
histogram.summary:
Total number of authentication required responses received from the Data Reduction Proxy.
An "Unexpired" value is recorded every time a new client config is fetched and parsed successfully. An "Expired" value is recorded every time Chrome receives an authentication failure from the data saver proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.AuthExpiredSessionKey
histogram.summary:
On receiving an authentication failure from the data reduction proxy, records whether the session key used in the request matches the current session key.
Recorded every time Chrome receives an authentication failure from the data saver proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.AuthFailure.LatencyPenalty
histogram.summary:
Time from when the request starts to the time when the auth failure is received from the data saver proxy.
Recorded every time an auth failure is received from the data saver proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.FetchFailedAttemptsBeforeSuccess
histogram.summary:
The number of failed attempts when retrieving the configuration from the Data Reduction Proxy API before a successful attempt took place.
Recorded every time a new client config is fetched and parsed successfully. If the device has a valid connection type but no Internet connectivity, this count will keep increasing.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.FetchLatency
histogram.summary:
Time from when the Data Reduction Proxy configuration request starts until it completes.
Recorded every time a new client config is fetched and parsed successfully.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.FetchResponseCode
histogram.summary: Count per HttpResponseCode when attempting to retrieve the Data Reduction Proxy configuration from the Data Reduction Proxy API.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.HTTPRequests
histogram.summary:
Upper bound on the count of HTTP requests that did or did not go through the data saver proxy.
An HTTP request may not go through data saver proxy if the config was unavailable, due to local bypass rules, or due to server side bypasses. If data saver is turned on, this metric is recorded every time proxy is resolved for an HTTP request.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.MainFrames
histogram.summary:
Upper bound on the count of main frame requests that did or did not go through the data saver proxy.
A main frame request may not go through data saver proxy if the config was unavailable, due to local bypass rules, or due to server side bypasses. If data saver is turned on, this metric is recorded every time proxy is resolved for an HTTP request.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.PersistedConfigIsExpired
histogram.summary: Records whether the persisted client config for data saver proxies read from the disk at the time of Chrome startup was expired or not. Recorded once at the time of Chrome startup.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.SentVariationHeaders
histogram.summary: Records whether the client config client sent variation headers in the config request.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.DaysSinceEnabled
histogram.summary: Records the number of days since data reduction proxy was last enabled by the user. Recorded only if the data reduction proxy was last enabled since this metric was added to Chromium (M-56). Recorded at Chrome startup if data reduction proxy is already enabled, and when the user enables the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.DaysSinceSavingsCleared.NegativeSystemClock
histogram.summary: Records the number of days since the data reduction savings were cleared because the system clock moved back by more than 1 day. Recorded at Chrome startup if data reduction proxy is enabled, and if the data reduction proxy savings were cleared in some previous Chrome session.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.DeleteBrowsingHistory.NumBuckets
histogram.summary: When the data savings are cleared because the user deletes their browsing history, record how many buckets were deleted. See also DataReductionProxy.SavingsCleared.Reason/User action - cleared browsing history.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.EnabledState
histogram.summary:
Samples of user interactions with the Data Saver settings menu across all platforms (android, IOS, desktop extension). Data is collected whenever user toggles the ON/OFF switch in the settings menu for reducing data usage.
For desktop, Off-to-On/On-to-Off histogram count will increment when the data saver extension is installed/uninstalled respectively as well.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperDetectionHTTP
histogram.summary: For each carrier, the total number of HTTP responses that have been checked for tampering. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperDetectionHTTPS
histogram.summary: For each carrier, the total number of HTTPS responses that have been checked for tampering. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperDetectionPassHTTP
histogram.summary: For each carrier, the total number of HTTP responses that passed the tamper detection. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperDetectionPassHTTPS
histogram.summary: For each carrier, the total number of HTTPs responses that passed the tamper detection. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperedHTTP
histogram.summary: The total number of HTTP responses that some part (specified by suffix name) have been tampered with. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperedHTTPS
histogram.summary: The total number of HTTPS responses that some part (specified by suffix name) have been tampered with. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HistoricalDataUsageLoadTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to load historical data usage from Level DB into memory. This UMA is reported each time the method to load historical data usage is called, which happens when user views data usage history.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.InvalidResponseHeadersReceived.NetError
histogram.summary: Net error codes observed when invalid response headers were received from data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LastWeekAggregateKB.Services
histogram.summary: Records the aggregate data use of services for the last week. Different conditions are added as suffixes to this histogram. Recorded when Chrome is started, so this does not include the current session. The aggregation happens when requests complete. The current week calculation is based on week starting from the Epoch day(Thursday, January 1, 1970), and every 7 days is treated as a week.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LastWeekAggregateKB.UserTraffic.Downstream.ContentType
histogram.summary: Records the aggregate data use for the last week. This is the downstream data use of all user-initiated traffic. Recorded when Chrome is started, so this does not include the current session. The aggregation happens when requests complete. The current week calculation is based on week starting from the Epoch day(Thursday, January 1, 1970), and every 7 days is treated as a week.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LevelDBOpenStatus
histogram.summary: Status of calling Open() on Data Reduction Proxy LevelDB.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LevelDBSize
histogram.summary: Size of Data Reduction Proxy LevelDB measured at startup.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LoFi.Accuracy
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 8/2017, per server-directed preview decision.
histogram.summary: Records the accuracy of estimated network quality when using Lo-Fi. Network quality predicted at the time of navigation start is compared with the network quality observed during the specified time interval following the start of the navigation. This metric is recorded only when the session is in Lo-Fi enabled field trial.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LoFi.ImplicitOptOutAction
histogram.summary: Counts of Lo-Fi implicit opt out actions. If the user chooses to "Load image" for k pages in a session, LoFi is disabled for the remainder of that session. If Lo-Fi is disabled for j consecutive sessions, Lo-Fi is disabled for that user until the next implicit opt out epoch, which may be in a later session, or never. k and j default to 3 and are assigned via the DataReductionProxyLoFi field trial.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LoFi.SessionState
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of October 2017 per transition to blacklist.
histogram.summary: Tracks Lo-Fi usage at the granularity of browser sessions. Counts for each session if Lo-Fi was used or not. In the case where Lo-Fi was not used, records if it was because the network quality was always good or because the user had implicitly opted out. The session where a user permanently opts out is counted in the Lo-Fi used bucket.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LoFi.TransformationType
histogram.summary: Counts of pageloads that received or requested, but did not receive, various Lo-Fi lite page transformations. Recorded when a lite page is requested or received through Data compression proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LoFi.UIAction
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 9/2016, replaced by Previews.ContextMenuAction.LoFi.
histogram.summary:
User interactions with the Lo-Fi snackbar and context menu option. These include:
Displays and clicks on the "Load images" snackbar. Displays and clicks on the "Load image" and "Load images"context menu options. Count of pages where the user has clicked "Load image" at least once.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.MissingViaHeader.Bytes
histogram.summary: Counts the response bytes of responses that Chrome expected to come through a data reduction proxy and have the data reduction proxy via header, but where the data reduction proxy via header was missing. Note that this does not include responses that were bypassed.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.MissingViaHeader.ResponseCode
histogram.summary: Counts the different HTTP response codes of responses that Chrome expected to come through a data reduction proxy and have the data reduction proxy via header, but where the data reduction proxy via header was missing.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.NetworkChangeEvents
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times various events occur when the data reduction proxy is enabled and the IP address of the client changes.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.NetworkProperties.CacheHit
histogram.summary: Records if the network properties of a network were found in the cache or not. Recorded every time there is a change in the connection type.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.Pingback.Attempted
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times that pageload metrics were queued to be attempted or not queued based on the reporting fraction. Recorded once per pageload.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.Pingback.CrashAction
histogram.summary: Counts various actions related to crash detection in the pingback client. This is recorded only when a renderer crash occurs on a Data Reduction Proxy page load.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.Pingback.Succeeded
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times that a batched pingback request succeeded or failed at being sent to the server. Recorded everytime a pingback request is attempted.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ProbeURL
histogram.summary: Counts various outcomes of requesting the data reduction proxy's probe URL.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ProbeURLNetError
histogram.summary: Reports the type of network error when the data reduction proxy probe fails due to a network error.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.PromoAction
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 3/2015, replaced by DataReductionProxy.UIAction.
histogram.summary: Samples which method was used by the user to dismiss the proxy promo. This is sampled when the promo leaves view, with the sampled value depending on which of four possible controls the user used.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.Protocol.AcceptTransform
histogram.summary: Records the sending of accepted transform headers to the data reduction proxy and also receiving transforms and policies from the data reduction proxy. Recorded on a per page request basis and also on a per image resource request basis for empty-image transforms. Headers for both types of requests (pages and resources) are recorded here for convenience in viewing in a single histogram. In general, the separate groups of request/receive buckets for either page or resource types can be compared.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.Protocol.NotAcceptingTransform
histogram.summary: Records the reason that a page request is not accepting proxy server transforms. Recorded on a per page request basis.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ProxyPrefMigrationResult
histogram.summary: Records the result of migrating the Data Reduction Proxy away from being configured via a proxy preference, including cases when no migration action was taken.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ProxySchemeUsed
histogram.summary: Records the scheme of the data reduction proxy server. Recorded for every request that is fetched successfully through a data reduction proxy server.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.Quic.DefaultAlternativeProxy
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated in 5/2017, since the experiment to use default QUIC alternative proxy was deprecated.
histogram.summary: Status of the availability of valid alternative data reduction proxy servers. This metric is recorded only when data reduction proxy delegate is queried for the value of the supported alternative proxy server.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.Quic.OnAlternativeProxyBroken
histogram.obsolete: As of M66 this has been superceded by Net.AlternativeProxyFailed.
histogram.summary: Number of times an alternative QUIC proxy was marked as broken.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.Quic.ProxyStatus
histogram.summary: Records the result of the attempt to use a data reduction proxy that supports QUIC. The metric is logged for every request for which the resolved proxy is a data reduction HTTPS proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.RequestCompletionErrorCodes
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated in 2/2016, since it didn't get recorded for many kinds of error codes when it should have been recorded, and Net.HttpRequestCompletionErrorCodes is a good enough replacement when filtering for clients with the Data Reduction Proxy enabled.
histogram.summary: Reports the different network errors that requests that try to use the data reduction proxy are completing with, including OK and ABORTED.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.RequestCompletionErrorCodes.MainFrame
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated in 2/2016, since it didn't get recorded for many kinds of error codes when it should have been recorded, and Net.HttpRequestCompletionErrorCodes is a good enough replacement when filtering for clients with the Data Reduction Proxy enabled.
histogram.summary: Reports the different network errors that main frame resource requests that try to use the data reduction proxy are completing with, including OK and ABORTED.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ResourceContentType
histogram.summary: Content type of the resource determined by data reduction proxy. Used for determining the data reduction proxy that should be used for fetching the resource. Logged once per resource for only the resources that are eligible for fetching by data reduction proxies.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ResponseProxyServerStatus
histogram.summary: Reports the state of the proxy server field in the response seen by the Data Reduction Proxy bypass logic.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.SavingsCleared.NegativeSystemClock
histogram.summary: Records if the data reduction savings were cleared because the system clock moved back by more than 1 day. Recorded at Chrome startup if the data reduction proxy is enabled.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.SavingsCleared.Reason
histogram.summary: Records why the data reduction savings were cleared. See also DataReductionProxy.DeleteBrowsingHistory.NumBuckets.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.SecureProxyCheck.Latency
histogram.summary: Time from when the Data Reduction Proxy secure proxy check starts until it completes.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.SettingsConversion
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 3/2015, replaced by DataReductionProxy.UIAction.
histogram.summary: Samples of user interactions with the ON/OFF switch in the settings menu for reducing data usage. Only the setting changes between entering the reducing data usage setting menu and leaving the menu will be sampled. So if a user enters the menu with OFF and leaves it with OFF, it is counted as one OFF to OFF conversion regardless of how many times they toggle the ON/OFF switch.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.SnackbarPromo.DataSavings
histogram.summary: Logs the data savings in MB of the data reduction proxy snackbar promo that was shown. The snackbar promo shows the user the amount of data Chrome has saved them using Data Saver.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.StartupNegativeSavingsPercent
histogram.summary: Records the negative data savings percentage at Chrome startup for data saver enabled users who have browsed a reasonable amount (at least 10MB since data saver was enabled).

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.StartupSavingsPercent
histogram.summary: Records the data savings percentage at Chrome startup for data saver enabled users who have browsed a reasonable amount (at least 10MB since data saver was enabled). Negative savings are not recorded.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.StartupState
histogram.summary: Samples of the state of the data reduction proxy on Chrome startup. The proxy will either be unavailable (the feature hasn't been rolled out to this user yet), not enabled (the feature is available but the user doesn't have it turned on), or enabled (the feature is enabled and turned on).

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.SuccessfulRequestCompletionCounts
histogram.summary: Count of the number of requests that were successfully fetched via data reduction proxy. The bucket represents the index of the data reduction proxy in the data reduction proxy list.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.SuccessfulRequestCompletionCounts.MainFrame
histogram.summary: Count of the number of main frame requests that were successfully fetched via data reduction proxy. The bucket represents the index of the data reduction proxy in the data reduction proxy list.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ThisWeekAggregateKB.Services
histogram.summary: Records the aggregate data use of services for the current week. Different conditions are added as suffixes to this histogram. Recorded when Chrome is started, so this does not include the current session. The aggregation happens when requests complete. The current week calculation is based on week starting from the Epoch day(Thursday, January 1, 1970), and every 7 days is treated as a week.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ThisWeekAggregateKB.UserTraffic.Downstream.ContentType
histogram.summary: Records the aggregate data use for the current week. This is the downstream data use of all user-initiated traffic. Recorded when Chrome is started, so this does not include the current session. The aggregation happens when requests complete. The current week calculation is based on week starting from the Epoch day(Thursday, January 1, 1970), and every 7 days is treated as a week.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.TimeToFirstDataSaverRequest
histogram.summary: Records the time duration between the data saver initialization and the first HTTP request that is resolved through the data saver proxy. On IP address changes, time duration between the IP address change event and the next subsequent HTTP request that resolves through the data saver proxy is recorded.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.UIAction
histogram.summary:
Samples of user interactions with the Data Reduction Proxy promos, First Run Experience, main menu footer, and settings menu. All samples are mutually exclusive. These samples include:
The user interactions with the First Run Experience. On Android when the user sees the FRE card, the Data Reduction Proxy defaults to ON. The user can disable the proxy via a switch on the card. The state of the proxy is sampled when the First Run Experience is completed and only reported if the Data Reduction Proxy card was shown.
The user interactions with the second run promo. On Android, there are two actions that dismiss this promo (enable or dismiss). This is sampled when the promo leaves view.
The user interactions with the Infobar promo. On Android, there are two actions that dismiss the promo (enable, dismiss). This is sampled when the promo leaves view.
The user interactions with the Snackbar promo. The user can either ignore the promo or click the action to the settings menu. From the settings menu, the user may enable or disable the proxy.
The user interactions with the main menu footer. The user can click the footer to enter the settings menu. From the settings menu, the user may enable or disable the proxy.
Any other user interaction with the ON/OFF switch in the settings menu for reducing data usage. Only the setting changes between entering the reducing data usage setting menu and leaving the menu will be sampled regardless of how many times they toggle the ON/OFF switch. Other samples that report ON/OFF settings menu changes are not also reported here.
The views of and user interactions with the site breakdown in the settings menu. The site breakdown can be sorted by data saved descending or data used descending, and expanded to view more sites. The Data Reduction statistics can also be reset.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.UserViewedOriginalSize
histogram.summary: The total original content size displayed to the user. Computed over the last 30 days. Recorded when the user views the data savings in the UI.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.UserViewedSavingsDifferenceWithBreakdown
histogram.summary: The difference between the total data savings as displayed to the user and the sum of the data savings for all hosts in the data use breakdown. Calculated as a percentage of `abs(summedSavingsTotal - displayedSavingsTotal) / (summedSavingsTotal + displayedSavingsTotal)`. Recorded when the user views the data savings in the UI.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.UserViewedSavingsPercent
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated March 2018. Percent no longer displayed.
histogram.summary: The percentage of data savings displayed to users. Computed over the last 30 days. Recorded when the user views the data savings in the UI.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.UserViewedSavingsSize
histogram.summary: The total data saved displayed to the user. Computed over the last 30 days. Recorded when the user views the data savings in the UI.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.UserViewedUsageDifferenceWithBreakdown
histogram.summary: The difference between the total data usage as displayed to the user and the sum of the data usage for all hosts in the data use breakdown. Calculated as a percentage of `abs(summedUsageTotal - displayedUsageTotal) / (summedUsageTotal + displayedUsageTotal)`. Recorded when the user views the data savings in the UI.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.WarmupURL.FetchAttemptEvent
