histogram.name: Extensions.Events.DispatchToComponentWithSuspendedEventPage
histogram.summary: Recorded every time an event is dispatched to a *component* extension with a suspended event page. Otherwise identical to Extensions.Events.DispatchWithSuspendedEventPage.

histogram.name: Extensions.Events.DispatchWithPersistentBackgroundPage
histogram.summary: Recorded every time an event is dispatched to an extension with a persistent background page. The event may or may not have been dispatched to the background page itself, for example it may have been dispatched to its popup. When this metric is recorded, Extensions.Events.Dispatch will also be recorded.

histogram.name: Extensions.Events.DispatchWithRunningEventPage
histogram.summary: Recorded every time an event is dispatched to an extension with an event page that was already running (i.e. at some point had been woken from its suspended state, see Extensions.Events.DispatchWithSuspendedEventPage). The event may or may not have been dispatched to the event page itself, for example it may have been dispatched to its popup. When this metric is recorded, Extensions.Events.Dispatch will also be recorded.

histogram.name: Extensions.Events.DispatchWithSuspendedEventPage
histogram.summary: Recorded every time an event is dispatched to an extension with an event page that was suspended. This implies that firing this event woke up the event page. The event may or may not have been dispatched to the event page itself, for example it may have been dispatched to its popup, but regardless it will wake the event page. When this metric is recorded, Extensions.Events.Dispatch will also be recorded.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionCacheCount
histogram.summary: Number of cached extensions on disk. Reported on Chrome OS during user session start.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionCacheSize
histogram.summary: Total size of .crx files in cache on disk. Reported on Chrome OS during user session start.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionCreationTime
histogram.summary:
The amount of time taken to create a single extension object. This includes parsing all the different extension manifest keys and initializating the associated manifest data.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionFrameMapCacheHit
histogram.summary: True if the cache of the extension frame map was hit during access on the IO thread.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionFrameMapLookupSuccessful
histogram.summary: True if the lookup for a frame in the extension frame map succeeded after a cache miss.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionHostMonitoring.MaxActiveLoading
histogram.summary: The maximum number of ExtensionHosts (primarily background/event pages) that were actively loading at any one time, within the first minute of Chrome startup. Emitted exactly one minute after startup.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionHostMonitoring.MaxInQueue
histogram.summary: The maximum number of ExtensionHosts (primarily background/event pages) in the queue awaiting being loaded (via an ExtensionHostQueue), within the first minute of Chrome startup. Emitted exactly one minute after startup.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionHostMonitoring.NumLoaded
histogram.summary: The total number of ExtensionHosts that completed loading in the first minute of Chrome startup. Emitted exactly one minute after startup.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionHostMonitoring.NumQueued
histogram.summary: The total number of ExtensionHosts (primarily background/event pages) that were added to a queue to start loading, within the first minute of Chrome startup. These may or may not end up starting, let alone finishing, depending on the queue behavior. Emitted exactly one minute after startup.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionInstalled
histogram.summary: An extension has been installed.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionLocation
histogram.summary: The number of extensions loaded at startup time grouped by Extension::Location.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionManagement_RefreshTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during ExtensionManagement::Refresh.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionRendererStateCacheHit
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 4/2016. ExtensionRendererState was replaced with ExtensionFrameMap.
histogram.summary: True if the cache for the ExtensionRendererState was hit during a lookup.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionRootPathLength
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Length of the Extensions dir path inside the profile directory.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionServiceInitTime
histogram.summary: Time taken for the ExtensionService to initialize, including the time it takes to load the extensions for the service's profile and parse their manifests. This happens during startup and also any time a new profile is loaded.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionServiceNotifyReadyListenersTime
histogram.summary: Time taken for the ExtensionService to notify all ready listeners that the extension system is now ready. This happens as part of the total initialization time of ExtensionService, measured in Extensions.ExtensionServiceInitTime.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionUninstalled
histogram.summary: An extension has been uninstalled.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionUpdaterFirstUpdateCheckErrorsGoogleUrl
histogram.summary: Records the error codes of the extension updater update check errors. These events are triggered only when the extension updater gets an error for the first time (before any retry) in the update check phase for a google.com domain.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionUpdaterFirstUpdateCheckErrorsNonGoogleUrl
histogram.summary: Records the error codes of the extension updater update check errors. These events are triggered only when the extension updater gets an error for the first time (before any retry) in the update check phase for a non google.com domain.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionUpdaterRawUpdateCalls
histogram.summary: The number of extensions that are checked for update. This number is emitted right before the extensions are split between the current extension updater and the unified extension updater (if used).

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionUpdaterUpdateCalls
histogram.summary: The number of extensions that are passed over to the extension updater for update check. Triggered when the extension updater starts doing update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionUpdaterUpdateFoundCount
histogram.summary: The number of extensions that have updates in an update check session. Triggered when the extension updater found an update for an extension.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExtensionUpdaterUpdateResults
histogram.summary: Records the update results of extensions in an extension updater session, grouped by ExtensionUpdaterUpdateResult.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExternalExtensionEvent
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records what happens to extensions that are sideloaded, grouped by the ExternalExtensionEvent enum.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExternalItemState
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of sideloaded apps/extensions loaded on startup grouped by enabled/disabled state.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExternalJsonCount
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of extensions referenced in the external extensions source at path chrome::DIR_EXTERNAL_EXTENSIONS.

histogram.name: Extensions.ExternalWarningUninstallationResult
histogram.summary: Whether or not the uninstallation of an external extension succeeded. Triggered when the external install warning is shown to the user and the user selects to remove the extension.

histogram.name: Extensions.FeatureProviderStaticInitTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during extensions::FeatureProvider::Static. Only measured while in the browser process.

histogram.name: Extensions.FileAccessAllowed
histogram.summary: The number of extensions (and friends) that could have been given access to the file:// scheme, and were, for users that have at least one extension that could have been given access. This excludes anything that doesn't show up in chrome://extensions (platform apps, hosted apps, component extensions), policy-installed extensions, and unpacked extensions. See also Extensions.FileAccessNotAllowed.

histogram.name: Extensions.FileAccessNotAllowed
histogram.summary: The number of extensions (and friends) that could have been given access to the file:// scheme, but weren't, for users that have at least one extension that could have been given access. This excludes anything that doesn't show up in chrome://extensions (platform apps, hosted apps, component extensions), policy-installed extensions, and unpacked extensions. See also Extensions.FileAccessAllowed.

histogram.name: Extensions.FileInstallation
histogram.summary: Record the time taken to physically move the extention files from temporary location to the final installation directory. It includes the time to flush the file system if ExtensionUseSafeInstallation field trial is enabled. This is recorded once per extension install/update.

histogram.name: Extensions.FontSettingsEventRouterCtorTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during the FontSettingsEventRouter construction.

histogram.name: Extensions.ForceInstalledFailureReason
histogram.summary: The reason why enterprise policy forced extensions were not installed. Recorded for each forced extension that failed to install after 5 minutes. Recorded together with "Extensions.ForceInstalledTimedOutCount" histogram.

histogram.name: Extensions.ForceInstalledLoadTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time elapsed during installation of enterprise policy forced extensions.

histogram.name: Extensions.ForceInstalledTimedOutAndNotInstalledCount
histogram.summary: Number of enterprise policy forced extensions that are not installed after 5 minutes if at least one of policy forced extensions is not yet enabled.

histogram.name: Extensions.ForceInstalledTimedOutCount
histogram.summary: Number of enterprise policy forced extensions that are not enabled after 5 minutes timeout, while might be already installed.

histogram.name: Extensions.FromWebstoreInconsistency
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of apps/extensions loaded on startup with an inconsistent "from webstore" state. This means an item that is flagged as from_webstore, but with either a non-webstore update_url or an external install location.

histogram.name: Extensions.FunctionCalls
histogram.summary: Number of calls to extension functions.

histogram.name: Extensions.Functions.FailedTime
histogram.summary: Recorded when an extension function call fails and finishes execution. The suffix indicates the exact bucket the function is in. See also Extensions.Functions.FailedTotalExecutionTime.

histogram.name: Extensions.Functions.FailedTotalExecutionTime
histogram.summary: The total amount of time it took to execute an extension function that failed from the time the extension function is called to the time the function responds. Note that since some extension functions are inherently slow (anything that requires user interaction, for instance), this is not a definitive source for function performance.

histogram.name: Extensions.Functions.HandleResponseElapsedTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time it takes to handle a response for an extension API request. Recorded on the renderer side once we receive the response IPC from the browser and have deserialized the arguments and passed them to the JS context. Only recorded if the response is successfully handled.

histogram.name: Extensions.Functions.StartRequestElapsedTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time it takes to start an extension API request. Recorded on the renderer side after we perform JS pre-processing, but includes all serialization and dispatching. Only recorded if the request is successfully dispatched.

histogram.name: Extensions.Functions.SucceededTime
histogram.summary: Recorded when an extension function call succeeds and finishes execution. The suffix indicates the exact bucket the function is in. See also Extensions.Functions.SucceededTotalExecutionTime.

histogram.name: Extensions.Functions.SucceededTotalExecutionTime
histogram.summary: The total amount of time it took to execute an extension function that succeeded from the time the extension function is called to the time the function responds. Note that since some extension functions are inherently slow (anything that requires user interaction, for instance), this is not a definitive source for function performance.

histogram.name: Extensions.Functions.SynchronousExecutionTime
histogram.summary: The synchronous amount of time an extension function takes. Note that many extension functions run asynchronously; see also Extensions.Functions.[Succeeded|Failed]TotalExecutionTime.

histogram.name: Extensions.GetUserDataTempDir
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: What happens when the extensions system tries to get a temp dir to unpack in?

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_AutoDisable3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when it is automatically disabled due to a permission increase (e.g., after an extension upgrade). To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument AutoDisable. For Sync users, this may be reported for each device, depending on whether the Sync update or the extension auto-update happen first.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_Install3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when it was installed. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument Install. For Sync users, this is reported for each device.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_InstallAbort3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when installation was aborted (e.g. because the parent window of the confirmation dialog went away), not including installation errors and user cancels. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument InstallAbort.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_InstallCancel3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when installation was canceled. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument InstallCancel.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_Load3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when it was loaded (which happens at profile open or extension install). To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument Load.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_ReEnable3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when it was re-enabled from a confirmation prompt. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument ReEnable.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_ReEnableAbort3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when the re-enable prompt was aborted (e.g. because the parent window of the confirmation dialog went away), not including installation errors and manual user cancels. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument ReEnableAbort.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_ReEnableCancel3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when the re-enable was canceled from the confirmation prompt. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument ReEnableCancel.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_Uninstall3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when it was uninstalled. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument Uninstall. For Sync users, this is reported for each device.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_WebStoreInstall3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when it was installed through the web store. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument WebStoreInstall. Contrary to the more-general HasPermissions_Install3 histogram, this one is NOT reported for each device.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_WebStoreInstallAbort3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when installation from the web store was aborted (e.g. because the parent window of the confirmation dialog went away), not including installation errors and user cancels. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument WebStoreInstallAbort.

histogram.name: Extensions.HasPermissions_WebStoreInstallCancel3
histogram.summary: Whether there were any permissions present in an extension when installation from the web store was canceled. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument WebStoreInstallCancel.

histogram.name: Extensions.HostedAppLaunchContainer
histogram.summary: Records the container used for a hosted app launch. The definition of 'launch' for the purposes of this histogram is different to that used for the launch type histograms. This definition is less inclusive, for example it does not include navigations to the hosted app due to normal browsing, which the other definition does include. This definition could be thought of more as explicit launches via a launch surface such as the app launcher, chrome://apps, or a shortcut.

histogram.name: Extensions.HostedAppUnlimitedStoragePersistentStorageUsage
histogram.summary: The usage (in kilobytes) of persistent storage in a hosted app with the unlimitedStorage permission. This is logged each time the storage is used*, so this also serves as a cap of the number of calls that would fail if the app did not have the unlimited storage permission. *Capped at once per 30 seconds so as to not impact performance.

histogram.name: Extensions.HostedAppUnlimitedStorageTemporaryStorageUsage
histogram.summary: The percentage of "normal" (that is, not unlimited) quota that a hosted app with unlimited storage is currently using. This is logged each time the storage is used*, so the number of times the app would use greater than 100% of the default storage can serve as a cap for the number of calls that would fail if the app did not have the unlimitedStorage permission. *Capped at once per 30 seconds so as to not impact performance.

histogram.name: Extensions.HostedAppUnlimitedStorageUsage
histogram.summary: The usage (in kilobytes) of a hosted app with the unlimitedStorage api permission, recorded once per run per app the first time we load storage for the hosted app. This is separate from the other metrics on hosted app unlimited storage usage because it is logged once per extension per run.

histogram.name: Extensions.IncognitoAllowed
histogram.summary: The number of extensions (and friends) that could have been allowed in incognito, and were, for users that have at least one extension that could have been allowed. This excludes anything that doesn't show up in chrome://extensions (platform apps, hosted apps, component extensions), policy-installed extensions, and unpacked extensions. See also Extensions.IncognitoNotAllowed.

histogram.name: Extensions.IncognitoNotAllowed
histogram.summary: The number of extensions (and friends) that could have been allowed in incognito, but weren't, for users that have at least one extension that could have been allowed. This excludes anything that doesn't show up in chrome://extensions (platform apps, hosted apps, component extensions), policy-installed extensions, and unpacked extensions. See also Extensions.IncognitoAllowed.

histogram.name: Extensions.InitExtensionControlledPrefsTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during ExtensionPrefs::InitExtensionControlledPrefs.

histogram.name: Extensions.InitPrefGetExtensionsTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during the GetExtensions call within ExtensionPrefs::InitPrefStore.

histogram.name: Extensions.InitPrefStoreTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during ExtensionPrefs::InitPrefStore.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectCssTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time for a CSS file to be injected into a page.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectedScriptExecutionTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to execute all scripts for one location within an extension. Recorded every time content scripts injected by extensions are executed. Unlike Extensions.InjectScriptTime, this includes execution time of asynchronously injected scripts.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectEnd_BlockingScriptCount
histogram.summary: Number of blocking scripts injected at document end by extensions.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectEnd_ScriptCount
histogram.summary: Number of scripts injected at document end by extensions.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectEnd_Time
histogram.summary: Time taken to inject all scripts at document end by extensions. Not reported if scripts are executed asynchronously.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectIdle_BlockingScriptCount
histogram.summary: Number of blocking scripts injected at document idle by extensions.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectIdle_ScriptCount
histogram.summary: Number of scripts injected at document idle by extensions.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectIdle_Time
histogram.summary: Time taken to inject all scripts at document idle by extensions. Not reported if scripts are executed asynchronously.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectScriptTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to inject all scripts by extensions.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectStart_BlockingScriptCount
histogram.summary: Number of blocking scripts injected at document start by extensions.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectStart_CssCount
histogram.summary: Number of css files injected by extensions.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectStart_ScriptCount
histogram.summary: Number of scripts injected at document start by extensions.

histogram.name: Extensions.InjectStart_Time
histogram.summary: Time taken to inject css/scripts at document start by extensions. Not reported if scripts are executed asynchronously.

histogram.name: Extensions.InstallPrompt.Accepted
histogram.summary: Whether the user accepted or aborted an extension installation.

histogram.name: Extensions.InstallPrompt.Type
histogram.summary: Type of the extension install prompt displayed when an extension installation is triggered.

histogram.name: Extensions.InstallPromptExperiment.ShowDetails
histogram.summary: Actions on the show details link grouped by action type when the install prompt trial is running.

histogram.name: Extensions.InstallPromptExperiment.ShowPermissions
histogram.summary: Actions on the show permissions link grouped by action type when the install prompt trial is running.

histogram.name: Extensions.InstallSource
histogram.summary: Installs grouped by the location property in prefs.

histogram.name: Extensions.InstallType
histogram.summary: Installs grouped by Extension::HistogramType.

histogram.name: Extensions.InstanceID.GetToken.OptionsCount
histogram.summary: The number of options provided to the GetToken request. Recorded when the extension has made a call to chrome.instanceID.getToken().

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadAll
histogram.summary: The number of extensions and themes loaded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadAllComponentTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to load all component extensions at profile open. This happens as part of the total initialization time of ExtensionService, measured in Extensions.ExtensionServiceInitTime.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadAllTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to load all non-component extensions at profile open. This happens as part of the total initialization time of ExtensionService, measured in Extensions.ExtensionServiceInitTime.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadAllTime2
histogram.summary: Time taken to load all non-component extensions at profile open and record metrics. This happens as part of the total initialization time of ExtensionService, measured in Extensions.ExtensionServiceInitTime.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadApp
histogram.summary: The number of apps loaded by each user at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadAppExternal
histogram.summary: The number of externally managed apps loaded by each user at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadAppUser
histogram.summary: The number of user-installed apps loaded by each user at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadBrowserAction
histogram.summary: The number of browser action extensions loaded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadContentPack
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 4/2015. Replaced by ManagedUsers.Whitelist.Count.
histogram.summary: The number of content-pack extensions loaded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadCreationFlags
histogram.summary: The creation flags of all extensions loaded at profile open grouped by Extension::InitFromValueFlags.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadExtension
histogram.summary: The number of extensions loaded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadExtensionExternal
histogram.summary: The number of externally managed extensions loaded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadExtensionUser
histogram.summary: The number of user-installed extensions loaded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadExternal
histogram.summary: The number of externally managed extensions and apps loaded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadHostedApp
histogram.summary: The number of hosted apps loaded by each user at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadNoExtensionAction
histogram.summary: The number of extensions that had neither a page nor browser action specified in their manifest. Recorded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadOffStoreItems
histogram.summary: The number of enabled extensions or apps the user has installed that do not update from the Chrome Web Store. Recorded during profile initialization.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadPackagedApp
histogram.summary: The number of legacy packaged apps loaded by each user at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadPlatformApp
histogram.summary: The number of platform apps loaded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadTheme
histogram.summary: The number of themes loaded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadType
histogram.summary: The number of extensions loaded at profile open grouped by Extension::HistogramType.

histogram.name: Extensions.LoadUserScript
histogram.summary: The number of converted user scripts loaded at profile open.

histogram.name: Extensions.LockedFullscreenStateRequest
histogram.summary: Records requests to enter or exit locked fullscreen mode through extension APIs. Used only on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: Extensions.LongInjectionTaskTime
histogram.summary:
The amount of time taken to inject content scripts. If multiple scripts are injected within a single task, this records the time taken to execute all the scripts.
This metric only logs tasks longer than 50 milliseconds. This threshold aligns with the definition of "long task" in Long Tasks API (https://w3c.github.io/longtasks/).
Note that this histogram itself doesn't tell you what percentage of content scripts are greater than 50 ms. See Extensions.Inject{Start,End,Idle}_Time and Extensions.InjectedScriptExecutionTime.* for the overall distribution.

histogram.name: Extensions.ManifestFetchFailureRetryCountGoogleUrl
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of times chrome retried to download an extension update manifest with a url on a google.com domain, before eventually giving up.

histogram.name: Extensions.ManifestFetchFailureRetryCountOtherUrl
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of times chrome retried to download an extension update manifest with a url on a non google.com domain, before eventually giving up.

histogram.name: Extensions.ManifestFetchSuccessRetryCountGoogleUrl
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of times chrome retried to download an extension update manifest with a url on a google.com domain, before eventually succeeding.

histogram.name: Extensions.ManifestFetchSuccessRetryCountOtherUrl
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of times chrome retried to download an extension update manifest with a url on a non google.com domain, before eventually succeeding.

histogram.name: Extensions.ManifestIconSetIconWasVisibleForPacked
histogram.summary: Reports whether an icon that's specified in the manifest is considered visible by our icon analysis code. This stat is only for packed extensions, which we check dynamically when the icon is requested. The stat is emitted the first time the default icon is requested, since we cache the icon that's selected.

histogram.name: Extensions.ManifestIconSetIconWasVisibleForUnpacked
histogram.summary: Reports whether loading an icon declarated in the extension's manifest icon set is considered visible by our icon analysis code. This stat is only for unpacked extensions, which we check at load time.

histogram.name: Extensions.ManifestReloadNeedsRelocalization
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of extension loads on startup where it is necessary to reload the mainfest because the locale has changed.

histogram.name: Extensions.ManifestReloadNotNeeded
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of extension loads on startup where it is not necessary to reload the extension's manifest.

histogram.name: Extensions.ManifestReloadUnpackedDir
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of extension loads on startup where it is necessary to reload the manifest because the extension is unpacked.

histogram.name: Extensions.ManifestVersion
histogram.summary: The manifest version of each loaded extension.

histogram.name: Extensions.Messaging.ExtensionPortsCreated
histogram.summary: The number of ports created in a given script context at a specific time. Recorded once at the destruction of the script context (when no more ports could be created) if and only if at least one port was created in the context. Only includes ports to extension contexts (i.e., not ports created to tabs or native apps).

histogram.name: Extensions.Messaging.GetPortIdAsyncTime
histogram.summary: The total amount of time between when an extension opens a new channel and when it receives the global port id from the browser and can begin sending messages.

histogram.name: Extensions.Messaging.GetPortIdSyncTime
histogram.summary: The amount of synchronous time taken for the renderer to request an extension message port id from the browser when a new channel is first opened by an extension.

histogram.name: Extensions.Messaging.IncludeChannelIdBehavior
histogram.summary: The behavior for including the TLS channel ID in the information sent with extension messaging. Recorded once per opened channel.

histogram.name: Extensions.Messaging.MessageSize
histogram.summary: The size, in bytes, of a message sent from an extension using one of the messaging APIs (e.g. chrome.runtime.sendMessage). All message sizes are logged, but messages over 64 MB in size aren't sent.

histogram.name: Extensions.Messaging.SetPortIdTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time for the renderer to inform the browser process of a new port being created with a certain id. This replaces the flow for Extensions.Messaging.GetPortId[A]SyncTime.

histogram.name: Extensions.NetworkDelay
histogram.summary: Time that network requests were blocked due to extensions.

histogram.name: Extensions.NetworkDelayPercentage
histogram.summary: Percentage of total lifetime a network request was blocked due to an extension.

histogram.name: Extensions.NetworkDelayRegistryLoad
histogram.summary: Time that network requests were blocked due to relevant rule registries loading.

histogram.name: Extensions.NetworkDelayStartup
histogram.obsolete: Never used. Added to source 06/2012. Removed from source and added here 09/2016.
histogram.summary: Delay of network requests due to waiting for extension declarativeWebRequest rules to be loaded from disk at startup.

histogram.name: Extensions.NonWebstoreLocation
histogram.summary: The number of apps/extensions with a non-webstore update_url loaded at startup time grouped by Extension::Location.

histogram.name: Extensions.NonWebStoreNewTabPageOverrides
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of non-WebStore extensions on startup that override the new tab page.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_AutoDisable
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 5/2014, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_AutoDisable2.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it is automatically disabled due to a permission increase (e.g., after an extension upgrade).

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_AutoDisable2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2015, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_AutoDisable3.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it is automatically disabled due to a permission increase (e.g., after an extension upgrade).

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_AutoDisable3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it is automatically disabled due to a permission increase (e.g., after an extension upgrade). To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument AutoDisable. For Sync users, this may be reported for each device, depending on whether the Sync update or the extension auto-update happen first.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_Install
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 5/2014, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_Install2.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was installed.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_Install2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2015, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_Install3.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was installed.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_Install3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was installed. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument Install. For Sync users, this is reported for each device.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_InstallAbort
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 5/2014, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_InstallAbort2.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation was aborted, not including installation errors and user cancels.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_InstallAbort2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2015, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_InstallAbort3.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation was aborted, not including installation errors and user cancels.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_InstallAbort3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation was aborted (e.g. because the parent window of the confirmation dialog went away), not including installation errors and user cancels. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument InstallAbort.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_InstallCancel
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 5/2014, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_InstallCancel2.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation was canceled.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_InstallCancel2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2015, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_InstallCancel3.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation was canceled.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_InstallCancel3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation was canceled. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument InstallCancel.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_Load
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 5/2014, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_Load2.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was loaded.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_Load2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2015, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_Load3.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was loaded.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_Load3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was loaded (which happens at profile open or extension install). To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument Load.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_ReEnable
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 5/2014, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_ReEnable2.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was re-enabled from a confirmation prompt.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_ReEnable2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2015, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_ReEnable3.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was re-enabled from a confirmation prompt.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_ReEnable3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was re-enabled from a confirmation prompt. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument ReEnable.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_ReEnableAbort
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 5/2014, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_ReEnableAbort2.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when the re-enable prompt was aborted, not including installation errors and manual user cancels.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_ReEnableAbort2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2015, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_ReEnableAbort3.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when the re-enable prompt was aborted, not including installation errors and manual user cancels.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_ReEnableAbort3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when the re-enable prompt was aborted (e.g. because the parent window of the confirmation dialog went away), not including installation errors and manual user cancels. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument ReEnableAbort.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_ReEnableCancel
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 5/2014, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_ReEnableCancel2.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when the re-enable was canceled from the confirmation prompt.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_ReEnableCancel2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2015, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_ReEnableCancel3.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when the re-enable was canceled from the confirmation prompt.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_ReEnableCancel3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when the re-enable was canceled from the confirmation prompt. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument ReEnableCancel.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_Uninstall
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 5/2014, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_Uninstall2.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was uninstalled.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_Uninstall2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2015, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_Uninstall3.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was uninstalled.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_Uninstall3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was uninstalled. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument Uninstall. For Sync users, this is reported for each device.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstall
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 5/2014, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstall2.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was installed through the web store.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstall2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 6/2015, replaced by Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstall3.
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was installed through the web store.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstall3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when it was installed through the web store. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument WebStoreInstall. Contrary to the more-general Permissions_Install3 histogram, this one is NOT reported for each device.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstallAbort
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation from the web store was aborted, not including installation errors and user cancels.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstallAbort2
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation from the web store was aborted, not including installation errors and user cancels.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstallAbort3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation from the web store was aborted (e.g. because the parent window of the confirmation dialog went away), not including installation errors and user cancels. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument WebStoreInstallAbort.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstallCancel
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation from the web store was canceled.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstallCancel2
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation from the web store was canceled.

histogram.name: Extensions.Permissions_WebStoreInstallCancel3
histogram.summary: The permissions present in an extension when installation from the web store was canceled. To find places where this histogram may be emitted, look for calls to ExtensionService::RecordPermissionMessagesHistogram with the argument WebStoreInstallCancel.

histogram.name: Extensions.PopupCreateTime
histogram.summary: The time taken between a user clicking on a button to open a popup, and the popup finishing loading its initial URL. This is a good way to measure the UI responsiveness of extension popups. It will be strictly larger than Extensions.PopupLoadTime2, which only measures the time it takes to load - there may be a delay between clicking and starting to load, for example, if the popup's host got caught in a queue.

histogram.name: Extensions.PopupLoadTime
histogram.summary: The time for an Extension's popup to load.

histogram.name: Extensions.PopupLoadTime2
histogram.summary: The time taken for an extension popup to load its initial URL. This may not include the time taken for an extension process to start, if the extension already has a background page running. It does not necessarily correlate with the amount of time since the popup was *clicked* either, since there may be a delay between the user clicking and the loading started. Extensions.PopupCreateTime measures this instead.

histogram.name: Extensions.ProcessManagerStartupHostsTime
histogram.summary: The time taken to start up persistent background pages for extensions in ExtensionProcessManager when the extension system notifies that it is ready.

histogram.name: Extensions.ResetPermissionsIncrease
histogram.summary: Whether the DISABLE_PERMISSIONS_INCREASE disable reason was removed from an extension while checking for a permissions increase. Recorded during startup and on reload and update, for each extension that has the DISABLE_PERMISSIONS_INCREASE reason.

histogram.name: Extensions.ResourceDirectoryTimestampQueryLatency
histogram.summary: The initialization latency (in milliseconds) introduced to each extension resource request by querying the directory timestamp.

histogram.name: Extensions.ResourceLastModifiedDelta
histogram.summary: The difference in seconds between an extension resource's last modification time and its extension base directory's creation time. Recorded on each extension resource request if the difference is non-negative (i.e., the resource's last modification time is more recent than the directory's creation time.) For cases where the directory creation date is more recent, see Extensions.ResourceLastModifiedNegativeDelta instead.

histogram.name: Extensions.ResourceLastModifiedNegativeDelta
histogram.summary: The absolute difference in seconds between an extension resource's last modification time and extension base directory's creation time. Recorded on each extension resource request if the difference is negative (i.e., the directory's creation time is more recent than the resource's last modification time.) For cases where the resource modification time is more recent, see Extensions.ResourceLastModifiedDelta instead.

histogram.name: Extensions.RuntimeHostPermissions.ExtensionHasWithheldHosts
histogram.summary: Whether an extension had host permissions withheld as a result of the RuntimeHostPermissions feature. Recorded once per extension at profile initialization if and only if the RuntimeHostPermissions feature is enabled and the extension requests any host permissions (i.e., could be affected by the feature).

histogram.name: Extensions.RuntimeHostPermissions.GrantedHostCount
histogram.summary: The number of hosts an extension has been granted explicit access to that it also requested (note that if the user granted unrequested hosts, those will not be included in this count). Recorded once per extension at profile initialization if and only if the RuntimeHostPermissions feature is enabled and the extension has had host permissions withheld. See also Extensions.RuntimeHostPermissions.ExtensionHasWithheldHosts.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackFailure
histogram.summary: Count the number of times a sandboxed extension unpack fails.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackFailureReason
histogram.summary: What caused a sandboxed extension unpack to fail?

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackFailureTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to unpack an extension, when the unpack fails.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackHashCheck
histogram.summary: Whether a CRX file hash sum was the same as in an updater manifest.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackInitialCrxPathLength
histogram.summary: Length of the initial path to the CRX to be unpacked.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackLinkFreeCrxPathLength
histogram.summary: Length of the normalized (link/junction free) path to the temporary copy of a CRX made during unpacking.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackRate
histogram.summary: Rate at which a CRX file is unpacked in Kilobytes per second.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackRate1To2mB
histogram.summary: Rate at which CRX files 1MB to 2MB are unpacked in Kilobytes per second.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackRate2To5mB
histogram.summary: Rate at which CRX files 2MB to 5MB are unpacked in Kilobytes per second.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackRate50kBTo1mB
histogram.summary: Rate at which CRX files 50kB to 1MB are unpacked in Kilobytes per second.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackRate5To10mB
histogram.summary: Rate at which CRX files 5MB to 10 MB are unpacked in Kilobytes per second.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackRateOver10mB
histogram.summary: Rate at which CRX files larger than 10MB are unpacked in Kilobytes per second.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackRateUnder50kB
histogram.summary: Rate at which CRX files under 50 KB are unpacked in Kilobytes per second.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackSuccess
histogram.summary: Count the number of times a sandboxed CRX unpack succeeds.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackSuccessCantGetCrxSize
histogram.summary: Count the number of times a sandboxed CRX unpack succeeds, but we can't get the file size.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackSuccessCrxSize
histogram.summary: Size of the .crx file, in KB, when the unpack succeeds.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackSuccessTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to unpack an extension, when the unpack succeeds.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackTempCrxPathLength
histogram.summary: Length of the path of the temporary copy of a CRX made during unpacking.

histogram.name: Extensions.SandboxUnpackUnpackedCrxPathLength
histogram.summary: Length of the path under which a CRX is unpacked.

histogram.name: Extensions.SettingsQuotaExceeded.BytesPerSetting
histogram.summary: Emitted when QuotaExceededError was called due to an extension using too many bytes for a given setting. The number has no natural numerator (normalizing factor) and thus is not particularly useful.

histogram.name: Extensions.SettingsQuotaExceeded.KeyCount
histogram.summary: Emitted when QuotaExceededError was called due to an extension using too many keys. The number has no natural numerator (normalizing factor) and thus is not particularly useful.

histogram.name: Extensions.SettingsQuotaExceeded.TotalBytes
histogram.summary: Emitted when QuotaExceededError was called due to an extension using too many bytes. The number has no natural numerator (normalizing factor) and thus is not particularly useful.

histogram.name: Extensions.ShouldAllowOpenURL.Failure
histogram.summary: When the web-accessible resource check in ChromeContentBrowserClientExtensionsPart::ShouldAllowOpenURL fails, this records the reason for the failure. This check is performed on navigations that utilize the OpenURL path as well as on transfers.

histogram.name: Extensions.ShouldAllowOpenURL.Failure.Scheme
histogram.summary: When the web-accessible resource check in ChromeContentBrowserClientExtensionsPart::ShouldAllowOpenURL fails, this records the scheme of the SiteInstance that initiated the blocked load. This check is performed on navigations that utilize the OpenURL path as well as on transfers.

histogram.name: Extensions.StartupDelay
histogram.obsolete: This has not been recorded since at least mid-2013.
histogram.summary: The time one extension delays network requests at startup.

histogram.name: Extensions.StartupDelay_Total
histogram.obsolete: This has not been recorded since at least mid-2013.
histogram.summary: The total time extensions delay network requests at startup.

histogram.name: Extensions.StorageFrontendInitTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during StorageFrontend::Init.

histogram.name: Extensions.SyncGetMessageBundle
histogram.summary: The amount of synchronous time spent retrieving an extension's message bundle (the collection of its i18n resources) from the browser.

histogram.name: Extensions.ThrottledNetworkRequestDelay
histogram.summary: The duration by which a network request was delayed while waiting for extension user script load. Recorded once per request that is delayed, at the time the resource resumes loading.

histogram.name: Extensions.ThrottledNetworkRequests
histogram.summary: The total number of network requests that were delayed by extension user script load. Recorded once each time network requests may have been delayed by user script load (i.e., once at the end of each batch of script loads).

histogram.name: Extensions.TimeYieldedBetweenContentScriptRuns
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between two asynchronously-injected content script runs.

histogram.name: Extensions.ToolstripLoadTime
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time taken to load a toolstrip.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnifiedExtensionUpdaterDownloadErrors
histogram.summary: Records the error codes of the unified extension updater download errors. These events are triggered when the extension updater fails to download the extension update package of an extension, which can happen only after the updater has successfuly determined that there is an update for the extension.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnifiedExtensionUpdaterUpdateCalls
histogram.summary: The number of extensions that are passed over to the unified extension updater for update check. Triggered when the new extension updater starts doing update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnifiedExtensionUpdaterUpdateCheckErrors
histogram.summary: Records the error codes of the unified extension updater update check errors. These events are triggered only when the new extension updater gets an error in the update check phase.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnifiedExtensionUpdaterUpdateResults
histogram.summary: Records the update results of extensions in an unified extension updater session, grouped by ExtensionUpdaterUpdateResult.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnifiedExtensionUpdaterUpdateServiceErrors
histogram.summary: The update service errors that the update service encouters during an update check session using update client.

histogram.name: Extensions.UninstallDialogAction
histogram.summary: The action that was taken from an extension uninstall dialog. Recorded once per dialog shown. Only recorded if all possible actions (including report abuse) were available on the dialog.

histogram.name: Extensions.UninstallSource
histogram.summary: The source for the extension uninstall. This is only recorded if an uninstall dialog is shown to the user (i.e., it is not recorded for internal "cleanups").

histogram.name: Extensions.UninstallType
histogram.summary: Uninstalls grouped by Extension::HistogramType.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnpackFailureInstallCause
histogram.summary: Count failing CRX installs, grouped by the way an extension can be installed.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnpackFailureInstallSource
histogram.summary: Count successful CRX installs, grouped by the location property in prefs. installed.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnpackSuccessInstallCause
histogram.summary: Count successful CRX installs, grouped by the cause of the install.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnpackSuccessInstallSource
histogram.summary: Count successful CRX installs, grouped by the location property in prefs.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckApp
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of legacy packaged apps and hosted apps that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckExtension
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of extensions that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckGap
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time in minutes between update checks.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckGoogleUrl
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of crx's with a Google-hosted update URL that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckNoUrl
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of crx's with no update URL checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckOtherUrl
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of crx's with a non-Google update URL that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckPackagedApp
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of packaged apps that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckTheme
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of themes that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateFrequencyCommandLineFlagIsUsed
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 3/2018 in https://crbug.com/357781 with the removal of the extensions-update-frequency command line flag.
histogram.summary: Logs whether or not the extensions-update-frequency command line flag is used. Recorded at profile creation if extensions autoupdate is enabled.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateManifestDuplicateEntryCount
histogram.summary: Counts the number of entries with the same extension ID found in the update manifest of an update check request. When the update manifest does not contain any update entry for an extension ID in the update check request, the count will be 0.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateManifestHasProdVersionMinCounts
histogram.summary: The number of prodversionmin attributes appearing in an update manifest of an update check request.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateOnLoad
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of extensions that were updated at browser startup.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdaterWriteCrx
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: What happened when the extension updater tried to write a file?

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateSource
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Updates grouped by the location property in prefs.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateType
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Updates grouped by Extension::HistogramType.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.InitiatorAccess
histogram.summary: Describes the different cases pertaining to host permissions check for the initiator URL of a network request. This is emitted whenever an extension is found to have access to a request url, when checked by the WebRequest API.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.ModifiedResponseHeaders
histogram.summary:
Tracks whether any interesting response header modifications were made for each request seen by the Web Request API. Logged for every network request as long as one or more enabled extensions is using a declarative or blocking filter to process onHeadersReceived events.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M69.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.OnBeforeSendHeadersEventResponse
histogram.summary:
For every network request visible to the Web Request API, this indicates whether any registered declarative rules or event listeners observed the onBeforeSendHeaders event.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M69.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.OnHeadersReceivedEventResponse
histogram.summary:
For every network request visible to the Web Request API, this indicates whether any registered declarative rules or event listeners observed the onHeadersReceived event.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M69.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.SpecialHeadersRemoved
histogram.summary:
Whether either or both of the special Accept-Language or User-Agent request headers were removed by an extension using the Web Request API. Logged for every network request as long as one or more enabled extensions is using a declarative or blocking filter to process onBeforeSendHeaders events.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M69.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.WS_CookiesAreModifiedOnBeforeSendHeaders
histogram.summary:
Whether cookies on a websocket connection are modified in OnBeforeSendHeaders.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.WS_CookiesAreModifiedOnHeadersReceived
histogram.summary:
Whether cookies on a websocket connection are modified in OnHeadersReceived.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.WS_RequestHeaders
histogram.summary: Whether certain headers are modified in OnBeforeSendHeaders.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.WS_RequestHeadersModification
histogram.summary:
Tracks request headers modifications on a websocket connection in OnBeforeSendHeaders.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.WS_ResponseHeadersAreModified
histogram.summary:
Whether response headers on a websocket connection are modified in OnHeadersReceived.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequestAction
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times an action is requested by extensions as part of the Web Request API. For a given web request stage, multiple actions may be logged.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequestBlockingCount
histogram.summary: Number of enabled extensions which have requested the "webRequestBlocking" permission. Includes all kinds of extensions, including component and unpacked extensions. Recorded during profile initialization.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequestEventFoundFrame
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 11/2017. No longer relevant after refactoring.
histogram.summary: Whether or not the render frame lookup for a given webRequest event succeeded.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebstoreDownload.FileDownload
histogram.summary: The time spent to download the crx file from the webstore to local disk. This is recorded once per successful download.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebstoreDownload.InterruptReason
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The reason a webstore download was interrupted.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebstoreDownload.InterruptReceivedKBytes
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of KBytes received for a webstore download before it was interrupted.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebstoreDownload.InterruptTotalKBytes
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The total expected size in KBytes of an interrupted webstore download.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebstoreDownload.InterruptTotalSizeUnknown
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the total size of an interrupted webstore download was known.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebUi.DocumentLoadedInMainFrameTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time between starting the provisional load and fully loading the document in the main frame of the chrome://extensions page. This corresponds to the WebContentsObserver::DocumentLoadedInFrame method.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebUi.LoadCompletedInMainFrame
histogram.summary: The amount of time between starting the provisional load and having completed the onload handler in the main frame of the chrome://extensions page. This corresponds to the WebContentsObserver::DocumentOnLoadCompletedInMainFrame method.

histogram.name: ExtensionService.AddVerified
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records whether adding a new/updated extension to the install verifier succeeded.

histogram.name: ExtensionService.VerifyAllSuccess
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The outcome of a call to ExtensionService::VerifyAllExtensions, whether it was called for bootstrapping or another reason (extension installed/uninstalled, etc.).

histogram.name: ExtensionService.ZipUnpackerDisabledReason
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 09/2018, with the removal of the "Zip Unpacker" component extension.
histogram.summary: Records the reason why the ZIP unpacker extension was disabled when the service attempted to re-enable the extension upon startup. Each entry indicates that the extension was disabled unintentionally and a fix was attempted.

histogram.name: ExtensionSettings.ShouldDoVerificationCheck
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: When loading the chrome://extensions page, this records whether we decided to do a verification check against the server (because the user had one or more extensions disabled due to verification failure).

histogram.name: ExtensionToolbarModel.BrowserActionsCount
histogram.summary: The number of icons the Browser Actions Container knows about (visible or in the overflow bucket). Does not count icons that have been permanently hidden by the user. Measured once per startup per (non-incognito) profile.

histogram.name: ExtensionToolbarModel.BrowserActionsPermanentlyHidden
histogram.summary: The number of Browser Action icons the user has elected to permanently hide (as opposed to putting them in the overflow bucket). Measured once per startup per (non-incognito) profile.

histogram.name: ExtensionToolbarModel.BrowserActionsVisible
histogram.summary: The number of visible icons in the Browser Actions Container (visible as in number of icons not in the overflow bucket). 0 means all icons are in the overflow bucket. MAX_INT means the toolbar is always showing all icons. Measured once per startup per (non-incognito) profile but only for those profiles that have one or more browser actions showing in the toolbar.

histogram.name: ExtensionUrlRequest.Latency
histogram.summary: The time taken to complete an extension url request.

histogram.name: ExtensionUrlRequest.OnReadCompleteError
histogram.summary: The error code for failures of incremental reads of a file stream for a chrome-extension:// URL. (See also ExtensionUrlRequest.OnReadCompleteResult for the success case).

histogram.name: ExtensionUrlRequest.OnReadCompleteResult
histogram.summary: The result of an incremental read of a file stream for a chrome-extension:// URL, representing a byte count. Logged in success cases (see also ExtensionUrlRequest.OnReadCompleteError).

histogram.name: ExtensionUrlRequest.SeekPosition
histogram.summary: When fetching a chrome-extension:// URL, this indicates the first byte position we read from. This will be greater than 0 in cases such as XHR's with a Range header, but will normally be 0 in the typical case of reading the entire file. This helps identify how frequently partial file reads are taking place.

histogram.name: ExtensionUrlRequest.TotalKbRead
histogram.summary: The total number of bytes read for a chrome-extension:// URL, logged when the job is finished (either successfully or not).

histogram.name: FactoryPingEmbargo.EndDateValidity
histogram.summary: Record the validity of |rlz_embargo_end_date| in RW_VPD. This is recorded when the VPD value is accessed, which may be multiple times in the same chrome invocation. This is not recorded when the VPD value is missing.

histogram.name: FaultTolerantHeap
histogram.summary: Records several bits of information about whether the Windows FaultTolerantHeap might be enabled. This is logged (from a delayed task) when the browser process starts up.

histogram.name: Favicons.CandidatesCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of favicon link tags on a page once the page has finished loading. The count includes the automatically added favicon.ico entry.

histogram.name: Favicons.CandidatesWithDefinedSizesCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of favicon link tags with a non empty sizes attribute once the page has finished loading.

histogram.name: Favicons.CandidatesWithTouchIconsCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of apple-touch-icon and apple-touch-icon-precomposed link tags once the page has finished loading.

histogram.name: Favicons.DownloadAttempts
histogram.summary: Records the number of icons requested until the best-fitting candidate was found or there were no candidates left to check. More than 15 attempts are unlikely and will be stored in overflow bucket 16. Less than 1 attempt means an error happened; these cases are in bucket 0.

histogram.name: Favicons.DownloadOutcome
histogram.summary: Records whether a download succeeded, failed or was skipped because it has failed previously.

histogram.name: Favicons.LargeIconService.BlacklistedURLMismatch
histogram.summary: Records the number of large icons that were fetched from the local cache (e.g. during NTP impression), where the URL (of the page or the icon) matches a hardcoded blacklist. Suspicious mismatches are reported as errors.

histogram.name: Favicons.LargeIconService.DownloadedSize
histogram.summary: Records the size (concretely the width) in pixel of the favicon downloaded from Google favicon server (size 0 denotes that download has failed).

histogram.name: Favicons.LargeIconService.FallbackSize
histogram.summary: Records the size (concretely the width) in pixel of the favicon that is used to generate a fallback style in the case when large enough favicon is not available (size 0 denotes that no favicon is available at all).

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.FCMMessageStatus
histogram.summary: Status of the message from the FCM channel. Recorded upon receiving response from the FCM channel.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.SubscriptionRequestStatus
histogram.summary: Status of subscription request to the Per User Topic server. Recorded upon receiving response from server.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.UnsubscriptionRequestStatus
histogram.summary: Status of unsubscription request to the Per User Topic server. Recorded upon receiving response from server.

histogram.name: Feed.Offline.GetStatusCount
histogram.summary: The number of urls that have offline status requested per call.

histogram.name: Feed.Offline.GetStatusDuration
histogram.summary: The number of milliseconds to round trip offline status for a set of URLs from Offline Pages component.

histogram.name: Feed.Scheduler.RefreshTrigger
histogram.summary: The scheduler watches for various triggers, which cause it to decide if a refresh is currently warranted or not. This histogram is emitted when a trigger causes a refresh.

histogram.name: Feed.Scheduler.RequestBehavior
histogram.summary: When NTP is opened, the scheduler host tells the Feed library how to act, if the existing content should be shown, immediately or with timeout, and if a refresh request should be started.

histogram.name: Feedback.RequestSource
histogram.summary: Records the source that requested showing the feedback app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ChangeDirectory.RootType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: Counts the number of directory-changed events, bucketed by the RootType of the directory newly displayed.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.CloudImport.UserAction
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser - Specific actions taken by user such initiating cloud import, canceling import, selecting a directory, or opting-in to Drive>Photos sync.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ComputersCount
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: number of Computers a user has available in the Files app. Computed every time the File Browser is opened (including file picker dialogs). NOTE: This data is biased towards users that use the Files App more often.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Create
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser opening mode.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.CrostiniShareDialog
histogram.summary: Dialog shown when using crostini app to open a file.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.CrostiniSharedPaths.Depth
histogram.summary: The depth from volume root of the path being shared. This is equivalent to how many '/' are in the path. E.g. 'Downloads' or 'My Drive' has depth 0. 'Downloads/foo/bar' has depth 2.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.DirectoryScan
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: time to scan a directory. Measured on every File Browser directory change.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.DownloadDestination.IsGoogleDrive.Changed
histogram.summary: Tracks whether download destination is set to a Google Drive folder when the download destination is changed by the user in the settings page.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.DownloadDestination.IsGoogleDrive.Started
histogram.summary: Tracks whether download destination is set to a Google Drive folder on startup.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.DownloadsCount
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: number of files and directories in the Downloads directory (not including the contents of nested directories). Computed every time the File Browser current directory changes to Downloads.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.FolderShortcut.Add
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: this is recorded when the user adds a folder shortcut.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.FolderShortcut.Count
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: number of saved folder shorcuts. This is recorded when the Files app is launched.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.FolderShortcut.Navigate
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: this is recorded when the user clicks or selects a folder shortcut and is navigated to the target folder.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.FolderShortcut.Remove
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: this is recorded when the user removes a folder shortcut.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.HardUnpluggedAroundSuspend.TimeSinceResume
histogram.obsolete: The bug which the UMA was investigating got fixed.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: time from the SuspendDone event to the DiskRemoved event. The UMA is added temporarily for crbug.com/433734.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.HardUnpluggedAroundSuspend.TimeUntilSuspend
histogram.obsolete: The bug which the UMA was investigating got fixed.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: time from the DiskRemoved event to the Suspend event. The UMA is added temporarily for crbug.com/433734.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Load
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser is an built-in extension without a background page. Its main.html file is loaded every time the user opens a File Browser tab or a file chooser dialog. The file is fairly large and the initialization is pretty expensive.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Location.OnEntryExpandedOrCollapsed.NonTopLevel
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: The locations (root types) of non-top-level entries when they are expanded or collapsed (expand icon clicked) in the directory tree.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Location.OnEntryExpandedOrCollapsed.TopLevel
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: The locations (root types) of top-level entries (root entries) when they are expanded or collapsed (expand icon clicked) in the directory tree.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Location.OnEntrySelected.NonTopLevel
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: The locations (root types) of non-top-level entries when they are clicked in the directory tree.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Location.OnEntrySelected.TopLevel
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: The locations (root types) of top-level entries (root entries) when they are clicked in the directory tree.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.MenuItemSelected
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: The commands selected in the menu by the files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.MountCrostiniContainer
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: time to start and mount the crostini container in order to show Linux Files.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.OpenFiles.RootType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: The locations (root types) of files which are opened by the file picker.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.OpeningFileType
histogram.summary: File types that were tried to be opened through browser.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.DisplayTime
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: time to display an image. Measured from the moment the user selected the image till the moment it is displayed (not counting the low resolution preview).

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.FileType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: the type of the file opened.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.LoadMode
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: the way the image has been loaded.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.LoadTime
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: time to load an image from a file.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.SaveResult
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: the result of a file save operation.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.SaveTime
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: time to save an image to a file.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.Size.MB
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: size of an image file in megabytes. Measured on every image load.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.Size.MPix
