histogram.name: Net.Compress.Proxy.BytesBeforeCompression
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The compressed number of bytes received per request that was compressed. Only includes requests sent through a proxy without SSL.

histogram.name: Net.Compress.Proxy.ShouldHaveBeenCompressed
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The uncompressed number of bytes received per request that was not compressed but appears to have been compressible. Only includes requests sent through a proxy without SSL.

histogram.name: Net.Compress.SSL.BytesAfterCompression
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The uncompressed number of bytes received per request that was compressed. Only includes requests sent over SSL.

histogram.name: Net.Compress.SSL.BytesBeforeCompression
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The compressed number of bytes received per request that was compressed. Only includes requests sent over SSL.

histogram.name: Net.Compress.SSL.ShouldHaveBeenCompressed
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The uncompressed number of bytes received per request that was not compressed but appears to have been compressible. Only includes requests sent over SSL.

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionInfo.MainFrame
histogram.summary: Application protocol used for main frame resources. Logged by the renderer only for request that accessed the network and have received response headers.

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionInfo.SubResource
histogram.summary: Application protocol used for subresources (resources other than main frame). Logged by the renderer only for request that accessed the network and have received response headers.

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionTypeCount
histogram.obsolete: The count was inaccurate (it counted transactions rather than connections)
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Each bucket is the number of connections of a particular type that the user has had during the session.

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionTypeCount2
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Each bucket is the number of successful connections of a particular type that the user has had during the session.

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionTypeCount3
histogram.summary: Each bucket is the number of successful connections of a particular type that the user has had during the session.

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionTypeFailCount2
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Each bucket is the number of failed connections of a particular type that the user has had during the session.

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionUsedSSLDeprecatedCipherFallback
histogram.summary: True if an HTTPS connection was made using the deprecated cipher suite fallback.

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionUsedSSLDeprecatedCipherFallback2
histogram.summary: For each successful HTTPS request, whether it used the deprecated cipher suite fallback.

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionUsedSSLv3Fallback
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: True if the HTTP request was to a server which requires SSLv3 fallback

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionUsedSSLVersionFallback
histogram.summary: Nonzero if the HTTP request was to a server which requires SSL version fallback. The value indicates the SSL version the request fell back on.

histogram.name: Net.ConnectionUsedSSLVersionFallback2
histogram.summary: For each successful HTTPS request, whether it used the SSL version fallback. The value indicates the SSL version the request fell back on

histogram.name: Net.ContentDecodingFailed.FilterType
histogram.obsolete: Obsoleted in favor of Net.ContentDecodingFailed2.FilterType below.
histogram.summary: For each CONTENT_DECODING_FAILED, record the filter that failed.

histogram.name: Net.ContentDecodingFailed2
histogram.summary: For each CONTENT_DECODING_FAILED, record the filter that failed.

histogram.name: Net.ContentDecodingFailed2.FilterType
histogram.obsolete: Obsoleted in favor of Net.ContentDecodingFailed2 above.
histogram.summary: For each CONTENT_DECODING_FAILED, record the filter that failed.

histogram.name: net.CookieBackingStoreUpdateResults
histogram.obsolete: Initial typo; only here to get results from builds before r59117. See "Cookie." group.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Whether or not updates to the backing store succeeded or failed, recorded every update.

histogram.name: net.CookieBetweenAccessIntervalMinutes
histogram.obsolete: Initial typo; only here to get results from builds before r59117. See "Cookie." group.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Intervals between access time updates for each cookie.

histogram.name: net.CookieCount
histogram.obsolete: Initial typo; only here to get results from builds before r59117. See "Cookie." group.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of cookies in the store (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time)

histogram.name: net.CookieDeletionCause
histogram.obsolete: Initial typo; only here to get results from builds before r59117. See "Cookie." group.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For each cookie removed from the store, the reason it was removed.

histogram.name: net.CookieDomainCount
histogram.obsolete: Initial typo; only here to get results from builds before r59117. See "Cookie." group.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For each domain, number of cookies in that domain (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time).

histogram.name: net.CookieDomainPerEtldp1Count
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For every top level domain, number of subdomains in that top level domain (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time).

histogram.name: net.CookieEtldp1Count
histogram.obsolete: Initial typo; only here to get results from builds before r59117. See "Cookie." group.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For every top level domain, number of cookies in that domain (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time).

histogram.name: net.CookieEvictedLastAccessMinutes
histogram.obsolete: Initial typo; only here to get results from builds before r59117. See "Cookie." group.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For each evicted (not expired) cookie, the amount of time since it was last used

histogram.name: net.CookieExpirationDurationMinutes
histogram.obsolete: Initial typo; only here to get results from builds before r59117. See "Cookie." group.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of minutes until cookie expires when set.

histogram.name: net.CookieTimeGet
histogram.obsolete: Initial typo; only here to get results from builds before r59117. See "Cookie." group.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to get cookies for each URL request.

histogram.name: net.CookieTimeLoad
histogram.obsolete: Initial typo; only here to get results from builds before r59117. See "Cookie." group.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to load the persistent cookie store at browser start.

histogram.name: Net.CountOfAlternateProtocolServers
histogram.summary: The total number of servers (HostPortPairs) to which alternative protocol was used. This counts the number of servers persisted to prefs file.

histogram.name: Net.CountOfAlternateProtocolServers.Memory
histogram.summary: The total number of servers (HostPortPairs in memory) to which alternative protocol was used.

histogram.name: Net.CountOfBrokenAlternativeServices
histogram.summary: The number of broken alternative services loaded from the prefs file on startup. Alternative services allow an origin server to specify additional means of interacting with it on the network. A broken alternative service is one whose most recent connection attempt failed and whose retry timeout period has not yet expired. Recorded when broken alternative services are loaded from the prefs file on startup.

histogram.name: Net.CountOfPipelineCapableServers
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 05/2014, related field trial already long expired.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The total number of servers (HostPortPairs) that support HTTP pipelining. This counts the number of servers persisted to prefs file.

histogram.name: Net.CountOfQuicServerInfos
histogram.summary: This counts the number of server configs persisted in prefs file.

histogram.name: Net.CountOfRecentlyBrokenAlternativeServices
histogram.summary: The number of recently broken alternative services loaded from the prefs file on startup. Alternative services allow an origin server to specify additional means of interacting with it on the network. A recently broken alternative service is one whose most recent connection attempt failed. Recorded when broken alternative services are loaded from the prefs file on startup.

histogram.name: Net.CountOfSpdyServers
histogram.summary: The total number of SPDY server names persisted to prefs file.

histogram.name: Net.CountOfSpdySettings
histogram.summary: The total number of SPDY Settings properties persisted to prefs file.

histogram.name: Net.CRLRequestFailedTimeMs
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2017-04-21 as it was Linux/CrOS only.
histogram.summary: When validating an HTTPS certificate we may have to block to fetch one or more revocation lists. This measures the amount of time that failures to get CRL information take.

histogram.name: Net.CRLRequestSuccess
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2017-04-21 as it was Linux/CrOS only.
histogram.summary: When validating an HTTPS certificate we may have to block to fetch one or more revocation lists. This records the fraction of successful requests.

histogram.name: Net.CRLRequestTimeMs
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2017-04-21 as it was Linux/CrOS only.
histogram.summary: When validating an HTTPS certificate we may have to block to fetch one or more revocation lists. This measures the amount of time that each fetch takes.

histogram.name: Net.Cronet.CertVerifierCache.DeserializeTime
histogram.summary: Measures time spent to deserialize and populate the net::CachingCertVerifier's cache.

histogram.name: Net.Cronet.CertVerifierCache.SerializeTime
histogram.summary: Measures time spent to serialize the net::CachingCertVerifier's cache.

histogram.name: Net.Cronet.PrefsInitTime
histogram.summary: Measures time spent creating the Cronet PrefService, including loading the prefs from disk synchronously.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentLength
histogram.summary: The total content size in KB of all HTTP/HTTPS response bodies in the previous calendar day. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentLength_DataReductionProxyEnabled
histogram.summary: The total content size in KB of all HTTP/HTTPS response bodies in the previous calendar day while the data reduction proxy setting was enabled. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentLength_DataReductionProxyEnabled_Https
histogram.summary: The total content size in KB of all HTTPS response bodies in the previous calendar day while the data reduction proxy setting was enabled. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentLength_DataReductionProxyEnabled_LongBypass
histogram.summary: The total content size in KB of all long-bypassed HTTP response bodies in the previous calendar day while the data reduction proxy setting was enabled. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentLength_DataReductionProxyEnabled_ShortBypass
histogram.summary: The total content size in KB of all short-bypassed HTTP response bodies in the previous calendar day while the data reduction proxy setting was enabled. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentLength_DataReductionProxyEnabled_Unknown
histogram.summary: The total content size in KB of all HTTP response bodies for requests that were not served by the enabled data reduction proxy for unknown reasons in the previous calendar day while the data reduction proxy setting was enabled. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentLength_DataReductionProxyEnabled_UnknownBypass
histogram.summary: The total content size in KB of all HTTP response bodies for requests that were not served by the enabled data reduction proxy for unknown reasons in the previous calendar day while the data reduction proxy setting was enabled. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentLength_ViaDataReductionProxy
histogram.summary: The total content size in KB of all HTTP/HTTPS response bodies in the previous calendar day via the data reduction proxy. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentPercent_DataReductionProxyEnabled
histogram.summary: The percentage of total HTTP/HTTPS response body size while the data reduction proxy is enabled to total HTTP/HTTPS response body size in the previous calendar day. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentPercent_DataReductionProxyEnabled_Https
histogram.summary: The percentage of total HTTPS response body size while the data reduction proxy is enabled to total HTTP/HTTPS response body size in the previous calendar day. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentPercent_DataReductionProxyEnabled_LongBypass
histogram.summary: The percentage of total long-bypassed response body size while the data reduction proxy is enabled to total HTTP/HTTPS response body size in the previous calendar day. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentPercent_DataReductionProxyEnabled_ShortBypass
histogram.summary: The percentage of total short-bypassed response body size while the data reduction proxy is enabled to total HTTP/HTTPS response body size in the previous calendar day. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentPercent_DataReductionProxyEnabled_Unknown
histogram.summary: The percentage of total body size of responses that were not served by the data reduction proxy for unknown reason while the data reduction proxy is enabled to total HTTP/HTTPS response body size in the previous calendar day. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentPercent_ViaDataReductionProxy
histogram.summary: The percentage of total HTTP/HTTPS response body size via the data reduction proxy to total HTTP/HTTPS response body size in the previous calendar day. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentSavingPercent
histogram.summary: The percentage of data saving in the previous calendar day. A negative saving will be shown as zero. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentSavingPercent_DataReductionProxyEnabled
histogram.summary: The percentage of data saving in the previous calendar day while the data reduction proxy was enabled. A negative saving will be shown as zero. This only counts responses while the data reduction proxy is enabled. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentSavingPercent_DataReductionProxyEnabled_Video
histogram.summary: The percentage of data saving in the previous calendar day while the data reduction proxy was enabled. A negative saving will be shown as zero. This only counts responses while the data reduction proxy is enabled. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received. If no video bytes were received while the data reduction proxy was enabled, the metric will not be reported.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentSavingPercent_ViaDataReductionProxy
histogram.summary: The percentage of data saving in the previous calendar day via the data reduction proxy. A negative saving will be shown as zero. This only counts responses via the data reduction proxy. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyContentSavingPercent_ViaDataReductionProxy_Video
histogram.summary: The percentage of data saving in the previous calendar day via the data reduction proxy. A negative saving will be shown as zero. This only counts responses via the data reduction proxy. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received. If no video bytes were received via the data reduction proxy, the metric will not be reported.

histogram.name: Net.DailyHttpContentLengthViaDataReductionProxy
histogram.summary: Total size in KB of all response bodies in the previous calendar day that were received through the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: Net.DailyHttpContentLengthWithDataReductionProxyEnabled
histogram.summary: Total size in KB of all response bodies in the previous calendar day that were received when the data reduction proxy was enabled.

histogram.name: Net.DailyHttpContentSavings
histogram.summary: The percentage of data saving in the previous calendar day. A negative saving will be shown as zero.

histogram.name: Net.DailyHttpContentSavings_DataReductionProxy
histogram.summary: The percentage of data saving in the previous calendar day when the data reduction proxy was enabled for at least some responses during the day. A negative saving will be shown as zero.

histogram.name: Net.DailyHttpOriginalContentLength
histogram.summary: Total size in KB specified in the X-Original-Content-Length headers of all responses in the previous calendar day. If the header is not present in a response, the size of the response body is used.

histogram.name: Net.DailyHttpReceivedContentLength
histogram.summary: Total size in KB of all response bodies in the previous calendar day.

histogram.name: Net.DailyOriginalContentLength
histogram.summary: The total size in KB specified in the X-Original-Content-Length headers of all HTTP/HTTPS response bodies in the previous calendar day. If the header is not present in a response, the size of the response body is used. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyOriginalContentLength_DataReductionProxyEnabled
histogram.summary: The total size in KB specified in the X-Original-Content-Length headers of all HTTP/HTTPS response bodies in the previous calendar day while the data reduction proxy is enabled. If the header is not present in a response, the size of the response body is used. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyOriginalContentLength_ViaDataReductionProxy
histogram.summary: The total size in KB specified in the X-Original-Content-Length headers of all HTTP/HTTPS response bodies in the previous calendar day via the data reduction proxy. If the header is not present in a response, the size of the response body is used. The metric is reported when the first response in the current day is received.

histogram.name: Net.DailyReceivedContentViaDataReductionProxy
histogram.summary: The percentage of Net.DailyHttpContentLengthViaDataReductionProxy in Net.DailyHttpReceivedContentLength.

histogram.name: Net.DailyReceivedContentWithDataReductionProxyEnabled
histogram.summary: The percentage of Net.DailyHttpContentLengthWithDataReductionProxyEnabled in Net.DailyHttpReceivedContentLength.

histogram.name: Net.DailyUserVisibleSavingsPercent_DataReductionProxyEnabled
histogram.summary: The percentage of data savings in past |DataReductionProxy::kNumDaysInHistorySummary| days. This number is displayed to users as their data savings.

histogram.name: Net.DailyUserVisibleSavingsSize_DataReductionProxyEnabled
histogram.summary: The total data saved in KB in past |DataReductionProxy::kNumDaysInHistorySummary| days. This number is used to compute the data savings displayed to the user.

histogram.name: Net.DetachableResourceHandler.Duration
histogram.summary: Duration of time that a request associated with DetachableResourceHandler takes.

histogram.name: Net.DhcpWpadCancelTime
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Measures time from initiating a fetch of a PAC file from DHCP WPAD to cancellation of the fetch. For a given fetch, only one of the cancellation or completion histograms will be added to.

histogram.name: Net.DhcpWpadCompletionTime
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Measures time from initiating a fetch of a PAC file from DHCP WPAD to completion of the fetch. For a given fetch, only one of the cancellation or completion histograms will be added to.

histogram.name: Net.DhcpWpadFetchError
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Tracks the net error codes received when the DHCP WPAD fetch fails to retrieve a PAC file (including PAC_NOT_IN_DHCP, which is not really an error but an indication that a PAC URL was not configured in DHCP).

histogram.name: Net.DhcpWpadGetAdaptersAddressesError
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Tracks the frequency of each of the different known error codes of calling the GetAdaptersAddresses Win32 API.

histogram.name: Net.DhcpWpadGetAdaptersAddressesTime
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Measures the time taken to call the GetAdaptersAddresses Win32 API, to validate our understanding that it should complete quickly enough to call synchronously from the network thread.

histogram.name: Net.DhcpWpadNumAdaptersAtWaitTimer
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Total number of adapters enabled for DHCP as seen when the wait timer in the DHCP WPAD code hits. This timer fires after a timeout from when we get some information from the first adapter to finish.

histogram.name: Net.DhcpWpadNumPendingAdaptersAtWaitTimer
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of adapters enabled for DHCP that we have not completed retrieving information for, as seen when the wait timer in the DHCP WPAD code hits. This timer fires after a timeout from when we get some information from the first adapter to finish.

histogram.name: Net.DhcpWpadUnhandledDhcpError
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Counts the number of errors from the DhcpRequestParams API that we do not have specific handling for, so that we can see if there is an abnormally high rate.

histogram.name: Net.DiskCache.Size
histogram.summary:
The sum of the size of all files in the HTTP cache directory.
This is recorded on startup, and then every 24 hours afterwards. However, it is only recorded for 0.1% of profiles, to reduce overhead for most users. This is meant to be a temporary metric to help debug a potential HTTP cache leak. We do not yet know which milestone we expect it to be removed in.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.DnsTask.ErrorBeforeFallback.Fast
histogram.summary: Counts of specific error codes returned by DnsTask. Only counts failures that took less than 10ms, which are probably local failures.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.DnsTask.ErrorBeforeFallback.Slow
histogram.summary: Counts of specific error codes returned by DnsTask. Only counts failures that took at least 10ms, which are probably remote failures or connectivity problems.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.DnsTask.Errors
histogram.summary: Counts of specific error codes returned by DnsTask if a subsequent ProcTask succeeded.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.DnsTask.FailureTime
histogram.summary: Duration of time taken by DnsTask in resolutions that failed. Excludes time spent in the subsequent fallback.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.DnsTask.SuccessTime
histogram.summary: Duration of time taken by DnsTask in resolutions that succeeded.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.JobQueueTime
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between the time the HostResolverImpl::Job was created and the time the Job was started.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.JobQueueTimeAfterChange
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between the last time the priority of a HostResolverImpl::Job changed (when a Request was attached or detached) and the time the Job was started.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.ProcTask.FailureTime
histogram.summary: Duration of time taken by ProcTask in resolutions that failed.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.ProcTask.SuccessTime
histogram.summary: Duration of time taken by ProcTask in resolutions that succeeded.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.ResolveCategory
histogram.summary: Whether a DNS resolution (single HostResolverImpl::Job) succeeded or failed, and whether it was speculative.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.ResolveError.Fast
histogram.summary: For DNS resolutions that failed after less than 10 ms, which are probably local failures, what error code the jobs failed with.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.ResolveError.Slow
histogram.summary: For DNS resolutions that failed after at least 10 ms, which are probably remote failures or connectivity problems, what error code the jobs failed with.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.ResolveFailureTime
histogram.summary: Duration of time taken by HostResolverImpl::Job in resolutions that failed. This is the time to resolve a hostname from start to finish. The main histogram and by-priority versions exclude speculative requests.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.ResolveSuccessTime
histogram.summary: Duration of time taken by HostResolverImpl::Job in resolutions that succeeded. This is the time to resolve a hostname from start to finish. The main histogram and by-priority versions exclude speculative requests.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.ResultAfterMalformedResponse
histogram.summary: When DnsUDPAttempt gets a malformed response, it returns an error but continues running. This histogram records all attempt outcomes that are returned to the transaction once a malformed response has been received, including the original malformed response.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.TotalTime
histogram.summary: Duration of time since a HostResolverImpl::Resolve request to the time a result is posted. Excludes canceled, evicted, and aborted requests. Includes cache hits (recorded as 0). Excludes speculative requests.

histogram.name: Net.DNS.TotalTimeNotCached
histogram.summary: Duration of time since a HostResolverImpl::Resolve request to the time a result is posted. Excludes canceled, evicted, and aborted requests and requests that returned synchronously (such as cache hits). Excludes speculative requests.

histogram.name: Net.DNS_Resolution_And_TCP_Connection_Latency
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.

histogram.name: Net.Dns_Resolution_And_TCP_Connection_Latency
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.

histogram.name: Net.DNS_Resolution_And_TCP_Connection_Latency2
histogram.summary: The time measured before starting DNS lookup until after the connection is complete.

histogram.name: Net.Dns_Resolution_And_TCP_Connection_Latency2
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.

histogram.name: Net.DNSNameCompliantIfValid
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 07/2017, not necessary to determine deprecation for invalid DNS names.
histogram.summary: True if |net::IsCanonicalizedHostCompliant| returns true. Used to see if IsCanonicalizedHostCompliant() runs afoul of real websites. This histogram is recorded when converting dotted DNS names into DNS query form, in preparation for issuing a DNS request. This histogram is only recorded if Net.ValidDNSName is true.

histogram.name: Net.DoubleGetExperiment_InitialResponseMethod
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of HTTP request responses with MS Office Docs MIME types. The responses are classified based on their method type and cacheability (POST, cacheable GET and non-cacheable GET). The histogram is used in Double GET Experiment, where successful non-cacheable GET requests are intercepted after initial response and repeated in order to determine how much reissuing non-cacheable GET requests influences their error rate. The histogram tracks only initial requests (not the repeated ones).

histogram.name: Net.DoubleGetExperiment_ResponseCode
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The response codes encountered for GET request repeated in Double GET Experiment. In the experiment successful non-cacheable GET requests are intercepted after initial response and repeated. The goal of the experiment is to measure how much reissuing non-cacheable GET requests influences their error rate.

histogram.name: Net.DownloadBandwidth
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2015. No longer generated.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Kbps on download streams exceeding 25KB. Measures from the beginning of the first byte received until the end of flowing data.

histogram.name: Net.ErrAborted.CountPerUpload
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated in favor of Net.ErrAborted.CountPerUpload2 which has a higher upper bound
histogram.summary: The count of the ABORTED bucket in Net.ErrorCodesForMainFrame3 at the time histograms are being uploaded.

histogram.name: Net.ErrAborted.CountPerUpload2
histogram.summary: The count of the ABORTED bucket in Net.ErrorCodesForMainFrame3 at the time histograms are being uploaded.

histogram.name: Net.ErrAborted.ProportionPerUpload
histogram.summary: The bucket proportion of the ABORTED bucket in Net.ErrorCodesForMainFrame3 at the time histograms are being uploaded.

histogram.name: Net.ErrAborted.ReceivedBytes
histogram.summary: The TotalReceivedBytes() at the time the request finishes with ERR_ABORTED.

histogram.name: Net.ErrAborted.SentBytes
histogram.summary: The TotalSentBytes() at the time the request finishes with ERR_ABORTED.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForHTTPSGoogleMainFrame
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 2012/5/16, replaced by Net.ErrorCodesForHTTPSGoogleMainFrame2, which measures the same data but includes ERR_ABORT and OK.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Errors resulting from loading pages from https://www.google.com. Note that this only counts the errors in "main frames", so it is a measure of the error pages that users actually see (it does not for example count the error codes for subresoures on a page). This is a temporary histogram, added in order to debug query loss from the SSL Search launch.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForHTTPSGoogleMainFrame2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 7/2018, replaced by Net.ErrorCodesForHTTPSGoogleMainFrame3.
histogram.summary: Positive net error codes that requests for pages end with, including net::OK and net::ERR_ABORTED. This only counts loads in "main frames" for https://www.google.com. Subresources or main frame navigations to other origins are not included.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForHTTPSGoogleMainFrame3
histogram.summary: Positive net error codes that requests for pages end with, including net::OK and net::ERR_ABORTED. This only counts loads in "main frames" for https://www.google.com (it does not for example count the error codes for subresoures on a page). It excludes main frame navigations that are converted to downloads (unless they fail / are aborted before it's known that they would be a download), and requests handled externally (like mailto links).

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForImages
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 7/2018, replaced by Net.ErrorCodesForImages2, which is recorded in a net-service friendly way.
histogram.summary: Net error codes that requests for images end with, including net::OK and net:ERR_ABORTED.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForImages2
histogram.summary: Net error codes that requests for images end with, including net::OK and net:ERR_ABORTED.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForMainFrame
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 2011/5/24, replaced by Net.ErrorCodesForMainFrame2, which measures the same data but uses a different bucket structure (adds guard buckets).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Positive net error code that a page failed with. Note that this only counts the errors in "main frames", so it is a measure of the error pages that users actually see (it does not for example count the error codes for subresoures on a page).

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForMainFrame2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 2012/5/16, replaced by Net.ErrorCodesForMainFrame3, which measures the same data but includes ERR_ABORTED and OK.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Positive net error code that a page failed with. Note that this only counts the errors in "main frames", so it is a measure of the error pages that users actually see (it does not for example count the error codes for subresoures on a page).

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForMainFrame3
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 7/2018, replaced by Net.ErrorCodesForMainFrame4, which is recorded in a net-service friendly way.
histogram.summary: Positive net error codes that requests for pages end with, including net::OK and net::ERR_ABORTED. This only counts loads in "main frames" (it does not for example count the error codes for subresoures on a page).

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForMainFrame4
histogram.summary: Positive net error codes that requests for pages end with, including net::OK and net::ERR_ABORTED. This only counts loads in "main frames" (it does not for example count the error codes for subresoures on a page). It excludes main frame navigations that are converted to downloads (unless they fail / are aborted before it's known that they would be a download), and requests handled externally (like mailto links).

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForSubresources
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 2012/5/16, replaced by Net.ErrorCodesForSubresources2, which measures the same data but includes ERR_ABORT and OK.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Positive net error code that a page failed with. Note that this only counts the errors in "subresources".

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForSubresources2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 7/2018, replaced by Net.ErrorCodesForSubresources3, which is recorded in a net-service friendly way.
histogram.summary: Net error codes that requests for "subresources" end with, including net::OK and net::ERR_ABORTED.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForSubresources3
histogram.summary: Net error codes that requests for "subresources" end with, including net::OK and net::ERR_ABORTED.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForTLS13ExperimentMainFrame
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 7/2018, replaced by Net.ErrorCodesForTLS13ExperimentMainFrame2, which is recorded in a net-service friendly way.
histogram.summary: Positive net error codes that requests for pages end with, including net::OK and net::ERR_ABORTED. This only counts loads in "main frames" for endpoints used in the initial TLS 1.3 deployment. Subresources or main frame navigations to other origins are not included.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorCodesForTLS13ExperimentMainFrame2
histogram.summary: Positive net error codes that requests for pages end with, including net::OK and net::ERR_ABORTED. This only counts loads in "main frames" for endpoints used in the initial TLS 1.3 deployment. Subresources or main frame navigations to other origins are not included.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorPageButtonPressedWhileInUnexpectedState
histogram.summary: Temporary histogram to investigate http://crbug.com/500556. Records which button on the network error page was pushed when either the URL Blink reports for the page is not the internal error page URL or the NetErrorHelper core has no error information for the current page (or both).

histogram.name: Net.ErrorPageButtonPressUnexpectedStates
histogram.summary: Temporary histogram to investigate http://crbug.com/500556. Records whether, when the handler for a button press on the network error page runs, which of the following are or are not true: The URL Blink reports for the page is not the internal error page URL or the NetErrorHelper core has no error information for the current page.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorPageCounts
histogram.summary: Counts of various events that can occur on the network error page. Note that this histogram enum buckets are not mutually exclusive and so a single error page load might report samples into multiple ones. Each bucket has descriptions that further explain the context of their samples.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorPageCounts.NavigationCorrectionLinksShown
histogram.summary: Counts of the type of navigation correction suggestions shown on the network error page. Multiple suggestions can be shown at the same time.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorPageCounts.NavigationCorrectionLinksUsed
histogram.summary: Usage of navigation correction suggestions shown on the network error page.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorPageCounts.SuggestionClicked
histogram.summary: Counts of the type of offline content suggestions clicked on the network error page. Reported upon click.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorPageCounts.SuggestionPresented
histogram.summary: Counts of the type of offline content suggestions shown on the network error page. Multiple suggestions can be shown at the same time. Reported when the suggestions are presented.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorResponseHasContentMainFrame
histogram.summary: The number of main frame 4xx/5xx responses that have content, to determine if it's worth hooking up an error page for those that don't. Intended to be removed from the binary soon.

histogram.name: Net.ErrorResponseHasContentNonMainFrame
histogram.summary: The number of non-main frame 4xx/5xx responses that have content, to determine if it's worth hooking up an error page for those that don't. Intended to be removed from the binary soon.

histogram.name: Net.ExpectCTHeader.ParseSuccess
histogram.summary: Sites can send an Expect-CT header to Chrome to indicate that they intend their sites to comply with Chrome's Certificate Transparency policy. This histogram is recorded whenever an Expect-CT header is processed on a connection. True indicates that the header parsed successfully, and false indicates that there was a parse failure.

histogram.name: Net.ExpectCTHeader.PolicyComplianceOnConnectionSetup
histogram.summary: Sites can send an Expect-CT header to Chrome to indicate that they intend their sites to comply with Chrome's Certificate Transparency policy. This histogram is recorded on connection setup, whenever Chrome connects to an Expect-CT site using a publicly trusted root. The histogram records the CT policy compliance status of the connection.

histogram.name: Net.ExpectCTHeader.PolicyComplianceOnHeaderProcessing
histogram.summary: Sites can send an Expect-CT header to Chrome to indicate that they intend their sites to comply with Chrome's Certificate Transparency policy. This histogram is recorded whenever an Expect-CT header is successfully parsed on a connection that chains to a publicly trusted root. The histogram records the CT policy compliance status of the connection on which the header was received.

histogram.name: Net.ExpectCTHeaderResult
histogram.summary: Sites can send an Expect-CT header to Chrome to indicate that they want a report to be sent when the connection does not comply with Certificate Transparency policy. This histogram is recorded whenever Chrome receives an Expect-CT header, and it records the result of processing the header, such as whether it was ignored due to a bad value.

histogram.name: Net.FileError_Flush
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code that a file Flush failed with. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileError_GetSize
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code that a file GetSize failed with. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileError_Open
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code that a file Open failed with. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileError_Read
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code that a file Read failed with. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileError_Seek
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code that a file Seek failed with. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileError_SetEof
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code that a file SetEof failed with. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileError_Write
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code that a file Write failed with. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileErrorRange_Flush
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code range that a file Flush failed with. Any value other than 0 indicates that we have received errors in a range outside of the one in which we recorded the specific errors in Net.FileError_Flush. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileErrorRange_GetSize
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code range that a file GetSize failed with. Any value other than 0 indicates that we have received errors in a range outside of the one in which we recorded the specific errors in Net.FileError_GetSize. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileErrorRange_Open
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code range that a file Open failed with. Any value other than 0 indicates that we have received errors in a range outside of the one in which we recorded the specific errors in Net.FileError_Open. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileErrorRange_Read
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code range that a file Read failed with. Any value other than 0 indicates that we have received errors in a range outside of the one in which we recorded the specific errors in Net.FileError_Read. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileErrorRange_Seek
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code range that a file Seek failed with. Any value other than 0 indicates that we have received errors in a range outside of the one in which we recorded the specific errors in Net.FileError_Seek. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileErrorRange_SetEof
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code range that a file SetEof failed with. Any value other than 0 indicates that we have received errors in a range outside of the one in which we recorded the specific errors in Net.FileError_SetEof. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.FileErrorRange_Write
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: System error code range that a file Write failed with. Any value other than 0 indicates that we have received errors in a range outside of the one in which we recorded the specific errors in Net.FileError_Write. The code is OS dependent, so when looking at the histogram don't mix OSes.

histogram.name: Net.ForceAlternativeService
histogram.obsolete: Data collection ended, corresponding feature removed.
histogram.summary: Track usage of alternative services forced by the force-alt-protocols command line flag. Triggered each time alternative services are queried for a given origin and the command line flag would make a difference (that is, there is no advertised alternative service for that origin).

histogram.name: Net.FoundSystemTrustRootsAndroid
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Whether or not system installed trust anchors could be distinguished from user installed trust anchors. Recorded on first certificate verification on Android 4.2 and later.

histogram.name: Net.FtpDataConnectionErrorCount
histogram.summary: The number of times each FTP Error was observed.

histogram.name: Net.FtpDataConnectionErrorHappened
histogram.summary: The number of Chrome sessions which encountered the indicates FTP Error. This prevents allowing a user that retried a connection many times (getting an error each time) from biasing the tallies.

histogram.name: Net.FtpServerTypeCount
histogram.summary: Each bucket is the number of times the FTP server type was encountered.

histogram.name: Net.FtpServerTypeCount2
histogram.summary: Each bucket is the number of times the FTP server type was encountered.

histogram.name: Net.GetProxyForUrl_FAIL
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The time spent waiting for WinHttpGetProxyForUrl to return with error.

histogram.name: Net.GetProxyForUrl_OK
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The time spent waiting for WinHttpGetProxyForUrl to return with success.

histogram.name: Net.GoogleConnectionInappropriateFallback
histogram.summary: Records a sample for each HTTPS request to a Google server that either succeeded or received an inappropriate_fallback alert. This is used to estimate how frequently the fallback is used to recover from a spurious network failure.

histogram.name: Net.GoogleConnectionUsedSSLVersionFallback
histogram.summary: Nonzero if the HTTP request was to a Google server which required SSL version fallback. The value indicates the SSL version the request fell back on. Since Google servers support TLS 1.2, any fallback is an indication of network middleware problems.

histogram.name: Net.GoogleConnectionUsedSSLVersionFallback2
histogram.summary: For each successful HTTPS request to a Google server, whether it used the SSL version fallback. The value indicates the SSL version the request fell back on. Since Google servers support TLS 1.2 and FALLBACK_SCSV, any fallback is an indication of a broken local SSL MITM proxy.

histogram.name: Net.GzipEncodingFixupResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed around 2015/03/18. The code which implemented Gzip encoding fixup was removed.
histogram.summary: Resources are sometimes transferred with an incorrect encoding type of gzip. net::Filter::FixupEncodingTypes() attempts to correct for these situations by applying a set of heuristics. This histogram counts the frequency of usage of these heuristics in the wild.

histogram.name: Net.HadConnectionType
histogram.obsolete: The count was inaccurate (it counted transactions rather than connections).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Each bucket is a boolean (0 or 1) indicating whether the user has had a connection of that type during the session.

histogram.name: Net.HadConnectionType2
histogram.obsolete: This statistic measures successful and failed connections, the new one only measures successful ones.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Each bucket is a boolean (0 or 1) indicating whether the user has had a connection of that type during the session.

histogram.name: Net.HadConnectionType3
histogram.summary: Each bucket is a boolean (0 or 1) indicating whether the user has had a successful connection of that type during the session.

histogram.name: Net.HadFtpServerType
histogram.summary: Each bucket is the number of sessions that encountered a given FTP server type. Each session reports a given server type at most once.

histogram.name: Net.HadFtpServerType2
histogram.summary: Each bucket is the number of sessions that encountered a given FTP server type. Each session reports a given server type at most once.

histogram.name: Net.HasBrokenNEON
histogram.summary: Records, on startup, for ARM devices running Android, whether the CPU is known to have a broken NEON unit. See https://crbug.com/341598.

histogram.name: Net.Http2ResponseStatusHeader
histogram.summary: Format of :status header value in HTTP/2 response.

histogram.name: Net.Http2SSLCipherSuite
histogram.summary: The SSL/TLS cipher suite that was negotiated, recorded for each successful HTTP/2 connection.

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthCacheAddEvicted
histogram.summary: Whether adding an entry to the HTTP auth cache evicted another entry.

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthCacheAddEvictedCreation
histogram.summary: When an HTTP auth cache entry is evicted, the time since it was created.

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthCacheAddEvictedLastUse
histogram.summary: When an HTTP auth cache entry is evicted, the time since it was last used.

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthCacheAddPathEvicted
histogram.summary: Whether adding a path to an entry in the HTTP auth cache evicted another path.

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthCacheEntriesExaminedWhenNoMatch
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 10/2018 when changing the cache implementation such that the number of entries examined no longer applies.
histogram.summary: The number of entries examined (equal to the cache size) when attempting to look up an HTTP auth cache entry (either by path or realm) and finding no matching entries. This is recorded whenever the lookup fails (i.e., value 0 in Net.HttpAuthCacheLookupPosition and Net.HttpAuthCacheLookupByPathPosition).

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthCacheLookupByPathPosition
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 10/2018 when changing the cache implementation such that the lookup position no longer applies.
histogram.summary: When looking up an HTTP auth cache entry by path, the position (1-indexed) of the entry on a hit, or 0 on a miss.

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthCacheLookupPosition
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 10/2018 when changing the cache implementation such that the lookup position no longer applies.
histogram.summary: When looking up an HTTP auth cache entry by realm, the position (1-indexed) of the entry on a hit, or 0 on a miss.

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthCount
histogram.summary: Per-authentication-scheme counts of authentication attempts and rejections.

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthPromptType
histogram.summary: Type of the HTTP auth prompt displayed.

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthResource
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of authentication requests for top level pages vs. sub-resources, such as images or iframes.

histogram.name: Net.HttpAuthTarget
histogram.summary: Per-authentication-scheme counts of authentication targets, such as secure servers or proxies.

histogram.name: Net.HttpConnectionLatency
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time between the HttpNetworkTransaction requesting a connection and the time it connected.

histogram.name: Net.HttpContentFreshnessLifetime
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Length of time that a received resource will be cacheable.

histogram.name: Net.HttpContentLength
histogram.summary: Size of the response body. This is the actual number of bytes received, which usually agrees with but is not necessarily the same as the size specified by the Content-Length header.

histogram.name: Net.HttpContentLengthCacheable
histogram.summary: Size of the response body if it is cacheable. This is the actual number of bytes received, which usually agrees with but is not necessarily the same as the size specified by the Content-Length header.

histogram.name: Net.HttpContentLengthCacheable24Hours
histogram.summary: Size of the response body if it is cacheable for at least 24 hours. This is the actual number of bytes received, which usually agrees with but is not necessarily the same as the size specified by the Content-Length header.

histogram.name: Net.HttpContentLengthCacheable4Hours
histogram.summary: Size of the response body if it is cacheable for at least 4 hours. This is the actual number of bytes received, which usually agrees with but is not necessarily the same as the size specified by the Content-Length header.

histogram.name: Net.HttpContentLengthDifference
histogram.summary: The difference between the size specified in the X-Original-Content-Length header and the size of the response body. This is zero if the X-Original-Content-Length header is not present in the response.

histogram.name: Net.HttpContentLengthDifferenceWithValidOCL
histogram.summary: The difference between the size specified in the X-Original-Content-Length header and the size of the response body. Only includes resources that have the X-Original-Content-Length header.

histogram.name: Net.HttpContentLengthV2
histogram.summary: Size of the response body. This is the actual number of bytes received, which usually agrees with but is not necessarily the same as the size specified by the Content-Length header. Replaces Net.HttpContentLength, recording non-DRP Http traffic as "Http.Direct" instead of "Http.Other".

histogram.name: Net.HttpContentLengthWithValidOCL
histogram.summary: Size of the response body. Only includes resources that have the X-Original-Content-Length header.

histogram.name: Net.HttpHeaderParserEvent
histogram.summary: Records the frequency with which a number of hacky HTTP header parsing rules are invoked. This histogram should be removed after we have enough data to know if we can remove the hacks.

histogram.name: net.HttpIdentSrcURL
histogram.summary: Count of requests which contained a basic auth username and password embedded in the URL itself.

histogram.name: Net.HttpJob.PrefilterBytesRead
histogram.summary: Total prefilter (e.g., before decompression) bytes read for an HttpJob request.

histogram.name: Net.HttpJob.TotalTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes to complete an HttpJob, from starting the transaction until we are done reading.

histogram.name: Net.HttpJob.TotalTimeCached
histogram.summary: Time it takes to complete an HttpJob, from starting the transaction until we are done reading, for jobs served from the cache.

histogram.name: Net.HttpJob.TotalTimeCancel
histogram.summary: Time it takes to complete an HttpJob, from starting the transaction until the job is killed. Note that we didn't detect the end of the data for this job.

histogram.name: Net.HttpJob.TotalTimeNotCached
histogram.summary: Time it takes to complete an HttpJob, from starting the transaction until we are done reading, for jobs not served from the cache.

histogram.name: Net.HttpJob.TotalTimeSuccess
histogram.summary: Time it takes to complete an HttpJob, from starting the transaction until we are done reading, for jobs when we read until no more data is available.

histogram.name: Net.HttpOriginalContentLength
histogram.summary: Size specified in the X-Original-Content-Length header. If this header is not present in the response, the size of the response body is used.

histogram.name: Net.HttpOriginalContentLengthV2
histogram.summary:
Net.HttpOriginalContentLength with non-data reduction proxy traffic properly labeled with the "Direct" suffix.
Size specified in the X-Original-Content-Length header. If this header is not present in the response, the size of the response body is used. Replaces Net.HttpOriginalContentLength, breaking down traffic by Direct, ViaDRP, Bypassed, and Other.

histogram.name: Net.HttpOriginalContentLengthWithValidOCL
histogram.summary: Size specified in the X-Original-Content-Length header. Only includes resources that have the X-Original-Content-Length header.

histogram.name: Net.HttpProxy.ConnectLatency
histogram.summary: Time taken to establish the connection to the HTTP proxy.

histogram.name: Net.HttpProxySocketRequestTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes to request a new (unused) HTTP proxy socket.

histogram.name: Net.HttpRequest.ContainsInvalidHeaderValuesInRFC7230
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 06/2016 because this is not continuously tracked.
histogram.summary: Whether a request contains invalid request header values in RFC 7230. This is counted once for every redirect leg. https://crbug.com/455099.

histogram.name: Net.HttpRequestCompletionErrorCodes
histogram.summary: The network error code that the HTTP request completes with, including OK and ABORTED.

histogram.name: Net.HttpRequestCompletionErrorCodes.MainFrame
histogram.summary: The network error code that the HTTP main frame resource request completes with, including OK and ABORTED.

histogram.name: Net.HttpResponse.ContainsInvalidHeaderValuesInRFC7230
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 06/2016 because this is not continuously tracked.
histogram.summary: Whether a response contains invalid response header values in RFC 7230. This is counted once for every redirect leg. https://crbug.com/455099.

histogram.name: Net.HttpResponseCode
histogram.summary: The count of HTTP Response codes encountered.

histogram.name: Net.HttpResponseCode_Nxx_MainFrame
histogram.summary: The count of HTTP Response codes encountered, in response to MAIN_FRAME requests only; saving only the hundreds digit, e.g. 100->1, 300->3.

histogram.name: Net.HttpResponseInfo.ConnectionInfo
histogram.summary: Application protocol used in HTTP response. Recorded every time a URLRequest completes.

histogram.name: Net.HttpResponseInfo.ConnectionInfo.MainFrame
histogram.summary: Application protocol used in HTTP responses to requests for main frames. Only includes requests that went over the network.

histogram.name: Net.HttpResponseInfo.ConnectionInfo.SubResource
histogram.summary: Application protocol used in HTTP responses to requests for resources other than main frames. Does not include internal Chrome requests. Only includes requests that went over the network.

histogram.name: Net.HttpServerProperties.UpdatePrefs
histogram.summary: The location in http_server_properties_manager.cc where UpdatePrefs was called.

histogram.name: Net.HttpSocketType
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The counts of the type of sockets (all HTTP sockets, regardless of any proxy used) used for HTTP[s].

histogram.name: Net.HttpStatusLineStatus
histogram.summary: Records how often violations of RFC 7230's header parsing rules are observed.

histogram.name: Net.HttpStreamFactoryJob.Alt.NextState
histogram.summary: Reports the next state that the Alternative Job is in.

histogram.name: Net.HttpStreamFactoryJob.Alt.State
histogram.summary: Reports the state that the Alternative Job is in.

histogram.name: Net.HttpStreamFactoryJob.Main.NextState
histogram.summary: Reports the next state that the Main Job is in.

histogram.name: Net.HttpStreamFactoryJob.Main.State
histogram.summary: Reports the state that the Main Job is in.

histogram.name: Net.HttpStreamFactoryJob.StreamReadyCallbackTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes for OnStreamReadyCallback to be called.

histogram.name: Net.HttpTimeToFirstByte
histogram.summary: Time from when an HTTP request is issued to when the first byte is processed.

histogram.name: Net.HttpTimeToFirstByte.LargeUpload
histogram.summary: Time from when an HTTP request is issued to when the first byte is processed, for requests with an upload that is > 1 MiB. Excludes chunked uploads.

histogram.name: Net.IOError_SocketReuseType
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of handleable socket errors (connection abort/close/reset) per socket reuse type.

histogram.name: Net.IOError_SocketReuseType_disable_late_binding
histogram.obsolete: Late bindings are on by default now.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of handleable socket errors (connection abort/close/reset) per socket reuse type. Socket late binding is disabled.

histogram.name: Net.IOError_SocketReuseType_enable_late_binding
histogram.obsolete: Late bindings are on by default now.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of handleable socket errors (connection abort/close/reset) per socket reuse type. Socket late binding is enabled.

histogram.name: Net.IOThreadCreationToHttpRequestStart
histogram.summary: Time from when the IOThread is created to when the first URL request is started. Only requests that are created for a profile while Chrome is starting up are considered.

histogram.name: Net.IPv6ConnectDuration
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Duration of time spent during the UDP-connect IPv6 probe.

histogram.name: Net.IPv6ConnectFailureMatch
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Whether the interface-enumeration IPv6 probe method failed given that the UDP-connect IPV6 probe failed.

histogram.name: Net.IPv6ConnectSuccessMatch
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Whether the interface-enumeration IPv6 probe method was successful given that the UDP-connect IPV6 probe was successful.

histogram.name: Net.IPv6Status
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The probe results when a test for IPv6 support is done.

histogram.name: Net.IPv6Status_retest
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The probe results when a test for IPv6 support is done, after a network change event.

histogram.name: Net.JobControllerSet.CountOfJobController
histogram.summary: This counts number of all the job controllers that are still alive if the count is a multiple of 100: 100, 200, 300, etc.

histogram.name: Net.JobControllerSet.CountOfJobController.NonPreconnect.PendingRequest
histogram.summary: This counts number of JobControllers that are still alive, not created for preconnect, and still have a HttpStreamFactoryImpl::Request pending.

histogram.name: Net.JobControllerSet.CountOfJobController.NonPreconnect.RequestGone
histogram.summary: This counts number of job controllers that are still alive, not created for preconnect but HttpStreamFactoryImpl::Request has completed.

histogram.name: Net.JobControllerSet.CountOfJobController.Preconnect
histogram.summary: This counts number of job controllers which are used for preconnect and are still alive.

histogram.name: Net.JobControllerSet.CountOfJobControllerAtShutDown
histogram.summary: This counts number of all the job controllers that are still alive.

histogram.name: Net.JobControllerSet.CountOfNonPreconnectAltJob
histogram.summary: This counts number of alternative jobs which are still alive.

histogram.name: Net.JobControllerSet.CountOfNonPreconnectMainJob
histogram.summary: This counts number of main jobs which are still alive.

histogram.name: Net.JobControllerSet.CountOfPendingRequest
histogram.summary: This counts number of HttpStreamFactoryImpl::Request which are still alive.

histogram.name: Net.JobControllerSet.CountOfPreconnect
histogram.summary: This counts number of job controllers which are used for preconnect and are still alive.

histogram.name: Net.KeepaliveStatisticsRecorder.PeakInflightRequests
histogram.summary: The peak number of concurrent outstanding requests with keepalive specified.

histogram.name: Net.KeepaliveStatisticsRecorder.PeakInflightRequests2
histogram.summary: The peak number of concurrent outstanding requests with keepalive specified. This histogram is reported whenever the peak number is updated. For example, if the inflight number of requests were 0 => 1 => 2 => 1 => 2 => 3 => 2, then 0, 1, 2, and 3 would be reported.

histogram.name: Net.KeepaliveStatisticsRecorder.PeakInflightRequestsPerProcess
histogram.summary: The peak number of concurrent outstanding requests with keepalive specified per render process.

histogram.name: Net.KeepaliveStatisticsRecorder.PeakInflightRequestsPerProcess2
histogram.summary: The peak number of concurrent outstanding requests with keepalive specified per render process. This histogram is reported whenever the peak number is updated. For example, if the inflight number of requests were 0 => 1 => 2 => 1 => 2 => 3 => 2, then 0, 1, 2, and 3 would be reported.

histogram.name: Net.LoadPrefetch.Pattern
histogram.summary: The completion status of prefetches that have finished loading.
histogram.details:
Measurement occurs at ResourceLoader::ResponseCompleted so requests canceled before that point are not registered.
This applies to requests with net::LOAD_PREFETCH, used by various prefetching features such as no-state prefetch and RESOURCE_TYPE_PREFETCH.
Note that "success from cache" means that the UrlRequest::was_cached() was true and unused_since_prefetch was false. "success from network" means that was_cached() was false. "success already prefetched" means that both was_cached() and unused_since_prefetch were true. Validated results are considered cached, even though a conditional network request is made.

histogram.name: Net.MainFrameNoStore
histogram.summary:
The distribution of storable vs "cache-control: no-store" main-frame resources.
Counted after response headers have completed and before the content has completed. Redirects are counted. All HTTP cache transactions are counted, not just those that require the network.

histogram.name: Net.MTPR_GetProxyForUrl_Thread_Wait_Time
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The time that a (non-cancelled) proxy resolution request was stalled waiting for an execution thread, for MultiThreadedProxyResolver.

histogram.name: Net.MTPR_GetProxyForUrl_Time
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The total time that it took for a (non-cancelled) proxy resolution request to complete, for MultiThreadedProxyResolver.

histogram.name: Net.NeedsHWCAP2Workaround
histogram.summary: Records, on startup, for ARM devices running Android, whether the kernel is missing AT_HWCAP2 from the ELF auxv and /proc/cpuinfo reports support for ARMv8 features. Those features should also be reported in AT_HWCAP2. This is to measure the workaround in https://crbug.com/boringssl/46.

histogram.name: Net.NetInternalsUi.Feature
histogram.summary: Counts the number of browser launches where chrome://net-internals and chrome://net-internals/#tests are used.

histogram.name: Net.NetworkConfigWatcherMac.SCDynamicStore.Create
histogram.summary: This is logged when SCDynamicStoreCreate fails in network_config_watcher_mac.cc.

histogram.name: Net.NetworkConfigWatcherMac.SCDynamicStore.Create.RunLoopSource
histogram.summary: This is logged when SCDynamicStoreCreateRunLoopSource fails in network_config_watcher_mac.cc.

histogram.name: Net.NetworkConfigWatcherMac.SCDynamicStore.NumRetry
histogram.summary: This is logged when retrying SCDynamicStore has stopped after either receiving a success or hitting the maximum number of retries in network_config_watcher_mac.cc. The 0 bucket is where no retry is performed, and the maximal bucket is where all retries have failed.

histogram.name: Net.NetworkErrorLogging.HeaderOutcome
histogram.summary: When Network Error Logging receives a "NEL:" header, what happens to it.

histogram.name: Net.NetworkErrorLogging.RequestOutcome
histogram.summary: When Network Error Logging observes a completed request that might generate a report, what happens to it. NEL observes all requests, since it can send reports for a fraction of successful requests as well so error rates can be calculated.

histogram.name: Net.NetworkErrorsRecovered.MainFrame
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: How often automatically retrying to download the main frame of a page in response to specific HTTP network errors succeeds.

histogram.name: Net.NetworkErrorsRecovered.Subresource
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: How often automatically retrying to download a subresource in response to specific HTTP network errors succeeds.

histogram.name: Net.NetworkErrorsUnrecovered.MainFrame
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: How often automatically retrying to download the main frame of a page in response to specific HTTP network errors returns another network error. Histogram includes only the error code that triggered the retry.

histogram.name: Net.NetworkErrorsUnrecovered.Subresource
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: How often automatically retrying to download a subresource in response to specific HTTP network errors returns another network error. Histogram includes only the error code that triggered the retry.

histogram.name: Net.NotifyAddrChangeFailures
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: On Windows, NetworkChangeNotifierWin calls NotifyAddrChange, which can fail for unknown reasons. This records the number of times it fails in a row before a successful call. If it never succeeds, or takes over 100 tries, a value of 100 is recorded. See http://crbug.com/69198

histogram.name: Net.NumDuplicateCookiesInDb
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2015-08-17 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/521135).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of duplicate cookies that were present in the cookie store during startup.

histogram.name: Net.NumQuicSessionsAtShutdown
histogram.summary: The number of QUIC sessions when the QuicStreamFactory is destroyed.

histogram.name: Net.OCSPRequestFailedTimeMs
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2017-04-21 as it was Linux/CrOS only.
histogram.summary: When validating an HTTPS certificate we may have to make one or more HTTP fetches to OCSP responders in order to get revocation information. This measures the amount of time that failures to get OCSP information take.

histogram.name: Net.OCSPRequestSuccess
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2017-04-21 as it was Linux/CrOS only.
histogram.summary: When validating an HTTPS certificate we may have to make one or more HTTP fetches to OCSP responders in order to get revocation information. This records the fraction of successful requests.

histogram.name: Net.OCSPRequestTimeMs
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2017-04-21 as it was Linux/CrOS only.
histogram.summary: When validating an HTTPS certificate we may have to make one or more HTTP fetches to OCSP responders in order to get revocation information. This measures the amount of time that each of those requests takes.

histogram.name: Net.OCSPResponseStapled
histogram.summary: When connecting over HTTPS, a server may include an OCSP response as part of the TLS handshake so that clients do not have to fetch it, provided the client requested the server do so. This measures whether or not a server included an OCSP response when it was requested.

histogram.name: Net.OSErrorsForGetAddrinfo
histogram.summary: Positive error code that was returned by the system library "getaddrinfo()". This error code is platform specific, so when there is a Windows/Linux conflict, both decodings are shown.

histogram.name: Net.OSErrorsForGetAddrinfo_Linux
histogram.summary: Positive error code that was returned by the system library "getaddrinfo()".

histogram.name: Net.OSErrorsForGetAddrinfo_Mac
histogram.summary: Positive error code that was returned by the system library "getaddrinfo()".

histogram.name: Net.OSErrorsForGetAddrinfo_Win
histogram.summary: Positive error code that was returned by the system library "getaddrinfo()".

histogram.name: Net.PacResultForStrippedUrl
histogram.summary:
Proxy Auto Config (PAC) allows specifying an arbitrary javascript program to pick network proxies on a per-request basis (based on the URL). This works by calling the script's FindProxyForURL() function with the target URL as its first argument.
Traditionally, the URL parameter passed into the script contains the exact URL being loaded, except maybe for having stripped the fragment and embedded identity portions of the URL.
This histogram records what happens when the URL passed into FindProxyForURL() additionally has had its path component stripped. Does it return the same proxy list as when calling FindProxyForURL() with the original (unmodified) URL?
This comparison is done only when the URL scheme implies a secure channel (i.e. https:// and wss://), in order to gather data for crbug.com/593759.

histogram.name: Net.Ping_ResponseStartedTime
histogram.summary: How long it took for an <a ping> request to receive a response. Only recorded if a response was received.

histogram.name: Net.Ping_Result
histogram.summary: The result of an <a ping> request, whether it received a response or timed out or failed for some other reason.

histogram.name: Net.PreconnectedLinkNavigations
histogram.summary: Indicate whether a link navigation was preceded by a recent pre-connect trigger (within 10 seconds). There is a high chance that loading the page used a preconnected TCP session.

histogram.name: Net.PreconnectedNavigation
histogram.summary: Indicate whether a URLRequest was preceded by a recent pre-connect trigger (within 10 seconds). There is a high chance that loading the resource used a preconnected TCP session.

histogram.name: Net.PreconnectMotivation
histogram.summary: When a preconnection is made, indicate what the motivation was.
histogram.details: Currently, the most common (only?) motivations are SELF_REFERAL, LEARNED_REFERAL and OMNIBOX. The SELF_REFERAL indicates that we made sure a second connection was available for a resource that either was never before seen, or has historically had no subresources. The LEARNED_REFERAL indicates that we "learned" that a subresource was commonly needed, and that motivated the TCP/IP preconnect. The OMNIBOX motivation happens when a search is being suggested, and we preconnect to the search provider. (WARNING: Prior to version 7.517.*, enums 7, 8, and 9 may be confused, as EARLY_LOAD_MOTIVATED was inserted new 6 value.)

histogram.name: Net.PreconnectProxyStatus
histogram.summary: Indicate whether there was a proxy to preclude preconnection.

histogram.name: Net.PreconnectSkippedToProxyServers
histogram.summary: Indicates number of times a preconnect to proxy server was skipped due to an existing pending preconnection request.

histogram.name: Net.PreconnectSubresourceEval
histogram.summary: What did we decide to do about a predicted resource, based on the historical expected number of connection that this subresource will require.
histogram.details: This is basically the current thresholding of the SubresourceExpectation, relative to current static thresholds, and taking into account whether preconnection is enabled (i.e., if preconnection is disabled, we'll never decide to preconnect).
