histogram.summary: Difference between the actual RTT observation and the estimated RTT. This metric is recorded on every request when the actual observation is higher than the estimated value.

histogram.name: NQE.DifferenceRTTEstimatedAndActual
histogram.obsolete: Replaced in June 2016 by NQE.Accuracy.HttpRTT.* metrics.
histogram.summary: Difference between the estimated RTT and the actual RTT observation. This metric is recorded on every request when the estimated value is higher than the actual observation.

histogram.name: NQE.EffectiveConnectionType.OnECTComputation
histogram.summary: Effective connection type estimated by the network quality estimator. Recorded every time the effective connection type is computed.

histogram.name: NQE.EndToEndRTT.OnECTComputation
histogram.summary: Rough estimate of the computed end-to-end round trip time. Recorded by the network quality estimator every time the effective connection type is computed.

histogram.name: NQE.EstimateAvailable.MainFrame
histogram.summary: This histogram records whether the estimate of the network quality metric was available or not. Recorded at every main frame request.

histogram.name: NQE.ExternalEstimateProvider.DownlinkBandwidth
histogram.summary: Records the estimate of the downlink bandwidth (in Kbps) provided by the external estimate provided. Recorded every time the external estimate provider provides a valid downlink bandwidth estimate to the network quality estimator.

histogram.name: NQE.ExternalEstimateProvider.RTT
histogram.summary: Records the estimate of the RTT provided by the external estimate provided. Recorded every time the external estimate provider provides a valid RTT estimate to the network quality estimator.

histogram.name: NQE.ExternalEstimateProvider.RTT.Accuracy.EstimatedObservedDiff
histogram.summary: Records the difference between the HTTP RTT estimated by the external estimate provider and the observed HTTP RTT. The HTTP RTT estimated by the external estimate provider at the time of navigation start is compared with the HTTP RTT observed during the specified time interval following the start of the navigation.

histogram.name: NQE.ExternalEstimateProviderStatus
histogram.summary: Records the interaction (availability and query count) of Network Quality Estimator with external estimates provider. Logged on network change events, everytime external estimate provider is queried for an updated estimate, and when it proactively notifies that an updated estimate is available.

histogram.name: NQE.FastestRTT
histogram.summary:
Rough estimate of the fastest round-trip-time, before a connectivity change is detected.
This metric is recorded when a connectivity change is detected. This will miss data from users whose connection type never changes and will be biased to users whose connection type changes frequently.

histogram.name: NQE.Kbps.ObservationSource
histogram.summary: Number of Kbps observations indexed by the source of the observation. Recorded by the network quality estimator every time a new observation is made or synthesized.

histogram.name: NQE.Kbps.OnECTComputation
histogram.summary: Rough estimate of the downstream peak throughput (in kilobits per second). Recorded by the network quality estimator every time the effective connection type is computed.

histogram.name: NQE.Kbps.RawObservation
histogram.summary: Downstream throughput observation received or synthesized by network quality estimator. Observations from different sources are recorded in separate histograms.

histogram.name: NQE.MainFrame.EffectiveConnectionType
histogram.summary:
Effective connection type estimated by network quality estimator. Suffixed with the connection type reported by the operating system.
This metric is recorded on main-frame requests.

histogram.name: NQE.MainFrame.Kbps
histogram.summary:
Rough estimate of the downstream peak throughput at different percentiles.
This metric is recorded on main-frame requests.

histogram.name: NQE.MainFrame.RTT
histogram.summary:
Rough estimate of the computed round trip time at the URLRequest layer at different percentiles.
This metric is recorded on main-frame requests.

histogram.name: NQE.MainFrame.TransportRTT
histogram.summary:
Rough estimate of the computed round trip time at the transport layer at different percentiles.
This metric is recorded on main-frame requests.

histogram.name: NQE.NetworkIdAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the NetworkID (SSID of the Wi-Fi network, or the MCC/MNC operator of the cellular network) was available or not. Recorded only on Wi-Fi and cellular networks. Recorded right after the connection type changes.

histogram.name: NQE.PeakKbps
histogram.summary:
Rough estimate of downstream peak throughput, before a connectivity change is detected.
This metric is recorded when a connectivity change is detected. This will miss data from users whose connection type never changes and will be biased to users whose connection type changes frequently.

histogram.name: NQE.Prefs.ReadCount
histogram.summary: Number of times the network quality prefs were read by the network quality estimator.

histogram.name: NQE.Prefs.ReadSize
histogram.summary: Count of the number of network IDs in the prefs read by the network quality estimator.

histogram.name: NQE.Prefs.WriteCount
histogram.summary: Number of times the network quality prefs were written by the network quality estimator.

histogram.name: NQE.RatioEstimatedToActualRTT
histogram.obsolete: Replaced in June 2016 by NQE.Accuracy.HttpRTT.* metrics.
histogram.summary: Ratio of the estimated RTT to the actual RTT observation multiplied by 100.

histogram.name: NQE.RenderThreadNotified
histogram.summary: Count of the number of times renderers were notified of the changes in the network quality estimator. This metric makes sense to compare in frequency relative to other events like the number of page loads or the number of renderers seen. It is expected that we will see at least 1 notification per renderer (on average), and less than 100.

histogram.name: NQE.RTT
histogram.summary:
Rough estimate of the computed round trip time at the URLRequest layer at different percentiles.
This metric is recorded immediately after a connectivity change is detected. The metric name is suffixed with the connection type before the connectivity change was detected. This will miss data from users whose connection type never changes and will be biased to users whose connection type changes frequently.

histogram.name: NQE.RTT.HangingRequest
histogram.summary: HTTP RTT observation received by the network quality estimator. Recorded when the request is detected as hanging.

histogram.name: NQE.RTT.NotAHangingRequest.EndToEndRTT
histogram.summary: HTTP RTT observation received by the network quality estimator. Recorded at the beginning of a request if the request is detected as not hanging because the observed HTTP RTT was comparable to the end to end RTT estimate.

histogram.name: NQE.RTT.NotAHangingRequest.HttpRTT
histogram.summary: HTTP RTT observation received by the network quality estimator. Recorded when the request is detected as not hanging because it was comparable to the HTTP RTT estimate.

histogram.name: NQE.RTT.NotAHangingRequest.MinHttpBound
histogram.summary: HTTP RTT observation received by the network quality estimator. Recorded when the request is detected as not hanging because the RTT value was lower than the minimum threshold RTT needed to be categorized as a hanging request.

histogram.name: NQE.RTT.NotAHangingRequest.TransportRTT
histogram.summary: HTTP RTT observation received by the network quality estimator. Recorded when the request is detected as not hanging because it was comparable to the transport RTT estimate.

histogram.name: NQE.RTT.ObservationSource
histogram.summary: Number of RTT observations indexed by the source of the observation. Recorded by the network quality estimator every time a new observation is made or synthesized.

histogram.name: NQE.RTT.OnECTComputation
histogram.summary: Rough estimate of the round trip time at the URLRequest layer. Recorded by the network quality estimator every time the effective connection type is computed.

histogram.name: NQE.RTT.RawObservation
histogram.summary: RTT observation received or synthesized by network quality estimator. Observations from different sources are recorded in separate histograms.

histogram.name: NQE.RTTObservations
histogram.obsolete: Replaced in May 2016 by NQE.MainFrame.RTT.* metrics.
histogram.summary: RTT observations at the URLRequest layer made by network quality estimator, recorded on every request.

histogram.name: NQE.ThroughputAnalyzer.HangingRequests.Erased
histogram.summary: Count of the requests that were marked as hanging by the heuristic algorithm in the throughput analyzer in the network quality estimator. A sample is recorded everytime the heuristic algorithm is run.

histogram.name: NQE.ThroughputAnalyzer.HangingRequests.NotErased
histogram.summary: Count of the requests that were not marked as hanging by the heuristic algorithm in the throughput analyzer in the network quality estimator. A sample is recorded everytime the heuristic algorithm is run.

histogram.name: NQE.ThroughputObservation.Hanging
histogram.summary: Throughput observation made by network quality estimator, and eventually discarded because the window (over which the througput was computed) was detected as hanging.

histogram.name: NQE.ThroughputObservation.NotHanging
histogram.summary: Throughput observation made by network quality estimator, and used when the window (over which the througput was computed) was detected as not hanging.

histogram.name: NQE.TransportRTT
histogram.summary:
Rough estimate of the computed round trip time at the transport layer at different percentiles.
This metric is recorded immediately after a connectivity change is detected. The metric name is suffixed with the connection type before the connectivity change was detected. This will miss data from users whose connection type never changes and will be biased to users whose connection type changes frequently.

histogram.name: NQE.TransportRTT.OnECTComputation
histogram.summary: Rough estimate of the computed round trip time at the transport layer. Recorded by the network quality estimator every time the effective connection type is computed.

histogram.name: NQE.UnweightedAverage.Accuracy.HttpRTT.EstimatedObservedDiff
histogram.summary: Records the difference between the estimated HTTP RTT and the observed HTTP RTT. HTTP RTT estimated by the network quality estimator at the time of navigation start is compared with the HTTP RTT observed during the specified time interval following the start of the navigation. Both the estimated and the observed RTT are computed using average algorithm that assigns equal weight to all observations.

histogram.name: NQE.UnweightedAverage.MainFrame.RTT
histogram.summary:
Rough estimate of the computed round trip time at the URLRequest layer computed using average algorithm that assigns equal weight to all observations.
This metric is recorded on main-frame requests.

histogram.name: NQE.WeightedAverage.Accuracy.HttpRTT.EstimatedObservedDiff
histogram.summary: Records the difference between the estimated HTTP RTT and the observed HTTP RTT. HTTP RTT estimated by the network quality estimator at the time of navigation start is compared with the HTTP RTT observed during the specified time interval following the start of the navigation. Both the estimated and the observed RTT are computed using weighted average algorithm.

histogram.name: NQE.WeightedAverage.MainFrame.RTT
histogram.summary:
Rough estimate of the computed round trip time at the URLRequest layer computed using weighted average algorithm.
This metric is recorded on main-frame requests.

histogram.name: ntp.searchurls.total
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: TBD

histogram.name: NtpHandler.AttachShownPageType
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 10/2011. No longer tracked, replaced with NewTabPage.DefaultPageType
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The default pane when the NTP is first opened.

histogram.name: NtpHandler.SelectedShownPageType
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 10/2011. No longer tracked, replaced with NewTabPage.SelectedPageType
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The pane selected when the user switches panes in the NTP.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.AccountRevoked.IsEmailId
histogram.summary: This histogram records whether the account ID is actually an email if we detect an account that has an account ID but not email.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.AccountRevoked.MigrationState
histogram.summary: This histogram records the account ID migration status from email to GAIA ID if we detect an account that has an account ID but not email.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.GetOAuth2AccessTokenFailure
histogram.summary: Failure reason of final OAuth2 access token retrieval call during Chrome OS login.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.GetOAuth2AccessTokenRetry
histogram.summary: Retry reason of failed OAuth2 access token retrieval call during Chrome OS login.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.ListAccountsFailure
histogram.summary: Failure reason of final ListAccounts call failure during Chrome OS login. This data is now included in Signin.ListAccountsFailure.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.ListAccountsRetry
histogram.summary: Retry reason of failed ListAccounts call during Chrome OS login. This data is now included in Signin.ListAccountsRetry.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.MergeSessionFailure
histogram.summary: Failure reason of MergeSession call during Chrome OS login, Chrome Signin or account addition. On all OSes as of M44 (previously CrOS only).

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.MergeSessionRetry
histogram.summary: Retry reason of failed MergeSession call during Chrome OS login, Chrome Signin or account addition. On all OSes as of M44 (previously CrOS only).

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.OAuthLoginGaiaCredFailure
histogram.summary: Failure reason of final OAuthLogin (with SID+LSID) call during Chrome OS login.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.OAuthLoginGaiaCredRetry
histogram.summary: Retry reason of failed OAuthLogin (with SID+LSID) call during Chrome OS login.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.OAuthLoginUberTokenFailure
histogram.summary: Failure reason of final OAuthLogin (with uber token) call during Chrome OS login. This data is now (M44+) included in Signin.UberTokenFailure.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.OAuthLoginUberTokenRetry
histogram.summary: Retry reason of failed OAuthLogin (with uber token) call during Chrome OS login. This data is now (M44+) included in Signin.UberTokenRetry.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.PostMergeVerification
histogram.summary: Outcome of Chrome OS GAIA cookie post-merge session verification process. It measures how often /MergeSession request collided with browser session restore process resulting in partially authenticated primary GAIA session.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.PreMergeVerification
histogram.summary: Outcome of Chrome OS GAIA cookie pre-merge session verification process. It measures how often we need to perform /MergeSession request to re-authenticated exisitng user with GAIA.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.SeedState
histogram.summary: Only applicable on M47 on Android. Measure the frequency of a suppressed error state when the account is not seeded.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.SessionRestore
histogram.summary: Outcome of Chrome OS GAIA cookie session restore process.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.SessionRestoreTimeToFailure
histogram.summary: How long it takes for the session restore to fail.

histogram.name: OAuth2Login.SessionRestoreTimeToSuccess
histogram.summary: How long it takes for the session restore to finish succeessfully.

histogram.name: OfflineIndicator.CTR
histogram.summary: Records events related to click through rate (CTR) calculation for Offline Indicator UI. The CTR can be calculated by computing Clicked/Displayed.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.AccessCount
histogram.summary: Number of accesses to the offline page since its creation. This is reported when the offline page was deleted.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.AccessEntryPoint
histogram.summary: Logs the UI location from which an offline page was launched.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.AccessPageCount
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times an offline page is accessed. Events are reported per offline pages namespace.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.AggregatedRequestResult
histogram.summary: Result of servicing requests that may contain offline page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.AggregatedRequestResult2
histogram.summary: Result of servicing requests that may contain offline page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ArchiveManager.ArchiveDirsCreationResult
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2017-10, and replaced by OfflinePages.ArchiveManager.ArchiveDirsCreationResult2 with suffixes for temporary and persistent pages.
histogram.summary: This is recorded every time the archive directory is being created. It doesn't include the case which the archive directory exists.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ArchiveManager.ArchiveDirsCreationResult2
histogram.summary: This is recorded every time the archive directory is being created. It doesn't include the case which the archive directory exists. It has suffixes indicating results for temporary or persistent archives directories.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.BackgroundLoadingFailedCode
histogram.summary: The error codes that caused a page load failure.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.CctApiDisableStatus
histogram.summary:
Reason for the Chrome Custom Tabs API prerender call to be ignored.
There are several causes for the Chrome Custom Tabs API calls to be ignored, this tracks the potential causes so we can see how often the prerenderer doesn't prerender due to these being switched off. Since this is checked elsewhere (CCTAPI), this only catches the user switching off the third party cookies or navigation prediction after the CCT API has checked.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.EffectiveConnectionType.OffliningStartType
histogram.summary:
Effective connection type at the start of an offlining attempt for an attempt that eventually failed.
This metric is recorded for RequestCoordinator::SavePageLater API calls.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.EffectiveConnectionType.PauseRequests
histogram.summary:
Effective connection type when pausing one or more background loads is requested.
This metric is recorded for RequestCoordinator::PauseRequests API calls.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.EffectiveConnectionType.RemoveRequests
histogram.summary:
Effective connection type when removing one or more background loads is requested.
This metric is recorded for RequestCoordinator::RemoveRequests API calls.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.EffectiveConnectionType.ResumeRequests
histogram.summary:
Effective connection type when resuming one or more background loads is requested.
This metric is recorded for RequestCoordinator::ResumeRequests API calls.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.EffectiveConnectionType.SavePageLater
histogram.summary:
Effective connection type when a background load is requested.
This metric is recorded for RequestCoordinator::SavePageLater API calls.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.FinalSavePageResult
histogram.summary: Status code of background offlining requests at the final step.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ImmediateStart.AvailableRequestCount
histogram.summary: Number of queued background load requests available for processing when processing starts immediately (vs. scheduled). This is for non-svelte devices (where svelte is determined by base::SysInfo::IsLowEnd()).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ImmediateStart.AvailableRequestCount.Svelte
histogram.summary: Number of queued background load requests available for processing when processing starts immediately (vs. scheduled). This is for svelte devices (where svelte is determined by base::SysInfo::IsLowEnd()).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ImmediateStart.UnavailableRequestCount
histogram.summary: Number of queued background load requests unavailable for processing when processing starts immediately (vs. scheduled). This is for non-svelte devices (where svelte is determined by base::SysInfo::IsLowEnd()).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ImmediateStart.UnavailableRequestCount.Svelte
histogram.summary: Number of queued background load requests unavailable for processing when processing starts immediately (vs. scheduled). This is for svelte devices (where svelte is determined by base::SysInfo::IsLowEnd()).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ImmediateStartStatus
histogram.summary: Status of attempt to immediately start offlining a page in the background while application is still concurrently using the foreground. This may be attempted when an web page download is initially requested or resumed.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.LoadingErrorStatusCode
histogram.summary: The combined http and net error codes that caused a page load failure.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.OfflinerRequestStatus
histogram.summary: Status code of background offlining (loading and saving) requests.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.OffliningPreviewStatus
histogram.summary: Whether any previews were selected for a page we were asked to make available offline.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.RequestFailure.StartedAttemptCount
histogram.summary: Number of started attempts by failed background load requests.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.RequestSuccess.StartedAttemptCount
histogram.summary: Number of started attempts by successful background load requests.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ResourceCompletion.Css
histogram.summary: True if all requested CSS was loaded when a background page loaded.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ResourceCompletion.Image
histogram.summary: True if all requested images were loaded when a background page loaded.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ResourceCompletion.Xhr
histogram.summary: True if all requested XHRs were completed when a background page loaded.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.SavePageFromCCT
histogram.summary: Whether the save page result came from chrome custom tabs.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ScheduledStart.AvailableRequestCount
histogram.summary: Number of queued background load requests available for processing when background scheduled processing starts. This is for non-svelte devices (where svelte is determined by base::SysInfo::IsLowEnd()).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ScheduledStart.AvailableRequestCount.Svelte
histogram.summary: Number of queued background load requests available for processing when background scheduled processing starts. This is for svelte devices (where svelte is determined by base::SysInfo::IsLowEnd()).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ScheduledStart.UnavailableRequestCount
histogram.summary: Number of queued background load requests unavailable for processing when background scheduled processing starts. This is for non-svelte devices (where svelte is determined by base::SysInfo::IsLowEnd()).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.ScheduledStart.UnavailableRequestCount.Svelte
histogram.summary: Number of queued background load requests unavailable for processing when background scheduled processing starts. This is for svelte devices (where svelte is determined by base::SysInfo::IsLowEnd()).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.TimeToCanceled
histogram.summary: Time from background request until it was canceled.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.TimeToSaved
histogram.summary: Time from background request until it was successfully saved.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.TimeToStart
histogram.summary: Time from background request until processing it was first started. This is for non-svelte devices.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.TimeToStart.Svelte
histogram.summary: Time from background request until processing it was first started on svelte device. Svelte is determined by base::SysInfo::IsLowEnd().

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Background.UnsupportedScheme.ConnectionType
histogram.summary:
Connection type when prerenderer reports Unsupported Scheme error.
To give insight about Unsupported Scheme errors that may be due to lost network connection (as data URL used for rendering an error page will manifest as an Unsupported Scheme error by the prerenderer).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.BatchDelete.Count
histogram.summary: Number of offline pages that are deleted in a batch.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.BatchDelete.TotalPageSize
histogram.summary: Total size, in kilobytes, of all offline pages that are deleted in a batch.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.CanSaveRecentPage
histogram.summary: Reports whether a web page complies with all requirements for it to be allowed to be saved as an offline page. This is reported only on Android, after a successful main frame navigation is finished.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.CleanupThumbnails.Count
histogram.summary: Number of thumbnails removed by the thumbnail cleanup task.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ClearAllStatus
histogram.summary: Status code of wiping out the offline page data.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ClearAllStatus2
histogram.summary: Status code of wiping out the offline page data.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ClearStorageBatchSize
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of Jan 2018, replaced by OfflinePages.ClearTemporaryPages.BatchSize.
histogram.summary: Number of pages deleted in a batch during one clear-storage request.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ClearStoragePreRunUsage
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated in Oct 2017 and replaced by OfflinePages.ClearStoragePreRunUsage2 as it was reporting data in bytes instead of the expected MiB.
histogram.summary: The total storage size used by all offline pages from a specific client namespace.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ClearStoragePreRunUsage2
histogram.summary: The total disk storage size used by all offline pages from a specific client namespace.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ClearStorageResult
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of Jan 2018, replaced by OfflinePages.ClearTemporaryPages.Result.
histogram.summary: Result of asking storage manager to clear storage.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ClearTemporaryPages.BatchSize
histogram.summary: Number of pages deleted in a batch in one clear storage task.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ClearTemporaryPages.Result
histogram.summary: Result of clearing temporary pages to release storage space.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ClearTemporaryPages.TimeSinceCreation
histogram.summary: When a temporary page is deleted due to storage pressure, reports the time since its creation. This metric should be useful to track the occurrence of "content thrashing", when automatically created temporary pages are forcing the deletion of recently saved temporary pages.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Consistency.DeleteOrphanedArchivesResult
histogram.summary: Whether an attempt to delete archive files without metadata was successful.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Consistency.DeletePagesMissingArchiveFileResult
histogram.summary: Whether an attempt to delete pages without archives was successful.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Consistency.ExpirePagesMissingArchiveFileResult
histogram.summary: Whether an attempt to expire pages without archives was successful.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Consistency.OrphanedArchivesCount
histogram.summary: Number of archives without metadata entry when checking consistency.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Consistency.PagesMissingArchiveFileCount
histogram.summary: Number of offline pages without archive file when checking consistency.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ConsistencyCheck.Legacy.DeletedHeadlessFileCount
histogram.summary: Number of files which are deleted during legacy dir clearing since they have no associated DB entry and live in private directory.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ConsistencyCheck.Persistent.ExpiredEntryCount
histogram.summary: Number of DB entries (in persistent namespaces) that have been missing their files for longer than 365 days, and deleted during maintenance task.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ConsistencyCheck.Persistent.MissingFileCount
histogram.summary: Number of files that are found missing during maintenance task, which is also the number of DB entries that are updated with valid file missing time.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ConsistencyCheck.Persistent.PagesMissingArchiveFileCount
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2018, since saving public offline pages to external download directory needs a different consistency check strategy.
histogram.summary: Number of persistent offline pages without archive file when checking consistency. It will only be reported if the number is larger than 0.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ConsistencyCheck.Persistent.PagesMissingDbEntryCount
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2018, since saving public offline pages to external download directory needs a different consistency check strategy.
histogram.summary: Number of archives without database entry when checking persistent page consistency. It will only be reported if the number is larger than 0.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ConsistencyCheck.Persistent.ReappearedFileCount
histogram.summary: Number of files that were marked as missing reappeared in the file system, which is also the number of DB entries that removes file missing time.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ConsistencyCheck.Persistent.Result
histogram.summary: Result of persistent page consistency check.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ConsistencyCheck.Temporary.PagesMissingArchiveFileCount
histogram.summary: Number of temporary offline pages without archive file when checking consistency. It will only be reported if the number is larger than 0.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ConsistencyCheck.Temporary.PagesMissingDbEntryCount
histogram.summary: Number of archives without database entry when checking temporary page consistency. It will only be reported if the number is larger than 0.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ConsistencyCheck.Temporary.Result
histogram.summary: Result of temporary page consistency check.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DeletePage.AccessCount
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 12/2017. Replaced by OfflinePages.AccessCount.
histogram.summary: Number of accesses to the offline page since its creation. This is reported when the offline page was deleted.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DeletePage.FreeSpaceMB
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2/2018. No longer used, duplicate of OfflinePages.SavePage.FreeSpaceMB.
histogram.summary:
The amount of free space available, in megabytes, on the user's device after the page is deleted.
Note that before M52 this operation was started before delete operation.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DeletePage.FreeSpacePercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of free space available on the user's device when the page is being deleted.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DeletePage.LastOpenToCreated
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 01/2018. Replaced by OfflinePages.PageAccessInterval.
histogram.summary: Length of time between when an offline page was created and was opened last time. This is reported when the page was deleted.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DeletePage.PageSize
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2/2018. No longer used, duplicate of OfflinePages.PageSize.
histogram.summary: Size of the offline page, in kilobytes, that was deleted.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DeletePage.TimeSinceLastOpen
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 01/2018. Replaced by OfflinePages.PageAccessInterval.
histogram.summary: Length of time between when an offline page was last opened and was deleted.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DeletePage.TotalPageSizeAsPercentageOfFreeSpace
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2018. Replaced by similar metric OfflinePages.StorageInfo.InternalUsagePercentage and OfflinePages.StorageInfo.ExternalUsagePercentage.
histogram.summary:
The percentage of space taken by offline pages from the free space that could be available, if the feature was not present. I.e. considering situation where the user has: Free Space, Offline content, other apps and data. This is a percentage of: Offline content / (Offline content + Free Space).
The value will be recorded after user deletes a single or multiple offline pages. In case pages are removed in bulk, this value will be reported once. This value is only reported with deleting, as we are trying to infer if lack of free space might have caused the user to delete.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DeletePageCount
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times an offline page is deleted. Events are reported per offline pages namespace.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DeletePageResult
histogram.summary: Result of removing an offline copy for a page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DownloadDeletedPageDuplicateCount
histogram.summary: The number of downloaded pages with the same URL that exist at the time that we delete a downloaded page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DownloadRequestTimeSinceDuplicateRequested
histogram.summary: If at request time there is another request with the same URL, this tracks this amount of time between creation of the most recent request and the current request.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DownloadRequestTimeSinceDuplicateSaved
histogram.summary: If at request time there is another downloaded page with the same URL, this tracks this amount of time between creation of the most recent existing page and the current request.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DownloadSavedPageDuplicateCount
histogram.summary: The number of downloaded pages with the same URL that exist at the time that we save a downloaded page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DownloadSavedPageTimeSinceDuplicateSaved
histogram.summary: If at save time there is another downloaded page with the same URL, this tracks this amount of time between creation of the most recent existing page and the current page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.DownloadUI.PrefetchedItemHasThumbnail
histogram.summary: Whether or not a thumbnail was provided for a prefetched offline article. Recorded when the item is shown in Downloads Home.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Edit.BookmarkUrlChangedForOfflinePage
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 5/2016. Offline pages no longer depend on bookmarks UI.
histogram.summary: Whether a user-edited bookmark URL had a saved offline page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ExpirePage.BatchSize
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 11/2016. Offline pages no longer use two-step expiration.
histogram.summary: Number of pages that are expired in a batch.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ExpirePage.PageLifetime
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 11/2016. Offline pages no longer use two-step expiration.
histogram.summary: Length of time between when an offline page was created and was expired.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ExpirePage.StoreUpdateResult
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 11/2016. Offline pages no longer use two-step expiration.
histogram.summary: Result of updating expired page in store.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ExpirePage.TimeSinceLastAccess
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 11/2016. Offline pages no longer use two-step expiration.
histogram.summary: Length of time between when an offline page was last opened and was expired.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Filter.OnlineWhenEntering
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 5/2016. Offline pages no longer depend on bookmarks UI.
histogram.summary: Whether user is connected when entering the filter with offline only content.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Filter.OnlineWhenLeaving
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 5/2016. Offline pages no longer depend on bookmarks UI.
histogram.summary: Whether user is connected when leaving the filter with offline only content.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.FirstOpenSinceCreated
histogram.summary: The time elapsed between creation of the offline page and the first time it was opened.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.IncognitoSave
histogram.summary: Whether the page that was being saved offline was in incognito mode.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.LastN.IsSavingSamePage
histogram.summary: Upon starting saving a new last_n offline page snapshot, report if that is being done for a page that is new (a navigation happened) or for one that has been previously saved in the same session (no navigation happened). This metric will assist in determining if there is the need to better track actual content changes in pages saved more than once by last_n.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.LaunchLocation
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 5/2016. Offline pages no longer depend on bookmarks UI.
histogram.summary: Logs a UI location from which an offline page is launched.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.LoadStatus
histogram.summary: Status code of loading from the offline pages metadata store.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.LoadSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether an attempt to load the offline pages metadata store was successful.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Model.ArchiveDirCreationTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time to create the offline pages archive directory.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Model.ConstructionToLoadedEventTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time to create the offline pages archive directory and load the offline page model.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Model.FinalLoadSuccessful
histogram.summary: Whether the metadata database was successfully loaded, after possibly several retries.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Model.InitAttemptsSpent
histogram.summary: The count of attempts spent to open the metadata database. In the best case, it's 1. There is a small upper limit. This is recorded only at success. Failures are counted by OfflinePages.Model.FinalLoadSuccessful.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.OfflinePageCount
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 5/2016. This was the dup of OfflinePages.SavedPageCount.
histogram.summary: Number of offline pages the user has. Android only.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.OfflineUsage
histogram.summary: Counts user-days when Chrome was used in specific ways regarding navigation to online and offline content. Buckets are mutually exclusive so that only one bucket is incremented per reported day. This metric is not recorded to UMA immediately due to potential data loss while offline. So day counters are persisted to prefs and reported to the histogram only when the next UMA data upload is likely to succeed. For more details see: https://goo.gl/GJXwFe.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.OnlineOnOpen
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 5/2016. Offline pages no longer depend on bookmarks UI.
histogram.summary: Whether the user was online when a saved page with offline copy was opened.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.OpenSinceLastOpen
histogram.summary: Length of time between two consecutive opens.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PageAccessInterval
histogram.summary: Length of time between accesses to an offline page. This is the same time period used for expiring temporary pages. This metric is recorded when an offline page is accessed.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PageLifetime
histogram.summary: Length of time between when an offline page was created and was removed.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PageSize
histogram.summary: Size of the saved copy of an offline page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PageSizeOnAccess.Offline
histogram.summary: Records the archive size of an offline page loaded during a navigation that took place while the device was considered to be offline or with poor connectivity.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PageSizeOnAccess.Online
histogram.summary: Records the archive size of an offline page loaded during a navigation that took place while the device was considered to be online.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PrefetchEnabled
histogram.summary: Counts user-days when candidate articles were suggested to the Offline Prefetch pipeline while it was enabled. This metric is not recorded to UMA immediately due to potential data loss while offline. So day counters are persisted to prefs and reported to the histogram only when the next UMA data upload is likely to succeed. For more details see: https://goo.gl/GJXwFe.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.ActionAttempts
histogram.summary: Number of attempts to perform a specific retriable pipeline action for each finished prefetch item.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.ActionRetryAttempts
histogram.summary: Number of attempts to perform a specific retriable pipeline action for each finished prefetch item.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.DownloadedArchiveSizeVsExpected
histogram.summary: Reports differences between the expected size of a prefetch archive -- as reported by the service -- and the actual downloaded file size.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.DownloadedFileSize
histogram.summary: Actual size of archive file successfully downloaded by offline page prefetching.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.DownloadExpectedFileSize
histogram.summary: Expected download size of archive file, after gzip content encoding, that started being downloaded by offline page prefetching. Sizes reported in this metric are less than the sizes reported in DownloadedFileSize due to content gzip encoding.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.DownloadFinishedUpdate
histogram.summary: Reports the possible outcomes of an archive download finishing and its respective prefetch item being updated in the store. Downloads are expected to succeed or fail but a respective item should always exist and be updated. The "item not found" cases are exceptions.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.FetchThumbnail.Complete
histogram.summary: Records the final status of attempts to fetch thumbnails for prefetched offline pages.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.FetchThumbnail.Complete2
histogram.summary: Records the final status of attempts to fetch thumbnails for prefetched offline pages. The 1st attempt takes place right after the prefetch item was sent to the cloud service as part of a request for the creation of its offline snapshot. The 2nd attempt occurs right after the offline snapshot was successfully downloaded by the client.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.FetchThumbnail.Start
histogram.summary: Reports each time a thumbnail fetch is attempted.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.FinishedItemErrorCode
histogram.summary: Error code representing the reason why each offline prefetch item was finalized.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.ItemLifetime
histogram.summary: The lifetime of an offline prefetch item, from creation until "zombie-fication" (after metrics collection).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.NotificationAction
histogram.summary: Actions taken with respect to the offline prefetch notification, includes show attempts to calculate CTR.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.OfflinePageImportResult
histogram.summary: Final result of an attempt to import a prefetched article into the Offline Pages system.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.ServiceGetOperationStatus
histogram.summary: Status returned by a request to the Offline Page Service's GetOperation method.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.ServiceGetPageBundleStatus
histogram.summary: Status returned by a request to the Offline Page Service's GetPageBundle method.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.StateCounts
histogram.summary: A prefetch item was seen in this state when starting the background task.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.StuckItemState
histogram.summary: A prefetch item was found in this state after more than 7 days since its creation, time enough for it to have been finalized by the pipeline.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Prefetching.UniqueUrlsAddedCount
histogram.summary: When receiving suggestions in the prefetch pipeline, emits the number of new suggestions added to the database. If there is an error, nothing is emitted.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PrefetchStore.StoreEvent
histogram.summary: Counts when the offline pages prefetch store is opened or closed.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PrefetchStore.TimeFromCloseToOpen
histogram.summary: Tracks the time between store closing and reopening again within a session. The store is meant to close itself after a period of inactivity. We are trying to assess how much time the store is unloaded from memory vs. time it is loaded and not used. This event will be reported when the store is opened after being closed within the same lifetime of Chrome.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PrefetchUsage
histogram.summary: Counts user-days categorized by how Offline Prefetch performed and had its content accessed by the user. Buckets are mutually exclusive so that only one bucket is incremented per reported day. This metric is not recorded to UMA immediately due to potential data loss while offline. So day counters are persisted to prefs and reported to the histogram only when the next UMA data upload is likely to succeed. For more details see: https://goo.gl/GJXwFe.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PublishArchive.CreateDirectoryError
histogram.summary: When publishing an archive, we create the download directory if it doesn't already exist. If we get an error, we record it here.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PublishArchive.MoveFileError
histogram.summary: When publishing an offline page, the move file step can fail. This returns the reason for failure as a linux errno.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PublishArchive.MoveFileFailureReason
histogram.summary: When a move file fails for publishing, what caused the failure.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PublishArchive.PublishSource
histogram.summary: When publishing an offline page, a page might not be found. This returns the publishing path that was used (ex: by Guid or by OfflineId).

histogram.name: OfflinePages.PublishPageResult
histogram.summary: Failure attempting to move a file while publishing downloaded page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.RedirectResult
histogram.summary: Result of automatic redirect to offline version of the page or back. Emitted exactly once when offline-to-online or online-to-offline redirect is determined to be needed and conveys the outcome of redirect.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.RedirectToOfflineCount
histogram.summary: Number of times an offline copy was loaded instead when the user is trying to load the online version of a saved page and there is no network connection.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.RedirectToOnlineCount
histogram.summary: Number of times an online version was loaded instead when the user is trying to load the offline copy of a saved page and there is network connection. connection.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.RequestJob.IntentDataChangedAfterValidation
histogram.summary: Records whether the content read from intent URI (file:// or content://) changed after the initial validation that was done to route the request when the intent was received. This is recorded at the time that Offline Page Request Handler tried to read the data, in preparation to show an offline page to a user.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.RequestJob.OpenFileErrorCode
histogram.summary: Positive net error code for opening the underlying file to serve the offline page, including net::OK.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.RequestJob.RangeHeader
histogram.summary: Track whether the range header is provided when an offline page is served.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.RequestJob.ReadFileErrorCode
histogram.summary: Positive net error code for the failure to read the underlying file to serve the offline page. net::OK is not included.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.RequestJob.SeekFileErrorCode
histogram.summary: Positive net error code for the failure to seek the underlying file to serve the offline page. net::OK is not included.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SavedPageCount
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 2/2018. Replaced by OfflinePages.SavedPageCountUponQuery.
histogram.summary: Number of saved pages restored from the offline pages metadata store when it is logged.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SavedPageCountUponQuery
histogram.summary: Total number of saved offline pages recorded when they are all queried from the store. This value is more meaningful when filtered by unique users as that eliminates multiple reports from the same Chrome instance.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SavePage.FreeSpaceMB
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2018. Replaced by similar metric OfflinePages.StorageInfo.InternalFreeSpaceMB.
histogram.summary:
The amount of free space available, in megabytes, on the user's device after the page is saved.
Note that before M52 this operation was started before save operation.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SavePage.FreeSpacePercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of free space available on the user's device when the page is being saved.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SavePage.PercentLoaded
histogram.summary: The percentage of the page load completed when the button to save an offline page is pressed.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SavePageCount
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times an offline page is saved. Events are reported per offline pages namespace.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SavePageResult
histogram.summary: Result of saving an offline copy for a page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SavePageTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time taken to save an offline copy for a page.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SharedPageWasOffline
histogram.summary: Whether a shared page was an offline page or not.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Sharing.PublishInternalPageResult
histogram.summary: Result of publishing internal page while sharing. CANCELLED means the page to publish isn't found. ALREADY_EXISTS means the page have already been published before.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.ShowOfflinePageOnBadNetwork
histogram.summary: Whether an offline page is shown, instead of error page, when the network is disconnected or poor condition.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SQLStorage.CreateDirectoryResult
histogram.summary: If the directory to store the SQLite database file does not exist, it is created. This is recorded every time the database is opened, even if the directory already exists, in which case it's reported as FILE_OK.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SQLStorage.StoreEvent
histogram.summary: Used as a counter to track different events related to offline page store inner working.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.SQLStorage.TimeFromCloseToOpen
histogram.summary: Tracks the time between store closing and reopening again. The store is meant to close itself after a period of inactivity. We are trying to assess how much time the store is unloaded from memory vs. time it is loaded and not used. This event will be reported when the store is opened after being closed within the same lifetime of Chrome.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.StorageInfo.ExternalArchiveSizeMiB
histogram.summary: The storage space used by the archive files of offline pages, including all files in public directory with mhtml/mht extensions. Collected after every time an offline page is saved successfully.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.StorageInfo.ExternalFreeSpaceMiB
histogram.summary: The amount of free space on the external drive (which contains the public download directory). Collected after every time an offline page is saved successfully.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.StorageInfo.ExternalUsagePercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of storage space on the external volume (that contains public download directory) used by offline pages archives.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.StorageInfo.InternalArchiveSizeMiB
histogram.summary: The storage space used by the archive files of offline pages, including the files in temporary and private directory. Collected after every time an offline page is saved successfully.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.StorageInfo.InternalFreeSpaceMiB
histogram.summary: The amount of free space on the internal drive (which contains the app directory). Collected after every time an offline page is saved successfully.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.StorageInfo.InternalUsagePercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of storage space on internal volume (that contains the app directory) used by offline pages archives.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.TabRestore
histogram.summary: Tracks the reload of contents of an existing tab that is being restored either because that tab is being presented for the first time after Chrome was restarted or because its contents were discarded. All buckets are mutually exclusive (adding up all reported values amounts to the total number of tracked tab restores). This metric is focused on last_n offline pages and is exclusive to Chrome on Android.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.TotalPageSize
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2018. Replaced by similar metric OfflinePages.StorageInfo.TotalArchiveSize.
histogram.summary:
Total size of all the offline pages saved by the user.
This value is recorded whenever the number of pages change, meaning after a page is added or removed. If pages are removed in bulk, this value will be reported only once.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.TotalPageSizePercentage
histogram.summary:
Total size of all the offline pages saved by the user as a percentage of total storage size.
This value is recorded whenever the number of pages change, meaning after a page is added or removed.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.TrustStateOnOpen
histogram.summary: The trust state of the offline page. This is recorded when an offline page is being opened.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Wakeup.BatteryPercentage
histogram.summary: Percentage of battery remaining when the offline page background loading task wakes up to check for work.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Wakeup.ConnectedToPower
histogram.summary: Whether the device is plugged in when the offline page background load task wakes up to check or work.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Wakeup.DelayTime
histogram.summary: Time delay from a dinosaur page to connection being available.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.Wakeup.NetworkAvailable
histogram.summary: Which network is available (if any) when the offling page background loading task wakes up to check for work.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.WebsiteSettings.ConnectedWhenOpenOnlineButtonClicked
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the browser was connected when Open online button was clicked on Page Info popup, which causes a reload of an offline page to online version.

histogram.name: OfflinePages.WebsiteSettings.OpenOnlineButtonVisible
histogram.summary: Indicates whether Open online was visible in Page Info popup, when it was shown for an offline page. (The button is visible only when Chrome is connected when the Page Info popup is opened for offline page.)

histogram.name: OfflinePolicy.SuccessfulResourceLoadPercentage
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: When a page is loaded in offline mode, the percentage of resources on that page that were successfully loaded.

histogram.name: Omnibox.AggressiveHistoryURLProviderFieldTrialBeacon
histogram.obsolete: Aggressive HistoryURL provider field trial deleted in spring 2012.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: A number that indicates what omnibox ranking behavior the user is seeing as part of the OmniboxAggressiveHistoryURLProvider field trial (OmniboxAggressiveHistoryURLProvider).

histogram.name: Omnibox.AnswerParseSuccess
histogram.summary: For each answer received in suggest responses, the number that are well-formed and contain all the required elements.

histogram.name: Omnibox.AnswerParseType
histogram.summary: The number of times each omnibox suggestion answer type (e.g., weather, sports score) was received and parsed successfully. Can be normalized with the count of emits to Omnibox.SuggestRequest.Success.GoogleResponseTime, which counts all successful suggest responses from Google, not just those with answers attached. Note that GoogleResponseTime is technically off given that it will still count cases where we fail to extract, deserialize, or parse the response. But these cases are rare to non-existent.

histogram.name: Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency
histogram.summary:
Records the time taken between a keystroke being typed in the omnibox and the text being painted. If there are multiple keystrokes before a paint, logs the time since the earliest one.
This duration is composed of three parts:
a) the time spent processing the initial input event
b) the time spent for the repaint task to be scheduled on the message loop
c) the time spent painting the Omnibox
d) (on views platforms) the time until the pixels are actually composited
There's a number of breakdown metrics to help diagnose a regression. First, Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency.ToPaint measures the combined time of (a) and (b). Omnibox.QueryTime2 is a good proxy for just (a). And there's also Omnibox.PaintTime that corresponds to (c). We don't have a direct metric for (d), but if neither Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency.ToPaint nor Omnibox.PaintTime regressed, then the regression must be in (d).
It's also possible to get insight into (b) via the UMA Task Profiler dashboard by looking at "average queue time" of the task DisplayScheduler::ScheduleBeginFrameDeadline.
Note: The semantics of this metric on views platforms changed in M62, as previously time (d) was not included in the metric.

histogram.name: Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency.Composited
histogram.obsolete: This became the new implementation of Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency as of 08/2017.
histogram.summary: A refinement of the Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency metric that attempts to more accurately track the latency by measuring until the point when the compositor has actually put pixels on the screen. In particular, this will capture the time used to also paint the Omnibox result views as a result of the character insertion, which is not covered by Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency. If there are multiple keystrokes before a paint, logs the time since the earliest one.

histogram.name: Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency.ToPaint
histogram.summary: Records the time taken between a keystroke being typed in the omnibox and the time when we're ready to paint the omnibox. This is a breakdown diagnostic metric for Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency, to help diagnose regressions in that metric. See that metric's description for more info.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ClipboardSuggestionShownAge
histogram.summary:
Recorded every time the omnibox is focussed and a recent URL from the user's clipboard is suggested. The value indicates the estimated age of the clipboard. (If Chrome observed the clipboard modification, this age is exact. If Chrome did not observe the modification, then it's a conservative estimate: the last time Chrome observed a clipboard modification, which is certainly older than the current clipboard. If Chrome never observed a clipboard modification, no clipboard suggestion is shown, meaning this histogram will never be emitted to.)
Intended to be compared with MobileOmnibox.PressedClipboardSuggestionAge.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ClipboardSuggestionShownNumTimes
histogram.summary:
Recorded every time the omnibox is focussed and a recent URL from the user's clipboard is suggested. The number emitted is the number of times the URL has been suggested within the same session including the current time. Thus, the third time it is shown, we'll emit a three to this histogram, and this histogram will have previously seen emits of one and two. If the clipboard content was the same during a previous run of Chrome and this URL was suggested during that run, those impressions are not counted. Also, if the clipboard content changes during a particular run of Chrome to another URL, the omnibox is focused and that URL is suggested, then content changes back and Chrome starts suggesting the older URL again, the counts start again from scratch. Chrome only remembers the number of times the URL was shown consecutively.
This value is useful to compare with the number of times a clipboard suggestion has been shown when it is clicked. This value can be obtained from OmniboxEvent records in which the selected suggestion is from Clipboard provider. In those cases, look in the Clipboard provider's ProviderInfo field for |times_returned_results_in_session|. Note that at the time of this writing that OmniboxEvent logs aren't recorded in incognito whereas histograms are. Thus, the total counts will not be comparable, though the distributions should be.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ClipboardSuggestionShownWithCurrentURL
histogram.summary:
Recorded every time the omnibox is focussed and a recent URL from the user's clipboard is suggested. The value indicates whether the current URL was shown (which would appear above the clipboard URL suggestion) or was absent (which ought to only happen when the omnibox is empty / the user is on the NTP).
On Android, the total count for this histogram can be usefully compared with the count of the user action FocusLocation in order to determine the rate at which a clipboard URL suggestion is shown in the omnibox. To determine the same rate on iOS, one needs to compare this histogram's count with the sum of the user actions MobileFocusedOmniboxOnNtp, MobileFocusedFakeboxOnNtp, and MobileFocusedOmniboxNotOnNtp.
Furthermore, on either platform, this histogram's count can be usefully compared with the count in the clipboard bucket of the Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Provider histogram to determine the clickthrough rate on these suggestions.

histogram.name: Omnibox.CutOrCopyAllText
histogram.summary: The number of cut or copy commands on all selected text in the omnibox. Gathered on desktop platforms (Win, Mac, Linux, Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Omnibox.DocumentSuggest.Requests
histogram.summary: Counts the number of document suggest requests the omnibox sent, were invalidated, and were completed successfully.

histogram.name: Omnibox.DocumentSuggest.ResultCount
histogram.summary: Number of results returned in each document suggestion reply. Logged for successful requests where the provider returned a parseable result set.

histogram.name: Omnibox.EnteredKeywordMode
histogram.summary: The number of times users enter keyword hint mode "Search ___ for:" and how.

histogram.name: Omnibox.FocusToEditTime
histogram.summary: The length of time between when a user focused on the omnibox and first modifies the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.FocusToOpenTime
histogram.summary: The length of time between when a user focused on the omnibox and opened an omnibox match (which could be what they typed or a suggestion).

histogram.name: Omnibox.FocusToOpenTimeAnyPopupState
histogram.summary: The length of time between when a user focused on the omnibox and opened an omnibox match (which could be what they typed or a suggestion). This is recorded regardless of whether the omnibox dropdown (a.k.a. popup) is open.

histogram.name: Omnibox.FocusToOpenTimeAnyPopupState2
histogram.obsolete: Replaced with Omnibox.FocusToOpenTimeAnyPopupState3 in January, 2017, which only differs in the bucketing and range.
histogram.summary:
The length of time between when a user focused on the omnibox and opened an omnibox match (which could be what they typed or a suggestion). This is recorded regardless of whether the omnibox dropdown (a.k.a. popup) is open. It is not recorded if a match is opened without triggering a focus event, e.g., when a user drags a URL to the omnibox to navigate.
To know how common this last condition is, compare the total count of this histogram to the total number of omnibox events.

histogram.name: Omnibox.FocusToOpenTimeAnyPopupState3
histogram.summary:
The length of time between when a user focused on the omnibox and opened an omnibox match (which could be what they typed or a suggestion). This is recorded regardless of whether the omnibox dropdown (a.k.a. popup) is open. It is not recorded if a match is opened without triggering a focus event, e.g., when a user drags a URL to the omnibox to navigate.
To know how common this last condition is, compare the total count of this histogram to the total number of omnibox events.

histogram.name: Omnibox.HardwareKeyboardModeEnabled
histogram.summary: iOS: Records whether a hardware keyboard is used to input text. This is recorded each time a UIKeyboardWillChangeFrameNotification is sent while editing the omnibox text.

histogram.name: Omnibox.HasLegalDefaultMatchWithoutCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether there was at least one legal default match without an |inline_autocompletion|. Recorded every time AutocompleteResult::SortAndCull() is called, which could happen multiple times on each keystroke.

histogram.name: Omnibox.HistoryQuickHistoryIDSetFromWords
histogram.summary: Times URLIndexPrivateData::HistoryIDSetFromWords(), which is called by the omnibox's HistoryQuick provider. URLIndexPrivateData::HistoryIDSetFromWords() can be called multiple times per keystroke due to, for example, the cursor being in the middle of the input string or SearchProvider's calls to Classify().

histogram.name: Omnibox.InputType
histogram.summary: The kind of input the user provided when using the omnibox to go somewhere. The type can be misleading. For example if the user typed 'http:/', it gets marked as a query because it cannot be opened as a URL even though the user probably wanted and selected a URL from the list of suggestions.

histogram.name: Omnibox.IsPasteAndGo
histogram.summary: Whether an omnibox interaction is a paste-and-search/paste-and-go action. (This histogram records both of these in the "True" bucket for this histogram because both of these are referred to as paste-and-go in the code.) These typically involve right-clicking in the omnibox and selecting that option from the dropdown.

histogram.name: Omnibox.IsPopupOpen
histogram.summary: Whether the omnibox popup (a.k.a. dropdown) is open at the time the user used the omnibox to go somewhere. It can be closed if, for instance, the user clicked in the omnibox and hit return to reload the same page. Also, because paste-and-search/paste-and-go actions ignore the current content of the omnibox dropdown (if it is open) when they happen, we pretend the dropdown is closed when logging these.

histogram.name: Omnibox.JustDeletedText
histogram.summary: Whether the user deleted text immediately before selecting an omnibox suggestion. This is usually the result of pressing backspace or delete.

histogram.name: Omnibox.NumEvents
histogram.summary: The number of times users used the omnibox to go somewhere.

histogram.name: Omnibox.NumTypedTerms
histogram.summary: The number of terms in the text the user entered in the omnibox when they used the omnibox to go somewhere. Terms are defined by splitting on whitespace. All values larger than 6 are recorded in bucket 6.

histogram.name: Omnibox.PageContext
histogram.summary: What the user was viewing when the user used the omnibox to go somewhere.

histogram.name: Omnibox.PaintTime
histogram.summary: Records the time to paint the omnibox contents. This is a subcomponent of Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency. Implemented on desktop platforms.

histogram.name: Omnibox.Paste
histogram.summary: The number of paste commands on the text in the omnibox. Reported every time a paste command is done.

histogram.name: Omnibox.PasteAndGo
histogram.summary: The number of paste-and-go commands on the text in the omnibox. Reported every time a paste-and-go command is done.

histogram.name: Omnibox.PhysicalWebProvider.SuggestionUsedWithoutOmniboxFocus
histogram.summary: Records a boolean value indicating whether the Physical Web provider was invoked by focusing the omnibox during the current omnibox session. This is unexpected; it's probably a bug somwhere. Recorded when the user selects an omnibox suggestion. Does not record when the Physical Web omnibox provider is disabled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.PhysicalWebProviderMatches
histogram.summary: The number of matches returned by PhysicalWebProvider. Emitted when the omnibox is first focused, unless the user is in incognito mode. Capped at 10.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ProgressBarBreakPointUpdateCount
histogram.summary: The number of progress bar break point updates from page load started to page load finished.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ProgressBarUpdateCount
histogram.summary: The number of progress bar frame updates from page load started to page load finished. If there is no animation, this matches Omnibox.ProgressBarBreakPointUpdateCount. Note that there can be additional updates after page load finished, animating to 100%, but this histogram doesn't include them.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ProviderTime
histogram.summary: The length of time taken by the named provider"s synchronous pass.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ProviderTime2
histogram.summary: The length of time taken by the named provider"s synchronous pass.

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryBookmarksTime
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time the HistoryContentProvider takes to perform a bookmark search.

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryGeolocationAcquisitionTime
histogram.summary: The elapsed time to acquire the location sent in the X-Geo header for an Omnibox query.

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryGeolocationHorizontalAccuracy
histogram.summary: The estimated horizontal accuracy of the location sent in the X-Geo header for an Omnibox query.

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryIosLocationAuthorizationStatus
histogram.summary: For iOS, whether the application is authorized to use location services when the user enters a search query into the Omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes for the omnibox to become responsive to user input after the user has typed N characters. This measures the time it takes to start all the asynchronous autocomplete providers (but not wait for them to finish).

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryTime2
histogram.summary: Time it takes for the omnibox to become responsive to user input after the user has typed N characters. This measures the time it takes to start all autocomplete providers (but not wait for the asynchronous ones to finish).

histogram.name: Omnibox.SaveStateForTabSwitch.UserInputInProgress
histogram.summary: When a user switches tabs, whether the omnibox had an edit in progress.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchEngine
histogram.obsolete: Made obsolete around Chrome 32. Use Omnibox.SearchEngineType instead.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The id of search engine that was used for search in omnibox. See src/chrome/browser/search_engines/template_url_prepopulate_data.cc for more info.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchEngineType
histogram.summary: The type of search engine associated with a match opened from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchProvider.AddHistoryResultsTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes to add all the raw history results to the list of matches.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchProvider.ConvertResultsTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes to convert all the results to matches and add them to a map, to keep the most relevant match for each result.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchProvider.GetMostRecentKeywordTermsDefaultProviderTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes for the omnibox to search the previous query history database for queries that start with the omnibox text.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchProviderMatches
histogram.summary: The number of matches returned by SearchProvider. Emitted on every call to SearchProvider::Start(), which effectively means every key stroke in the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SelectedPosition
histogram.summary: The index of the item that the user selected in the omnibox popup (a.k.a. dropdown) list. 0 means the inline suggestion shown within the omnibox. This is also the same suggestion shown as the top item in the dropdown. The second item in the dropdown will be recorded as bucket 1. The selected position is always set to 0 when the popup is closed at the time of navigation or if the user did a paste-and-search or paste-and-go action.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.NearbyURLCount
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated in M58, replaced with Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.NearbyURLCount.AtFocus and Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.NearbyURLCount.AtMatchCreation.
histogram.summary:
The number of nearby Physical Web URLs when the user focused the omnibox. Recorded when the user accepts an omnibox suggestion, regardless of whether the suggestion came from PhysicalWebProvider. Capped at 50.
In M57, the values recorded by this histogram were determined to be bugged. It's believed that it was recording uninitialized values due to a corner case on Android that allows an omnibox suggestion to be selected without first focusing the omnibox. In addition to the new histograms, Omnibox.PhysicalWebProvider.SuggestionUsedWithoutOmniboxFocus was added to detect this case. https://crbug.com/691059

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.NearbyURLCount.AtFocus
histogram.summary:
The number of nearby Physical Web URLs when Physical Web Provider was last focused (i.e., the start of the current session). Recorded when the user accepts an omnibox suggestion, regardless of whether the suggestion came from PhysicalWebProvider. Capped at 50.
Does not record if the omnibox was not focused during the current session. Omnibox.PhysicalWebProvider.SuggestionUsedWithoutOmniboxFocus records true when we hit this case.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.NearbyURLCount.AtMatchCreation
histogram.summary: The number of nearby Physical Web URLs when Physical Web Provider last constructed matches. Recorded when the user accepts an omnibox suggestion, regardless of whether the suggestion came from PhysicalWebProvider. Capped at 50.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.OfferedTabMatch
histogram.summary:
Measures whether the suggestion that was selected by the user offered a tab match. A tab match occurs when the URL specified by the suggestion matches the URL navigated to by another tab in the browser, within the margin of our URL stripping scheme.
This metric typically only makes sense with respect to Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.OfferedTabMatch. Only recent versions of the browser send the metric. Therefore, other metrics may include datapoints not covered by this metric (unless one narrows the data by version.)

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Provider
histogram.summary: The provider of the suggestion the user selected when the user used the omnibox to go somewhere.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Provider.ByPageContext
histogram.summary: The provider of the suggestion the user selected when the user used the omnibox to go somewhere, only for omnibox interactions that start in the specified page context:

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.ProviderAndResultType
histogram.summary: The provider and result type of the suggestion the user selected when the user used the omnibox to go somewhere.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.ProviderAndResultType.ByPageContext
histogram.summary: The provider and result type of the suggestion the user selected when the user used the omnibox to go somewhere, only for omnibox interactions that start in the specified page context:

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Search.Experimental.ForegroundToFirstMeaningfulPaint.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from the page first appearing in the foreground to its first meaningful paint. Only recorded on navigations that use a prerender that is to a search query suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Search.Experimental.NavigationToFirstMeaningfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation start to first meaningful paint. Only recorded for a search query suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Search.ForegroundToFirstContentfulPaint.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from the page first appearing in the foreground to its first contentful paint. Only recorded on navigations that use a prerender that is to a search query suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Search.NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation start to first contentful paint. Only recorded for a search query suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Search.NavigationToFirstForeground.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from a page being navigated to in prerender to it first showing up in foreground. Only recorded on navigations that used a prerender that was to a search query suggestion selected from the omnibox. This is only recorded on pages that experience a first contentful paint.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.SearchVsUrl
histogram.summary: The rough type of the suggestion the user selected when the user used the omnibox to go somewhere.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.SearchVsUrl.ByPageContext
histogram.summary: The rough type of the suggestion the user selected when the user used the omnibox to go somewhere, only for omnibox interactions that start in the specified page context:

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.SelectedTabMatch
histogram.summary:
Measures whether the suggestion that was selected by the user offered a tab match and specifically that the user chose the tab match over normal navigation. A tab match occurs when the URL specified by the suggestion matches the URL navigated to by another tab in the browser, within the margin of our URL stripping scheme. By selecting the tab match, the browser simply switches focus to the matching tab.
This metric typically only makes sense with respect to Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.OfferedTabMatch. Only recent versions of the browser send the metric. Therefore, other metrics may include datapoints not covered by this metric (unless one narrows the data by version.)
This metric should be stricly less than Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.OfferedTabMatch, and is typically used to discover the percentage of offered tab matches that the user accepts.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.URL.Experimental.ForegroundToFirstMeaningfulPaint.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from the page first appearing in the foreground to its first meaningful paint. Only recorded on navigations that use a prerender that is to a URL suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.URL.Experimental.NavigationToFirstMeaningfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation start to first meaningful paint. Only recorded for a URL suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.URL.ForegroundToFirstContentfulPaint.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from the page first appearing in the foreground to its first contentful paint. Only recorded on navigations that use a prerender that is to a URL suggestion selected from the omnibox.
