histogram.summary: How long a browsing session was measured to be. Logged when a Browser window becomes active if the previous activation was longer than the minimum configured in the "CrossDevicePromo" experiment. Desktop only.

histogram.name: Signin.XDevicePromo.BrowsingSessionDurationComputed
histogram.summary: How often browsers are considered activated, which in turn triggers the CrossDevicePromo. Logged every time a Browser window becomes active. We need this to estimate QPS for RPC calls. Desktop only.

histogram.name: Signin.XDevicePromo.Eligibility
histogram.summary: The reasons for which a profile is or is not eligible for the Desktop Cross Device Sign In Promo. Logged every time a new Browsing session is detected as part of a Browser window becoming active. Desktop only.

histogram.name: Signin.XDevicePromo.Initialized
histogram.summary: Tracks if profiles initialized the XDevicePromo, and if not, why. Logged at Profile startup and (if not initialized then) when a new browsing session is detected during Browser window activation. Desktop only.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.CacheSizeOnInit
histogram.summary: The size of the cache at the time that the index has finished initializing.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.CheckCRCResult
histogram.summary: Whether or not the CRC was checked at the moment when the last reference to a read-only entry stream is closed.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.ConsistencyResult
histogram.summary: Record the outcome of the simple DiskCache consistency check. The consistency check is executed every time a simple DiskCache is initialized. This check includes both inspecting the schema on disk and upgrading the schema to the latest version if necessary.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.CreationToIndex
histogram.summary: The time from the creation of the simple cache backend until the index has been loaded from disk.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.CreationToIndexFail
histogram.summary: The time from the creation of the simple cache backend until the index fails to load.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.DiskCloseLatency
histogram.summary: The time to close (write out footers and close the file) the cache entry on disk. Includes only successful operations.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.DiskCreateLatency
histogram.summary: The time to access (open for creation and write initial headers) the cache entry on disk. Includes only successful operations.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.DiskDoomLatency
histogram.summary: The time to remove the cache entry on disk. Includes only individually doomed entries, not entries bulk-erased during eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.DiskOpenLatency
histogram.summary: The time to access (open and read headers from all files) the cache entry on disk. Includes only successful operations.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.DiskOpenStream2NonTinyLatency
histogram.summary: The time to open the stream 2 file successfully on disk cache entry open with known key, when the size of payload is greater than 32 bytes.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.DiskOpenStream2TinyLatency
histogram.summary: The time to open the stream 2 file successfully on disk cache entry open with known key, when the size of payload is 32 bytes or less.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.DiskWriteLatency
histogram.summary: The time to write a chunk to the cache entry on disk. Includes only successful operations.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.EntryCreatedAndStream2Omitted
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2018-02. Not creating stream 2 on CreateEntry entries is indeed a good idea, since stream 2 writes are done on things opened with OpenEntry. (Which also means this metric wasn't good at evaluating the prevalence of stream 2 in general).
histogram.summary: Whether, upon creation of a new cache entry, the file for stream 2 was omitted since that stream was empty.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.EntryCreationResult
histogram.summary: For entry creation operations that were sent to the disk, the result of creation.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.EntryCreationTime
histogram.summary: The time, in ms, spent creating a new entry on disk.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.EntryOpenedAndStream2Removed
histogram.summary: Whether, upon opening of an existing cache entry, stream 2 was empty and the file for that stream was therefore removed.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.EntryOperationsPending
histogram.summary: At the time that operations are run, the number of pending operations on a particular entry.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.CacheSizeOnStart
histogram.summary: The size of the cache at the beginning of an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.CacheSizeOnStart2
histogram.summary: The size of the cache at the beginning of an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.EntryCount
histogram.summary: The number of entries to be erased in an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.MaxCacheSizeOnStart
histogram.summary: The maximum allowed size of the cache at the beginning of an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.MaxCacheSizeOnStart2
histogram.summary: The maximum allowed size of the cache at the beginning of an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.Result
histogram.summary: The result of an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.SizeOfEvicted
histogram.summary: The number of bytes to be erased in an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.SizeOfEvicted2
histogram.summary: The amount of memory freed in an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.SizeWhenDone
histogram.summary: The size of the cache after running an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.SizeWhenDone2
histogram.summary: The size of the cache after running an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.TimeToDone
histogram.summary: Time spent completing an eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.Eviction.TimeToSelectEntries
histogram.summary: Time spent selecting entries for eviction.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.FileDescriptorLimiterAction
histogram.summary: This even is emitted when SimpleCache is forced to either close some files due to being over FD limit, or reopen files it had to close due to such limit earlier.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.FileDescriptorLimitHard
histogram.summary: The maximum limit of how many file descriptors a process can open. Emitted each time the browser is launched, if the limit could be retrieved. (This is the highest value we could raise the current limit to if we liked.)

histogram.name: SimpleCache.FileDescriptorLimitSoft
histogram.summary: The current limit of how many file descriptors a process can open. Emitted each time the browser is launched, if the limit could be retrieved. (We can raise this to the maximum limit if we like, without root access.)

histogram.name: SimpleCache.FileDescriptorLimitStatus
histogram.summary: The result of trying to get the file descriptor limit. Emitted each time the browser is launched.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.GlobalOpenEntryCount
histogram.summary: The number of open entries across all caches backed by the Simple Cache. An entry is opened whenever a caller asks to open it to read or write cache data, and remains open until the last caller asks to close it. Logged whenever an entry is opened or closed.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.HeaderSize
histogram.summary: The size of the header stream of a Simple Cache entry, emitted every time the headers are written or rewritten.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.HeaderSizeChange
histogram.summary: How the header size has changed in a Simple Cache entry, emitted every time a write operation occurs on the header stream. (This includes the initial write, rewrites, and other writes that we couldn't classify.)

histogram.name: SimpleCache.HeaderSizeDecreaseAbsolute
histogram.summary: The absolute size decrease of the header stream of a Simple Cache entry, emitted every time the headers are rewritten with a smaller size.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.HeaderSizeDecreasePercentage
histogram.summary: The relative size decrease of the header stream of a Simple Cache entry, emitted every time the headers are rewritten with a smaller size.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.HeaderSizeIncreaseAbsolute
histogram.summary: The absolute size increase of the header stream of a Simple Cache entry, emitted every time the headers are rewritten with a larger size.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.HeaderSizeIncreasePercentage
histogram.summary: The relative size increase of the header stream of a Simple Cache entry, emitted every time the headers are rewritten with a larger size.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexCorrupt
histogram.summary: For each index load, whether the index file was corrupt.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexCreatedEntryCount
histogram.summary: The number of entries in a newly created index file.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexEntriesLoaded
histogram.summary: Number of entries loaded from the index file on start.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexEntriesRestored
histogram.summary: Number of entries restored from disk when there was no index or the index was corrupted.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexFileStateOnLoad
histogram.summary: The state the index file is at when an attempt is made to load from it.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexInitializationWaiters
histogram.summary: At the time of index initialization, the number of enqueued jobs awaiting index initialization.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexInitializeMethod
histogram.summary: The method used to initialize the simple cache index.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexLoadTime
histogram.summary: Time (as measured on the worker pool) spent loading the index file.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexNumEntriesOnInit
histogram.summary: The number of entries in the index at the time that the index has finished initializing.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexNumEntriesOnWrite
histogram.summary: The number of entries written to the index on a flush.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexRestoreTime
histogram.summary: Time (as measured on the worker pool) spent restoring the index file by iterating directory entries.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexStale
histogram.summary: For each index load, whether the index file was stale.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexWriteInterval.Background
histogram.summary: The interval between index saves, for apps in the background.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexWriteInterval.Foreground
histogram.summary: The interval between index saves, for apps in the foreground.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexWriteReason
histogram.summary: The reason an index was written to disk. Recorded every time an index is saved.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexWriteReasonAtLoad
histogram.summary: Recorded immediately after loading a fresh index (INDEX_STATE_FRESH or INDEX_STATE_FRESH_CONCURRENT_UPDATES), the reason the loaded index was written to disk.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexWriteToDiskTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time spend writing the index file to disk, measured starting at the beginning of the write on the callback thread, and calculated using the completion time on the worker pool.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexWriteToDiskTime.Background
histogram.summary: The amount of time spend writing the index file to disk, for apps in the background, measured starting at the beginning of the write on the callback thread, and calculated using the completion time on the worker pool.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.IndexWriteToDiskTime.Foreground
histogram.summary: The amount of time spend writing the index file to disk, for apps in the foreground, measured starting at the beginning of the write on the callback thread, and calculated using the completion time on the worker pool.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.KeyMatchedOnOpen
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2016-05 as the match checking was moved back into the entry itself, so this result is now reported in the SimpleCache.x.SyncOpenResult histograms.
histogram.summary: For each call to OpenEntry, whether the key on disk matched the request key.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.LastClusterLossPercent
histogram.summary: For each file in the Simple Cache, the percentage of disk space used by the cluster loss, the unused disk space in the last 4096 byte cluster of the file.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.LastClusterSize
histogram.summary: For each file in the Simple Cache, the number of bytes in the last 4096 byte cluster when the entry is saved to disk.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.MaxCacheSizeOnInit
histogram.summary: The maximum allowed size of the cache at the time that the index has finished initializing.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.NumOpsBlockedByPendingDoom
histogram.summary: Number of operations that were queued behind a particular doom operation (with a mass doom counting as a single operation).

histogram.name: SimpleCache.OpenEntryIndexState
histogram.summary: At the time that an entry is opened, the state of that entry in the index.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.PercentFullOnInit
histogram.summary: The fullness (in percent) of the cache at the time that the index has finished initializing. The percentage is calculated as (cache_size * 100) / max_cache_size.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.QueueLatency.CreateEntry
histogram.summary: Delay between when the I/O portion of CreateEntry is enqueued and when its execution begins.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.QueueLatency.OpenEntry
histogram.summary: Delay between when the I/O portion of OpenEntry is enqueued and when its execution begins.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.QueueLatency.PendingDoom
histogram.summary: Delay between when an operation is deferred due to a pending doom for its key, and when it can resume execution.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.ReadIsParallelizable
histogram.summary: For each Read operation, whether it could have been issued in parallel of a previous Read operation.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.ReadResult
histogram.summary: The outcome of Entry::ReadData in the simple cache.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.ReadStream1FromPrefetched
histogram.summary: Whether a read from stream 1 (conventionally used for payload body) was satisfied from prefetched data. Reported only on the first read operation on the stream (including if there are multiple readers, or even some writers).

histogram.name: SimpleCache.StaleIndexExtraEntryCount
histogram.summary: Count of the number of entries recorded in the index, but not actually present in the cache. Recorded each time a stale index is found and a directory rescan is required.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.StaleIndexMissedEntryCount
histogram.summary: Count of the number of entries present in a cache, but not recorded in the index. Recorded each time a stale index is found and a directory rescan is required.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.StaleIndexQuality
histogram.summary: The quality of a "stale" index compared to a full directory rescan. Recorded immediately after every directory scan for stale indexes.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncCheckEOFHasCrc
histogram.summary: For each EOFRecord found with a valid magic number, indicates if the record also contains a CRC.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncCheckEOFResult
histogram.summary: The result, at the synchronous layer, of checking the EOF record of a cache entry.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncCloseResult
histogram.summary: The result, at the synchronous layer, of closing a cache entry.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncCreatePlatformFileError
histogram.summary: The platform error reported when attempting to create a new cache entry at the synchronous layer.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncCreateResult
histogram.summary: The result, at the synchronous layer, reported when attempting to create a new cache entry.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncKeySHA256Result
histogram.summary: The result of the the key SHA256 check done when opening stream 0 for each entry.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncOpenDidPrefetch
histogram.summary: Whether an attempt was made to prefetch the entire file when executing disk_cache::Backend::OpenEntry.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncOpenEntryAge
histogram.summary: The age of the entry (time since last modified), when opened at the synchronous layer.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncOpenPlatformFileError
histogram.summary: The platform error reported when attempting to create a new cache entry at the synchronous layer.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncOpenResult
histogram.summary: The result, at the synchronous layer, reported when attempting to open a new cache entry.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.SyncWriteResult
histogram.summary: The result, at the synchronous layer, of writing to a cache entry.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.WriteDependencyType
histogram.summary: Shows whether a write operation depends on the previous operation in queue particularly in the aspect of its possibility to run in parallel.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.WriteResult
histogram.obsolete: Replaced 2013/09/03 by WriteResult2, which adds "fast empty return", which previously showed up as "success".
histogram.summary: The outcome of Entry::WriteData in the simple cache.

histogram.name: SimpleCache.WriteResult2
histogram.summary: The outcome of Entry::WriteData in the simple cache.

histogram.name: SimpleGeolocation.Request.Event
histogram.summary: Events in reqests processing of IP-based SimpleGeolocation.

histogram.name: SimpleGeolocation.Request.HasCellTowers
histogram.summary: This boolean histogram counts SimpleGeolocationRequests carrying a nonzero number of cell towers.

histogram.name: SimpleGeolocation.Request.HasWiFiAccessPoints
histogram.summary: This boolean histogram counts SimpleGeolocationRequests carrying nonzero number of WiFi access points.

histogram.name: SimpleGeolocation.Request.ResponseCode
histogram.summary: Http response codes in IP-based SimpleGeolocation.

histogram.name: SimpleGeolocation.Request.ResponseFailureTime
histogram.summary: The time elapsed between the sending of the first API request and the time the final (failed) response was recorded. Includes all retries.

histogram.name: SimpleGeolocation.Request.ResponseSuccessTime
histogram.summary: The time elapsed between the sending of the first API request and the time the final (successfull) response was recorded. Includes all retries.

histogram.name: SimpleGeolocation.Request.Result
histogram.summary: Result of SimpleGeolocationRequest.

histogram.name: SimpleGeolocation.Request.Retries
histogram.summary: Number of retries until the final response was recorded.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.DaysSinceLastShortcutLaunch
histogram.summary: The number of days between launches of an origin saved to homescreen or as a bookmark app shortcut. Recorded at the second and each following launch of a specific origin from a shortcut, independently per origin.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.EngagementPercentageForHTTPS
histogram.summary: The percentage of total site engagement accumulated by this profile for HTTPS URLs as a proportion of all engagement for HTTPS and HTTP URLs. Recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.EngagementScore
histogram.summary: Distribution of the engagement scores accumulated by a user, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.EngagementScore.HTTP
histogram.summary: Distribution of the engagement scores accumulated by a user, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter. Limited specifically to HTTP URLs.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.EngagementScore.HTTPS
histogram.summary: Distribution of the engagement scores accumulated by a user, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter. Limited specifically to HTTPS URLs.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.EngagementScore.IsZero
histogram.summary: The distribution of zero versus non-zero engagement scores accumulated by the user, recorded at the same time as SiteEngagementService.EngagementScore.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.EngagementScoreBucket
histogram.summary: The percentage of sites on a user's profile that have engagement scores that fall in these buckets, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.EngagementType
histogram.summary: The type of engagement (navigation, user input, etc.) which led to an accumulation in site engagement.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.MeanEngagement
histogram.summary: The mean site engagement per origin accumulated by this profile, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.MedianEngagement
histogram.summary: The median site engagement per origin accumulated by this profile, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.OriginsEngaged
histogram.summary: The total number of origins with a non-zero site engagement score for this profile, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.OriginsWithMaxDailyEngagement
histogram.summary: The number of origins which have reached the daily site engagement point cap, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter. Thus, each bin N contains the number of clients where at least N origins have reached the maximum daily site engagement point cap.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.OriginsWithMaxEngagement
histogram.summary: The number of origins which have reached the absolute site engagement point cap, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter. Thus, each bin N contains the number of clients where at least N origins have reached the maximum absolute site engagement point cap.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.PercentOriginsWithMaxEngagement
histogram.summary: The percentage of all origins recorded by the site engagement service which have reached the absolute site engagement point cap, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.ScoreDecayedFrom
histogram.summary: The site engagement score of an origin prior to applying decay. Recorded at the first engagement event after decay, independently per decay event.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.ScoreDecayedTo
histogram.summary: The site engagement score of an origin once decay has occured. Recorded at the first engagement event after decay, independently per decay event.

histogram.name: SiteEngagementService.TotalEngagement
histogram.summary: The total site engagement accumulated by this profile, recorded at startup per non-incognito profile, and then upon the first engagement-increasing event every hour thereafter.

histogram.name: SiteIsolatedCodeCache.Behaviour
histogram.obsolete: Split this histogram into SiteIsolatedCodeCache.JS.Behaviour and SiteIsolatedCodeCache.WASM.Behaviour to collect statistics for JS and WASM code caches in separate histograms.
histogram.summary: The behaviour of site isolated javascript code cache recorded for each cache transaction. It records if the request was serviced and if serviced how it was serviced for ex: hit, miss, update.

histogram.name: SiteIsolatedCodeCache.JS.Behaviour
histogram.summary: The behaviour of site isolated javascript code cache recorded for each cache transaction. It records if the request was serviced and if serviced how it was serviced for ex: hit, miss, update.

histogram.name: SiteIsolatedCodeCache.WASM.Behaviour
histogram.summary: The behaviour of site isolated web assembly code cache recorded for each cache transaction during WASM compilation. It records if the request was serviced and if serviced how it was serviced for ex: hit, miss, update.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.AllResponses
histogram.summary: The count of all network responses received by a renderer. Each response is corresponding to one URL requested by a renderer. Incremented when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.BrowsingInstanceCount
histogram.summary: The count of all current BrowsingInstances. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.CurrentRendererProcessCount
histogram.summary: The count of all renderer processes, including WebUI and extensions. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.Flags.IsolateOrigins
histogram.summary: True if the --isolate-origins mode was enabled from the command line, about:flags, or enterprise policy. Recorded on browser startup and then once every 24 hours.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.Flags.SitePerProcess
histogram.summary: True if the --site-per-process mode was enabled from the command line, about:flags, or enterprise policy. Recorded on browser startup and then once every 24 hours.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateAllSitesProcessCountEstimate
histogram.summary: The upper bound of the predicted renderer process count if we isolated all sites, subject to the process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateAllSitesProcessCountLowerBound
histogram.summary: The lower bound of the predicted renderer process count if we isolated all sites, subject to the process limit. Happens to be the number of unique sites. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateAllSitesProcessCountNoLimit
histogram.summary: The predicted renderer process count if we isolated all sites and if there were no process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateAllSitesTotalProcessCountEstimate
histogram.summary: The predicted total process count if we isolated all sites, subject to the process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateExtensionsProcessCountEstimate
histogram.summary: The upper bound of the predicted renderer process count if we isolated only Chrome extensions, subject to the process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateExtensionsProcessCountLowerBound
histogram.summary: The lower bound of the predicted renderer process count if we isolated only Chrome extensions, subject to the process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateExtensionsProcessCountNoLimit
histogram.summary: The predicted renderer process count if we isolated only Chrome extensions and if there were no process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateExtensionsTotalProcessCountEstimate
histogram.summary: The predicted total process count if we isolated only Chrome extensions, subject to the process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateHttpsSitesProcessCountEstimate
histogram.summary: The upper bound of the predicted renderer process count if we isolated only HTTPS (not HTTP) sites, subject to the process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateHttpsSitesProcessCountLowerBound
histogram.summary: The lower bound of the predicted renderer process count if we isolated only HTTPS (not HTTP) sites, subject to the process limit. Happens to be the number of isolated sites. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateHttpsSitesProcessCountNoLimit
histogram.summary: The predicted renderer process count if we isolated only HTTPS (not HTTP) sites and if there were no process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateHttpsSitesTotalProcessCountEstimate
histogram.summary: The predicted total process count if we isolated only HTTPS (not HTTP) sites, subject to the process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateNothingProcessCountEstimate
histogram.summary: The upper bound of the estimated renderer process count if we isolated no sites, subject to the process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateNothingProcessCountLowerBound
histogram.summary: The lower bound of the predicted renderer process count if we isolated no sites, subject to the process limit. Happens to be the number of isolated sites. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateNothingProcessCountNoLimit
histogram.summary: The predicted renderer process count if we isolated no sites and if there were no process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateNothingTotalProcessCountEstimate
histogram.summary: The predicted total process count if we isolated no sites, subject to the process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.IsolateOrigins.Size
histogram.summary: The number of currently enabled isolated origins. This includes origins specified via the --isolate-origins command-line flag as well as those configured via enterprise policy. Recorded on browser startup.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.LowMemoryMode.Transition
histogram.summary: The number of times a render process transitions its main thread isolate into or out of memory savings mode. This is recorded per-renderer-process upon the creation of the first main frame or the destruction of the last main frame.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.OutOfProcessIframes
histogram.summary: The count of all out-of-process iframes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.ProxyCount
histogram.summary: The count of all RenderFrameProxyHosts. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.ProxyCountPerBrowsingInstance
histogram.summary: The count of RenderFrameProxyHosts in each BrowsingInstance. Recorded each UMA ping, once per BrowsingInstance.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.ReusePendingOrCommittedSite.CouldReuse
histogram.summary: Whether SiteInstances with a ProcessReusePolicy of REUSE_PENDING_OR_COMMITTED_SITE could reuse an existing RenderProcessHost or they had to create a new one. True when they reused an existing process, false when they created a new one.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.SiteInstancesPerBrowsingInstance
histogram.summary: The count of SiteInstances in a single BrowsingInstance. Recorded each UMA ping, once per BrowsingInstance.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.Browser.Action
histogram.summary: Various actions related to cross-site document blocking in the browser process, indicating whether the response was blocked from the renderer. Recorded as the response is processed in the network stack.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.Browser.Allowed.ContentScript
histogram.summary:
The total count of responses that were would be blocked by the cross-site document blocking logic in the browser process, but were only allowed because the request was initiated by a content script of an extension. Sampled with a resource type (0-17) when the response is allowed.
Note that this histogram is not reimplemented in the NetworkService version of Cross-Origin Read Blocking feature. This should be okay since we hope to gather enough data before NetworkService ships.
TODO(lukasza): Around Q4 2018: Remove code after we've gathered enough data.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.Browser.Blocked
histogram.summary: The total count of responses that were blocked by the cross-site document blocking logic in the browser process. Sampled with a resource type (0-17) when the response is blocked; up to 1024 bytes of the response are considered. This is also recorded with a suffix indicating the MIME type category.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.Browser.Blocked.ContentLength.ValueIfAvailable
histogram.summary: For each responses blocked by the cross-site document blocking logic in the browser process, logs network::ResourceResponseInfo::content_length of the response (but only if the content length value was available - if the value was not -1).

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.Browser.Blocked.ContentLength.WasAvailable
histogram.summary: For each responses blocked by the cross-site document blocking logic in the browser process, logs whether network::ResourceResponseInfo::content_length was available (i.e. value was 0 or greated) or not available (i.e. value was equal to -1) if the value was available (i.e. the value was not -1).

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.Browser.BlockedForParserBreaker
histogram.summary: The total count of responses that were blocked by the cross-site document blocking logic in the browser process, due to the presence of a Javascript parser-breaker pattern (like "for(;;);", or ")]}'", or a non-empty JSON dictionary like "{\"a\":". Sampled with a resource type (0-17) when the response is blocked; up to 1024 bytes of the response are considered.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.Browser.BytesReadForSniffing
histogram.summary: The number of bytes of the network response buffered for sniffing purposes, when attempting to sniff the response to determine if it should be blocked as a cross-site document. Recorded after sniffing is attempted.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.DataLength
histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: The number of bytes in the first network packet for a response with headers that imply potential illegal cross-site access. Recorded when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.HTML.Blocked
histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: The count of blocked cross-site document responses due to having HTML content type header and contents sniffed as HTML. Sampled with value of 1 when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.HTML.Blocked.NonRenderableStatusCode
histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: The count of responses with a nonrenderable HTTP status code among blocked cross-site document responses due to their HTML contents. Sampled with value 1 when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.HTML.Blocked.RenderableStatusCode
histogram.summary: The count of responses with a renderable HTTP status code sub-categorized by their requesting context type (e.g., image, script, etc.) among blocked cross-site document responses due to their HTML contents. Sampled with a resource type (0-14) when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.HTML.Blocked.RenderableStatusCode2
histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: The count of responses with a renderable HTTP status code sub-categorized by their requesting context type (e.g., image, script, etc.) among blocked cross-site document responses due to their HTML contents. Sampled with a resource type (0-17) when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.HTML.NoSniffBlocked.NonRenderableStatusCode
histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: The count of responses with a nonrenderable HTTP status code among blocked cross-site document responses due to having HTML content type and nosniff headers. Sampled with value 1 when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.HTML.NoSniffBlocked.RenderableStatusCode
histogram.summary: The count of responses with a renderable HTTP status code sub-categorized by their requesting context type (e.g., image, script, etc.), among blocked cross-site document responses due to having HTML content type and nosniff headers. Sampled with a resource type (0-14) when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.HTML.NoSniffBlocked.RenderableStatusCode2
histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: The count of responses with a renderable HTTP status code sub-categorized by their requesting context type (e.g., image, script, etc.), among blocked cross-site document responses due to having HTML content type and nosniff headers. Sampled with a resource type (0-17) when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.HTML.NotBlocked
histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: The count of not blocked responses despite having an HTML content type header due to the failure of content sniffing. Sampled with value 1 when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.HTML.NotBlocked.MaybeJS
histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: The count of responses that may be parsed as JavaScript among not blocked responses. Sampled with value 1 when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.JSON.Blocked
histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: The count of blocked cross-site document responses due to having JSON content type header and contents sniffed as JSON. Sampled with value 1 when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: The count of responses that may be parsed as JavaScript among not blocked responses with a JSON content type header. Sampled with value 1 when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: SiteIsolation.XSD.MimeType
histogram.obsolete: Removed in March 2018; in M67+, browser-process CORB policy is enabled.
histogram.summary: MIME type codes for content type header values of potentially cross-site document responses, excluding same-site or not http(s) urls. Sampled with a MIME type code (0-4) when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.summary: The count of responses that may be parsed as JavaScript among not blocked responses with an XML content type. Sampled with value 1 when the first network packet of a response of this type is received.

histogram.name: Skia.DrawScaleFactor
histogram.summary: The scale factor of any images drawn by Skia.

histogram.name: Skia.DrawTiled
histogram.summary: Whether a call to drawBitmap(Rect) or drawImage(Rect) used the tiled or non-tiled rasterization path.

histogram.name: Skia.FilterQuality
histogram.summary: The count of images drawn by Skia with a given filter quality.

histogram.name: Skia.LockTexturePath
histogram.summary: The path taken by Skia when it attempts to lock a texture for use.

histogram.name: SmartLock.EligibleDevicesCount
histogram.summary: A count of the number of eligible SmartLock devices which can be used to unlock the user's Chromebook. Logged each time the list of eligible devices is updated. TODO(jhawkins): Log this again once we send this data from eligible phones.

histogram.name: SmartLock.EnabledDevicesCount
histogram.summary: A count of the number of SmartLock devices which the user has enabled to unlock the user's Chromebook, i.e., the device connected to in the SmartLock setup flow. Logged each time the list of eligible devices is updated.

histogram.name: SmartLock.EnabledState
histogram.summary: The state of the SmartLock feature for the current user, set at login. The value is 'enabled' if the user has successfully enabled SmartLock via setup, disabled if the user has explicitly disabled the feature after it has been enabled, and unset if the feature is passively disabled, i.e., has never been enabled by the user.

histogram.name: SmartLock.ToggleFeature
histogram.summary: The number of times the user actively enabled or disabled the SmartLock feature via Settings.

histogram.name: SmartLock.ToggleFeature.Disable.Result
histogram.summary: The result of the operation to disable SmartLock.

histogram.name: Snackbar.Shown
histogram.summary: Records how many times a particular snackbar was shown to the user.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.CleaningTime
histogram.summary: The time between sending the user's response to the Chrome Cleaner process and the Cleaner process terminating. This histogram is logged only for successfully completed runs of the cleaner.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.CleanupResult
histogram.summary: The final status of the Chrome Cleanup Tool run (e.g. succeeded, failed, reboot required).

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.DownloadHttpResponseCode
histogram.summary: The HTTP response code for the Chrome Cleanup Tool download request.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.DownloadStatus
histogram.summary: An indication if an attempt to download the Chrome Cleanup Tool succeeded or the reason why it failed.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.DownloadStatusErrorCode
histogram.summary: The HTTP response or net error code when trying to download the Chrome Cleanup Tool.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.HasCompleted
histogram.summary: The state of software reporter cleaner tool runs. A value of "Not Completed" can mean either the tool crashed, or the tool was still running when Chrome checked.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.HasRebooted
histogram.summary: Whether a reboot happened since the software reporter cleaner tool has finished execution with a pre-reboot exit code.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.NumberOfDownloadAttempts
histogram.summary: The number of attempts to download the Chrome Cleanup tool until it either succeeds or fails.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.RebootPromptShown
histogram.summary: Indicates how the user was prompted to reboot the machine to complete a run of the Chrome Cleanup Tool.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.RebootResponse
histogram.summary: Indicates that the user accepted to initiate a reboot to complete a run of the Chrome Cleanup Tool. This is logged the user starts a reboot from the cleanup card in the Settings page. We can't track when the reboot is not initiated, because it can happen at any moment in the future (there is no prompt blocking the user).

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.RunningTime
histogram.summary: How long it took to run the software reporter cleaner tool.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.ScanningTime
histogram.summary: The time between launching the Chrome Cleaner process and the cleaner having scanned the user's machine and Chrome receiving an IPC call with the results.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.SettingsPageActiveOnRebootRequired
histogram.summary: Indicates if the Settings page was the active tab once a cleanup finished with reboot required.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.TimeToCompleteDownload
histogram.summary: The time between the first attempt to download the Chrome Cleanup tool and a successful download or the last unsuccessful attempt to download without retrying.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Cleaner.Version
histogram.summary: The build version of the software reporter cleaner tool.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.CleanerLogsAcceptance
histogram.summary: Whether the user accepted to upload logs from Chrome Cleanup Tool. This is logged when the user accepts the Chrome prompt to start a cleanup either from the prompt dialog or from the cleanup card in the Settings page.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.CleanupCard
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome Cleanup Tool card in the Settings page has been shown.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.CleanupStarted
histogram.summary: Indicates where the user started a cleanup from (e.g. from the prompt dialog or the settings page).

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.ExperimentErrors
histogram.summary: Whether a configuration error prevented the experimental Software Reporter from running.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.FoundUwSReadError
histogram.summary: Whether there was an error reading the registry key containing UwS found by the Software Removal Tool.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.IPCDisconnected
histogram.summary: Indicates when the IPC with the Chrome Cleanup Tool process is disconnected from Chrome. Success is logged when it's disconnected and no longer needed (the user already responded to the prompt in Chrome or the cleaner process terminated normally); failures are logged when the IPC is disconnected, but communication between Chrome and the cleaner process is still required (e.g. while Chrome is waiting for scanning results or when the cleaner is waiting for the user's response from Chrome).

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.LastUploadResult
histogram.summary: The result of the most recent SRT log upload.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.LogsUploadEnabled
histogram.summary: If logs uploads are enabled in the Software Reporter or the reason why it is disabled. Recorded before each run of the Software Reporter.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.LogsUploadResult
histogram.summary: The result of the most recent Software Reporter logs upload. Written by the Software Reporter in the registry and sent by Chrome after the reporter finishes.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.LogsUploadResultRegistryError
histogram.summary: Error encountered when reading the software reporter logs upload result from the registry.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.MajorVersion
histogram.summary: As SoftwareReporter.MinorVersion, but a double word combination of the other components of the version of the software reporter. The high word is the first component when there are more than one, and the low word is either the second or third one, depending if there are at least 3, or 4 components.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.MemoryUsed
histogram.summary: The memory used by the software reporter tool as reported by the tool itself via the registry. Logged just after the software reporter tool has finished.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.MinorVersion
histogram.summary: The last component of the version of the software reporter that was executed so we can identify when users are not getting their component updated as quickly as we expect it.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.NoPromptReason
histogram.summary: The reason why the user has not been prompted to run the Chrome Cleanup Tool (e.g. no unwanted software found, user recently prompted).

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.NumberOfFilesToDelete
histogram.summary: The number of files to be deleted by the Chrome Cleanup Tool.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.OnDemandUpdateRequired
histogram.summary: Whether an on-demand update of the Software Reporter component was needed for a user-initiated cleanup.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.OnDemandUpdateSucceeded
histogram.summary: Whether an on-demand update of the Software Reporter component succeeded, when needed for a user-initiated cleanup.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.PostCleanupSettingsReset
histogram.summary: The number of profiles whose settings will be reset once a successful cleanup completes.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.PromptDialog.TimeUntilDone
histogram.summary: The time between the Chrome Cleaner dialog being shown and the dialog being closed.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.PromptDialogResponse
histogram.summary: The user response on the modal Chrome Cleanup Tool prompt dialog (e.g. accepted, cancelled).

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.PromptShown
histogram.summary: Whether the user has been prompted to run the Chrome Cleanup Tool.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.PromptShownWithType
histogram.summary:
Whether the user has been prompted to run the Chrome Cleanup Tool and which type of prompt has been shown.
This is logged once a prompt is presented to the user.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.PromptUsage
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 2017-11-01. Replaced with SoftwareReporter.PromptDialogResponse.
histogram.summary: Usage of the Software Removal Tool (SRT) Prompt.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.ReporterSequenceResult
histogram.summary: Indicates the result of a reporter sequence once it ends.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.ReporterSequenceType
histogram.summary: Indicates the type of a reporter sequence run on the user system. Logged when the sequence is scheduled to start.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.RunningTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took to run the software reporter tool as reported by the tool itself via the registry. Logged just after the software reporter tool has finished.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.RunningTimeAccordingToChrome
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took for the software reporter to run as measured by chrome. Logged just after the software reporter tool has finished.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.RunningTimeRegistryError
histogram.summary: Error encountered when reading the software reporter tool's start and end times from the registry.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.ScannerLogsAcceptance
histogram.summary: Whether the user accepted to upload scanner logs from Chrome Cleanup Tool. This is logged when the user starts a scan for the Settings page and is only logged for user-initiated cleanups.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.Step
histogram.summary: The registration and execution steps for the software reporter.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.TaggedProfileForResetting
histogram.summary: Registers when a profile is tagged for settings reset before a run of the Chrome Cleanup Tool.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.UploadFailureCount
histogram.summary: The total count of SRT log upload failures experienced by this machine for all time. This value is reported at startup by Chrome and is capped at 64.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.UploadLongestFailureRun
histogram.summary: The longest run of upload failures logged by SRT runs. This value is reported at startup by Chrome and is capped at 64.

histogram.name: SoftwareReporter.UploadSuccessCount
histogram.summary: The total count of successful SRT log uploads experienced by this machine for all time. This value is reported at startup by Chrome and is capped at 64.

histogram.name: SpecialLocale.PromotionDialog
histogram.summary: Records how users interact with the special locale promotion dialog.

histogram.name: Spellcheck.Android.Available
histogram.summary: Whether the Android spellchecking service was available at the start of a new spellchecking session. Note that the user can disable the Android spellchecking service in settings, in which case it will show up as not available in the histogram.

histogram.name: SpellCheck.Android.Latency
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it takes to receive results from the spellchecking service on Android. More precisely this is the SystemClock.elapsedRealtime() between the SpellCheckerSession.getSentenceSuggestions() and the invocation of the onGetSentenceSuggestions() callback.

histogram.name: SpellCheck.SpellingService.Enabled
histogram.summary: Whether the user has opted in to asking Google for spelling suggestions. Recorded both when spelling is initialized and when the preference is changed.

histogram.name: Sqlite.AppCache.Error
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Error codes returned by sqlite for the appcache db.

histogram.name: Sqlite.AutoCommitTime
histogram.summary: Record times for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE statements run outside of an explicit transaction.

histogram.name: Sqlite.CloseFailure
histogram.summary: Error which prevented database close.

histogram.name: Sqlite.CommitTime
histogram.summary: Record time spent in explicit COMMIT statements.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Cookie.Error
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Error codes returned by sqlite the cookie db.

histogram.name: Sqlite.DatabaseTracker.Error
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Error codes returned by sqlite the websqldb tracker db.

histogram.name: Sqlite.DeprecationVersionResult
histogram.summary: Annotations for which bits of sql::MetaTable::CheckDeprecated() fire.

histogram.name: Sqlite.DomainBoundCerts.Error
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Error codes returned by sqlite for the domain-bound certs db.

histogram.name: Sqlite.DomStorageDatabase.Error
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Error codes returned by sqlite for the domstorage db.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Error
histogram.summary: SQLite extended error codes.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Error.IOERR
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: SQLite extended SQLITE_IOERR codes for all databases.

histogram.name: Sqlite.History.Error
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Error codes returned by sqlite for the history db.

histogram.name: Sqlite.MemoryKB.OneDay
histogram.summary: SQLite memory footprint from sqlite3_memory_used() recorded 1 day after first database is opened by sql::Database.

histogram.name: Sqlite.MemoryKB.OneHour
histogram.summary: SQLite memory footprint from sqlite3_memory_used() recorded 1 hour after first database is opened by sql::Database.

histogram.name: Sqlite.MemoryKB.OneWeek
histogram.summary: SQLite memory footprint from sqlite3_memory_used() recorded 1 week after first database is opened by sql::Database.

histogram.name: Sqlite.MemoryKB.TenMinutes
histogram.summary: SQLite memory footprint from sqlite3_memory_used() recorded 10 minutes after first database is opened by sql::Database.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Migration.History
histogram.summary: Time used to migrate History database schema to a new version.

histogram.name: Sqlite.OpenFailure
histogram.summary: Error which prevented database open.

histogram.name: Sqlite.OpenProbeFailure
histogram.summary: Error from first read of the database.

histogram.name: Sqlite.QueryTime
histogram.summary: Record times for all statements run against the database.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Quota.Error
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Error codes returned by sqlite for the quota db.

histogram.name: Sqlite.RazeDatabase
histogram.summary: Errors attempting to Raze() database.

histogram.name: Sqlite.RazeDatabase2
histogram.summary: Errors on second attempt to Raze() database.

histogram.name: Sqlite.RazeDatabaseTruncate
histogram.summary: Errors truncating database for Raze().

histogram.name: Sqlite.RecoveryAttachError
histogram.summary: SQLite error code from failed ATTACH in sql::Recovery::Init().

histogram.name: Sqlite.RecoveryEvents
histogram.summary: Records specific failure and success cases in sql::Recovery implementation, to determine which cases warrant further development. This histogram tracks detected errors, some of which may cause multiple results. See recovery.cc for details.

histogram.name: Sqlite.RecoveryHandle
histogram.summary: Error from sqlite3_backup_init() in sql::Recovery.

histogram.name: Sqlite.RecoveryStep
histogram.summary: Error from sqlite3_backup_step() in sql::Recovery.

histogram.name: Sqlite.SizeKB
histogram.summary: Size in kilobytes of pre-existing database at startup.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Stats
histogram.summary: Stats for different API calls in sql/.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Text.Error
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Error codes returned by sqlite the full text db.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Thumbnail.Error
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Error codes returned by sqlite for the thumbnail db.

histogram.name: Sqlite.UpdateTime
histogram.summary: Record times for statements which could update the database file. Includes commit and autocommit time.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Version
histogram.summary: Version of pre-existing database at startup.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Vfs
histogram.summary: Buffer sizes passed to browser-process SQLite VFS functions.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Vfs_Events
histogram.summary: I/O operations measured by browser-process SQLite VFS wrapper.

histogram.name: Sqlite.Web.Error
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Error codes returned by sqlite the web db.

histogram.name: sri.resource_integrity_mismatch_event
histogram.summary: When resources are checked for mismatching integrity and whether the mismatch forces a refetch.

histogram.name: SSL.CertificateErrorHelpCenterVisited
histogram.summary: When the "Learn more" link on an SSL interstitial is clicked, this histogram records if the navigation succeded, if it failed with an interstitial, or if it failed with a different error.

histogram.name: SSL.CertificateErrorReportEvent
histogram.summary:
Users can opt in to send reports of certificate validation errors to Google. This records the outcome whenever Chrome sends such a report.
Some certificate validation reports might not be received because proxies, firewalls or other content filters might filter report uploads.

histogram.name: SSL.CertificateErrorReportFailure
histogram.summary: Users can opt in to send reports of certificate validation errors to Google. This records the error code whenever Chrome fails to send such a report.

histogram.name: SSL.ExpectCTReportFailure
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated as of 07/2016. Replaced with SSL.ExpectCTReportFailure2.
histogram.summary: Sites can opt in to Expect CT, a reporting feature that sends a report whenever a TLS connection does not have valid Certificate Transparency information associated with it. This records the error code when Chrome fails to send an Expect CT report.

histogram.name: SSL.ExpectCTReportFailure2
histogram.summary: Sites can opt in to Expect CT, a reporting feature that sends a report whenever a TLS connection does not have valid Certificate Transparency information associated with it. This records the error code when Chrome fails to send an Expect CT report.

histogram.name: SSL.ExpectCTReportSendingAttempt
histogram.summary: Sites can opt in to Expect CT, a reporting feature that sends a report whenever a TLS connection does not have valid Certificate Transparency information associated with it. This metric fires whenever Chrome attempts to send an Expect CT report.

histogram.name: SSL.InsecureContent
histogram.summary: Keeps track of various insecure content events, such as loading an HTTP script from an HTTPS page.

histogram.name: SSL.MarkHttpAsStatus
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated December 2017 (M65). This information in this histogram can be found in LoginCustomFlags (for users who have set a flag) or by filtering to users with the MarkHttpAs feature enabled (for seeing the field trial breakdown).
histogram.summary: Reports whether the user has selected to mark HTTP origins with an experimental UI treatment (such as showing a warning on HTTP pages that collect sensitive information). This histogram is recorded whenever the security level (the overall security state of a page, roughly corresponding to the lock icon) is computed for an HTTP page.

histogram.name: SSL.MarkNonSecureAsStatus
histogram.summary: Reports whether the user has selected to mark non-secure origins as Neutral (the status quo), Dubious, or Non-Secure.

histogram.name: SSORecallPromo.AccountsAvailable
histogram.summary: Number of accounts available for Single Sign On with the current device, recorded at the time the SSO Promo is shown.

histogram.name: SSORecallPromo.PromoAction
histogram.summary: Action the user takes when the Single Sign On promotion is shown.

histogram.name: SSORecallPromo.PromoSeenCount
histogram.summary: Number of times the promotion has been seen on the current device.

histogram.name: Stability.Android.PendingMinidumpsOnStartup
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated in M60. Roughly 50% of Chrome startups that had *any* pending minidumps had at least one pending minidump without any logcat output. About 5% had multiple minidumps without any logcat output.
histogram.summary: The number of un-uploaded minidumps present in the Android Crash Reports directory. Each minidump file (should) correspond to a crash. This is recorded on startup, just prior to initiating uploading for these minidumps. This is intended as a temporary metric, to be removed around M60: http://crbug.com/699785

histogram.name: Stability.Android.PendingMinidumpsOnStartup.SansLogcat
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated in M60. Roughly 50% of Chrome startups that had *any* pending minidumps had at least one pending minidump without any logcat output. About 5% had multiple minidumps without any logcat output.
histogram.summary: The number of un-uploaded minidumps present in the Android Crash Reports directory that do not yet have logcat output attached. Each minidump file (should) correspond to a crash. This is recorded on startup, just prior to initiating uploading for all pending minidumps. This is intended as a temporary metric, to be removed around M60: http://crbug.com/699785

histogram.name: Stability.Android.ProcessedCrashCounts
histogram.summary: Individual enum counts specific conditions of child process terminations. There are too many individual states to enumerate into a single histogram, so this effectively does the aggregration that normally happens on the server side in the client instead. The benefit is this is easily extensible in the future, and downside is that it does not allow additional server side analysis. Note a single child process termination may fall into multiple enums.

histogram.name: Stability.Android.RendererCrash
histogram.summary: Counts renderer crashes including OOMs. Android only. Mirrors old stability proto renderer crashes.

histogram.name: Stability.Android.StrongBindingOomRemainingBindingState
histogram.summary: Record the state of child service bindings left when a child service with strong binding is OOM killed by Android. Recorded when such a OOM kill is detected by browser. Note this does not distinguish process type, and is for example recorded for both GPU and renderer process.

histogram.name: Stability.Android.StrongBindingOomRemainingStrongBindingCount
histogram.summary: Record the count of strongly bound child services left when a child service with strong binding is OOM killed by Android. Recorded when such a OOM kill is detected by browser. Note this does not distinguish process type, and is for example count both GPU and renderer process.

histogram.name: Stability.BadMessageTerminated.Chrome
histogram.summary: Count of processes killed by chrome/browser because they sent an IPC that couldn't be properly handled. Categories are the reasons (code locations) for the kills.

histogram.name: Stability.BadMessageTerminated.Content
histogram.summary: Count of child processes killed because they sent an IPC that couldn't be properly handled. Categories are the reasons (code locations) for the kills.

histogram.name: Stability.BadMessageTerminated.Extensions
histogram.summary: Count of extension processes killed because they sent an IPC that couldn't be properly handled. Categories are the reasons (code locations) for the kills.

histogram.name: Stability.BadMessageTerminated.GuestView
histogram.summary: Count of processes killed because they sent a bad IPC that couldn't be properly handled. Categories are the reasons (code locations) for the kills.

histogram.name: Stability.BadMessageTerminated.NaCl
histogram.summary: Count of processes killed because they sent a NaCl IPC that couldn't be properly handled. Categories are the reasons (code locations) for the kills.

histogram.name: Stability.BadMessageTerminated.PasswordManager
histogram.summary: Count of processes killed because they sent a bad IPC that couldn't be properly handled. Categories are the reasons (code locations) for the kills.

histogram.name: Stability.BrowserExitCodes
histogram.summary: Records the exit code of the browser process (on Windows) from the previous launch. On crash, the exit code will indicate the exception code of the crash. This is emitted in stability data with the first report on a subsequent launch. Note: Due to https://crbug.com/805754, the enum labels for RESULT_CODE_* are off by 1 for M56 and earlier.

histogram.name: Stability.ChildFrameCrash.ShownAfterCrashingReason
histogram.summary: Logs the reason that a crashed subframe was shown. Logged at most once per crash, and only if the subframe was hidden when it crashed and became visible later.

histogram.name: Stability.ChildFrameCrash.Visibility
histogram.summary: Logged after detecting that a sad subframe is shown (logged at most once per crash).

histogram.name: Stability.CrashedProcessAge.Extension
histogram.summary: The age of a crashed extension process. Not logged on iOS. Logged together with CrashExitCodes.Extension.

histogram.name: Stability.CrashedProcessAge.Renderer
histogram.summary: The age of a crashed extension process. Not logged on iOS. Logged together with CrashExitCodes.Renderer.

histogram.name: Stability.ExitFunnel
histogram.summary: Temporary instrumentation to record the Windows browser's exit path. See http://crbug.com/412384.

histogram.name: Stability.Internals.DataDiscardCount
histogram.summary: Number of times stability data was discarded. This is accumulated since the last report, even across versions. This is logged during stability metric recording for the following log sent.

histogram.name: Stability.Internals.InitialStabilityLogDeferredCount
histogram.summary: Number of times the initial stability log upload was deferred to the next startup. This is logged during stability metric recording for the following log sent.

histogram.name: Stability.Internals.SystemCrashCount
histogram.summary: Number of times a browser crash was detected, where the browser was last known to be alive in a Windows system session that terminated abnormally.

histogram.name: Stability.Internals.VersionMismatchCount
histogram.summary: Number of times the version number stored in prefs did not match the serialized system profile version number. This is logged during stability metric recording.

histogram.name: Stability.MobileSessionShutdownType
histogram.summary: Type of the shutdown. This histogram is recorded at startup and logs a sample for the previous session. A clean shutdown is one where the app enters the background before being killed. Other shutdowns are all some sort of crash. The absence of a crash log indicates that the app was killed by the OS, normally due to memory pressure. If memory pressure is extremely high, the app may not have had a chance to response to a memory warning before being killed. For first launch after upgrade, the crash reports may be discarded before this histogram is written to so it's not possible to determine if there's a valid crash log present or not.

histogram.name: Stability.RendererUnresponsiveBeforeTermination
histogram.summary: If the renderer is considered unresponsive from the browser's perspective before the process is terminated. This metric is being used to figure out crash reports that have all idle threads and perhaps the process is being terminated when it shouldn't be. crbug.com/615090.

histogram.name: StackSamplingProfiler.ProfileDeserializationTime
histogram.summary: Amount of time taken to deserialize a call stack profile string.

histogram.name: StackSamplingProfiler.ProfileSerializationTime
histogram.summary: Amount of time taken to serialize a call stack profile.

histogram.name: Stars.Goog_Related
histogram.summary: Percentage of clips with Google related urls (points to internal Google resources). Logs every time user goes to chrome://bookmarks.

histogram.name: Stars.Goog_Related_20_Percent
histogram.summary: Percentage of clips with Google related urls within first 20 (points to internal Google resources). Logs every time user goes to chrome://bookmarks.

histogram.name: Stars.Images_Percent
histogram.summary: Percentage of clips with images. Logs every time user goes to chrome://bookmarks.

histogram.name: Stars.Images_Percent_First20
histogram.summary: Percentage of clips with images within first 20. Logs every time user goes to chrome://bookmarks.

histogram.name: Stars.LaunchLocation
histogram.summary: Logs a UI location from which a bookmark is launched.

histogram.name: Stars.No_Images_Snippets
histogram.summary: Percentage of clips without images or snippets. Logs every time user goes to chrome://bookmarks.

histogram.name: Stars.No_Img_No_Snippet_20_Percent
histogram.summary: Percentage of clips without images or snippets within first 20. Logs every time user goes to chrome://bookmarks.

histogram.name: Startup.AfterStartupTaskCount
histogram.summary: The number of after-startup tasks that were queued prior to startup completion and deferred until that time.

histogram.name: Startup.AfterStartupTaskDelayedUntilTime
histogram.summary: Time from the process creation until deferred after-startup tasks began running.

histogram.name: Startup.Android.Cold.TimeToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.summary: Android: The time from the activity creation point to the first contentful paint of the first loaded page. It's not recorded when the first loaded page is non http(s) page like a chrome error page, a new tab page, a blank page. It's also not recorded if the application wasn't in the foreground since the start till the end of event.

histogram.name: Startup.Android.Cold.TimeToFirstNavigationCommit
histogram.summary: Android: The time from the activity creation point to the moment the first navigation is committed, i.e. when renderer gets the first byte of the document. It's not recorded when the first loaded page is non http(s) page like a chrome error page, a new tab page, a blank page. It's also not recorded if the application wasn't in the foreground since the start till the end of event.

histogram.name: Startup.Android.Experimental.Cold.TimeToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.obsolete: This metric was deprecated in M68 in favour of "Startup.Android.Cold.TimeToFirstContentfulPaint.Tabbed". It has moved out of experimental and records a smaller range of numbers (medium times instead of long times). The ".Tabbed" suffix is added since the histogram is being tracked for WebApkActivity using the ".WebApk" suffix.
histogram.summary: Android: The time from the activity creation point to the first contentful paint of the first loaded page. It's not recorded when the first loaded page is non http(s) page like a chrome error page, a new tab page, a blank page. It's also not recorded if the application wasn't in the foreground since the start till the end of event.

histogram.name: Startup.Android.Experimental.Cold.TimeToFirstNavigationCommit
histogram.obsolete: This metric was deprecated in M68 in favour of "Startup.Android.Cold.TimeToFirstNavigationCommit.Tabbed". It has moved out of experimental and records a smaller range of numbers (medium times instead of long times). The ".Tabbed" suffix is added since the histogram is being tracked for WebApkActivity using the ".WebApk" suffix.
histogram.summary: Android: The time from the activity creation point to the moment the first navigation is committed, i.e. when renderer gets the first byte of the document. It's not recorded when the first loaded page is non http(s) page like a chrome error page, a new tab page, a blank page. It's also not recorded if the application wasn't in the foreground since the start till the end of event.

histogram.name: Startup.AppListFirstPaintColdStart
histogram.summary: Time for a newly created browser process to perform the first paint of the app launcher, when started with the --show-app-list flag and with no currently running Chrome processes.

histogram.name: Startup.AppListFirstPaintWarmStart
histogram.summary: Time for a running browser process to perform the first paint of the app launcher. Measured from the time a second Chrome process started, which sent its --show-app-list command line argument to the already-running process and will soon exit.

histogram.name: Startup.BlockForCrashpadHandlerStartupTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during in crash_report::BlockUntilHandlerStarted().

histogram.name: Startup.BringToForegroundReason
histogram.summary: Records the cause, each time Chrome is brought to the foreground. Currently only checks if a NotificationUIManager notification was shown in the last 5 seconds (includes Web Notifications, but not media or Cast).

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserLaunchURLCount
histogram.summary: Measured when Chrome is invoked, this counts the number of URLs passed via command line. Note that this handles the case of a URL passed to Chrome starting up, or when a running Chrome is given the message to open a new URL.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMainRunnerImplInitializeLongTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during BrowserMainRunnerImpl::Initialize.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMainRunnerImplInitializeStep1Time
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during the first section of BrowserMainRunnerImpl::Initialize.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMainRunnerImplInitializeStep2Time
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during the second section of BrowserMainRunnerImpl::Initialize.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMainToRendererMain
histogram.summary: Time from the ChromeMain() entry in the browser process to the first RendererMain() entry.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStart.To.MainNavigationStart
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2/2018, as MessageLoopStart is after MainNavigationStart with browser-side navigation.
histogram.summary: Time between Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTime and Startup.FirstWebContents.MainNavigationStart. Recorded explicitly to allow easy breakdown of Startup.FirstWebContents.MainNavigationStart when diagnosing issues.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStart.To.NonEmptyPaint2
histogram.summary: Time between Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTime and Startup.FirstWebContents.NonEmptyPaint2. Recorded explicitly to allow easy breakdown of Startup.FirstWebContents.NonEmptyPaint2 when diagnosing issues.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartHardFaultCount
histogram.summary: The number of hard faults incurred in the browser process from startup to start of the main thread's message loop, not including first runs of the browser. This is only reported on Windows 7 and greater.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartHardFaultCount.FirstRun
histogram.summary: The number of hard faults incurred in the browser process from startup to start of the main thread's message loop on first run. This is only reported on Windows 7 and greater.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartHardFaultCount.Success
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 12/2015. No longer tracked because values collected on stable show that the function pretty much never fails (succeeds 99.9996% of the time).
histogram.summary: If OS support was detected (Windows 7 and greater) indicates whether it was possible to determine the number of hard faults that have occurred in the process from startup to start of the main thread's message loop. This can fail because the underlying call is inherently racy.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTime
histogram.summary: Time from browser startup to the start of the main thread's message loop.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTimeFromMainEntry
histogram.summary: Time from main entry to the start of the main thread's message loop. This stat is only recorded after 7 minutes of OS uptime to try to mitigate the variance resulting from Chrome being autostarted. Replaced with Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTimeFromMainEntry2 which is recorded all the time.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTimeFromMainEntry.FirstRun
histogram.summary: Time from main entry to the start of the main thread's message loop on first run. This stat is only recorded after 7 minutes of OS uptime to try to mitigate the variance resulting from Chrome being autostarted. Replaced with Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTimeFromMainEntry.FirstRun2 which is recorded all the time.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTimeFromMainEntry.FirstRun2
histogram.summary: Time from main entry to the start of the main thread's message loop on first run.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTimeFromMainEntry2
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2018-01 in favor of Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTimeFromMainEntry3 which does not involve a conversion from Time to TimeTicks.
histogram.summary: Time from main entry to the start of the main thread's message loop.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTimeFromMainEntry3
histogram.summary: Time from main entry to the start of the main thread's message loop.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserOpenTabs
histogram.summary: Time taken to open the initial tab or to restore tabs from previous session.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserProcessImpl_PreMainMessageLoopRunTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during BrowserProcessImpl::PreMainMessageLoopRun.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserWindow.FirstPaint
histogram.summary: Time from browser startup to the time the first Browser window has finished painting its children.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserWindow.FirstPaint.CompositingEnded
histogram.summary: Time from browser startup to the time the GPU has finished compositing after first Browser window has finished painting its children. At this point the Browser interface is visible on screen. Measures how much time does it take for GPU to actually paint the first time.

histogram.name: Startup.BrowserWindowDisplay
histogram.summary: Time from browser startup to the time the browser window initially becomes visible.

histogram.name: Startup.ChromeCast.TimeToDisplayVideo
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 7/2015. To be replaced with Cast events.
histogram.summary: The elapsed time from the ChromeCast application launch to the first video frame displayed.

histogram.name: Startup.ColdStartWithExternalURLTime
histogram.summary: The duration of all initializations in a cold start triggered by an external URL.

histogram.name: Startup.ColdStartWithoutExternalURLTime
histogram.summary: The duration of all initializations in a cold start not triggered by an external URL.

histogram.name: Startup.CreateFirstProfile
histogram.summary: How long it takes to load the original profile synchronously on the UI thread.

histogram.name: Startup.Experimental.FirstWebContents.MainFrameLoad.ManyBuckets
histogram.summary: An experimental metric only collected on the dev and canary channels. Measure the elapsed time from process launch to the first main frame load of the first web contents. Uses significantly more buckets, with reduced ranges.

histogram.name: Startup.Experimental.FirstWebContents.MainFrameLoad.StandardBuckets
